subject: The MTC is... 😬😳
body:
...veeeery busy! ⏰️ It's really tough to make time for companion study, personal study and language study. 📖 This isn't to say that I'm not having a great time (I am) but it's GO GO GO! 🏃‍♂️ All of your waking hours are taken up by something, but I can see the value 🤑 in pretty much anything we are doing, and I know that when we are out in the field I will be happy that I paid attention to stuff.

I am hoping to spend more time ⏳️ during the following weeks working on drafts of emails, but because of how little time I have to work on stuff today I will keep things fairly brief. 🩳 I do want to share just how nice it is to have time for personal gospel study. It is definitely something I haven't normally prioritized over my art or even Duck Game 🦆, but I hope that when I get home I will be able to better implement it into my daily schedule, its so nice to be able to focus in on one thing. 😤

I think that I have really underestimated the importance of the scriptures in my life, reading through various parts of the Book of Mormon have really awakened my spritual senses; I feel like I am much more confident in teaching with it as a resource.😈

A scripture/s that stood out to me this week was Moroni 10:3-5 📘

"3 Behold, I would exhort you that when ye shall read these things, if it be wisdom in God that ye should read them, that ye would remember how merciful the Lord hath been unto the children of men, from the creation of Adam even down until the time that ye shall receive these things, and ponder it in your hearts. 4 And when ye shall receive these things, I would exhort you that ye would ask God, the Eternal Father, in the name of Christ, if these things are not true; and if ye shall ask with a sincere heart, with real intent, having faith in Christ, he will manifest the truth of it unto you, by the power of the Holy Ghost. 5 And by the power of the Holy Ghost ye may know the truth of all things."

It's a pretty simple few scriptures but it's been very helpful in teaching practice. 🤩

Anyways, it's overall been a great week and welcoming to the MTC, and I know that this is exactly where I need to be. 😎

がんばって！！

-きりあんちょろう -(Elder Killian) kaden.killian@missionary.org
summary: Busy! Meeting trainer, investigators; sprained foot; enjoying scripture study.

subject: FW: 👔 Weekly 🧾 email #2-3 sort of
body: -------- Original message --------From: killianfamily Date: 8/3/24 11:03 PM (GMT-07:00) To: killianfamily@gmail.com Subject: 👔 Weekly 🧾 email #2-3 sort of Alright, I had a lovely email all typed out but somehow it got deleted so this will have to span both weeks unfortunately 😅- Brief Summary of Week 2 -This week was a very good time, especially since we started Japanese Immersion, which basically means that in our 6 hours of class each day, our teachers (and ourselves) are only allowed to speak in Japanese. I understand most of the stuff being said, but I mostly enjoy hearing my district trying to speak only in Japanese. I know an OK amount of Japanese vocabulary, but the grammar is nuts. There are 7 (technically 8) ways to conjugate verbs, and the sentence structure is also entirely different from English, which takes some getting used to. Food was mid as usual, but I did figure out the hardest part of my mission so far: Definitely my companion 👱 While I dont have anything against him, almost everything he does drives me insane 😂 I would rather have a conversation with literally anyone else in our district 😂 I have made it my objective (mostly over week 3) to do nice things for him wherever I can, and encourage him even if it takes me a full minute of gritting my teeth to spit out the words (it did). After prayer and much thinking on my end I came to the conclusion that I will be less frustrated and irritated if I think about the things that he does well at and if I just try to serve him. I find it hard to feel bad when I am doing nice things for others so that's probably a big part of it.That's about it for week 2, now onto 🥁 ...Week 3 🤑Time flies when you're sick and miserable ❤️‍🔥❤️‍🔥 Half joking, I think it's a near-impossible task to make me miserable (*cough cough* Klondike). Jokes aside, the start of Week 3 I had a miserable cold. I was super lightheaded and stuffy for a day or two before I remembered my parents sent me an electric thermometer 🌡(I swear I have more equipment than a military base). I scanned myself and had a fever of 102 degrees, so I went to bed early and slept the next morning where we would have had our tutoring session. I was mostly better a couple days after, likely due to a daily emergen-c vitamin C packet paired with some flu and allergy medicine. 💊💊 Before I knew it it was Thursday, and I couldn't really remember anything specific that happened throughout the week. Probably the most interesting thing that happened was my parents sent me some Nutella packets and I was able to upgrade my peanut butter banana sandwiches 🥪. Japanese classes have been super fun, I really just love the language and getting to hear it for 6 hours a day is great. I was able to do a teensy bit of painting with my fancy watercolors, but it's usually pretty busy with personal study and language study. On that note, not gonna lie I've been loving my personal scripture study. I've made it my personal goal to read through all of Alma before I leave the MTC which may or may not be feasible with an hour of scripture reading a day. We'll have to see 🫠Japanese classes this week were great, especially after I recovered from my sickness. On P-day I went to the temple and did sealings for the first time which was really neat 😁It's pretty late as of writing this so I gotta wrap this up, but I just wanna say I appreciate all of yall, I debate as to how many people read through my ramblings, but I definitely wish I could tell yall these things in person. Hopefully I'll get better at writing my emails as time goes on 🙏Seriously love yall, see ya next week ❤️Weekly Scripture 📙"Then Jesus said unto them, My time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready."Not super sure why this one stuck out to me, but what I get from it is that Christ has his parts (numerous and extremely important parts) to play, but we always have stuff that we can or should be doing as well for both our own salvation and just to live a good life.John 7:6https://www.churchofjesuschrist.org/study/scriptures/nt/john/7?id=p6&lang=eng#p6Elder Killian Kaden.killian@missionary.org
summary: Sent email from T-Mobile device, discussed Japanese immersion, companion issues, planned improvements.

subject: ✨️I built a cardboard AC 🌬✨️
body: Weekly email number 4 👾 (Half way through the MTC)Highlight of the week was either getting to drink like 6 glasses of milk a day 🐄 or getting to build a cardboard air redirection system ☢️ (both of which I have pictures of (although one is a picture of me after having drank all of the cafeteria milk))This week has been great all-round. Language immersion and classes have been really exhausting but I'm mostly just happy to have the practice for when i'm out in the field. It's questionable whether everyone else agrees with 7 hours of classes a day, but they will definitely not complain when they're teaching in Japan 😂Last week I mentioned how the hardest part about my mission was having my companion, and while it still probably is most of the time, things have gotten better as I seek to serve and share valuable time with him. Definitely going better on that front.This week I've been loving what I've read from Alma, I never knew just how neat of a story it was reading about Alma and Amulek, they went through a lot together. One of our instructors also shared about it during our companion interview thing and said that things would go well if we strengthen each other. I think that as I am more open and friendly with my companion we'll be better able to achieve that. :^)Our district has also grown a lot together over the past little while, probably because we are all working hard together with such a difficult language. Speaking of which, my Japanese is slowly but surely coming along. It's definitely not easy, but I'm finding I am more able to get across my general intentions which is a big win. ✌️Food this week was probably just average (maybe made worse by the fact that the lines take upwards of 40 minutes for a single plate) but I have genuinely been drinking 4-6 glasses of milk a day, and that makes me very happy 😁I am also proud to report that I have made no shortage of jokes throughout each day, and I sincerely hope that it makes things a little more 🧸able for those around me. (Not sorry)I think I'd probably explode if I didn't share my energy with those around me. I'm physically tired by the end of each day but I haven't yet felt like it has been a drag, which I'm a bit surprised about to be honest.Well, that's most of my allotted time for now, but I love yall, and I am thinking about yall :)じゃあね👋Scripture of the week(From Alma 17)11 And the Lord said unto them also: Go forth among the Lamanites, thy brethren, and establish my word; yet ye shall be patient in long-suffering and afflictions, that ye may show forth good examples unto them in me, and I will make an instrument of thee in my hands unto the salvation of many souls.I Feel like this is pretty relevant to me especially as we're sometimes having a tough time in the MTC (will probably not get easier once we get to Japan lol) But as we are willing to sometimes go against the norm, we will be strengthened in our trials 💪In the name of Jesus Christ, Amen.Bye for real :)
summary: Travelled to Japan, drank milk, built cardboard system, improved Japanese, growing district cohesion.

subject: Weekly Email #5 👞
body: In this email I tried to break stuff up into days a little and write more each day of the week; if you guys have any feedback, or just emails in general I'd love to write a response or get/give advice! I can only get back to yall on Saturdays but I wanna keep in touch where possible 📧❤️Sunday was great, during one of our long personal study sessions I wrote this poem:CryLord, rend my flesh-Carve Thy will upon my chest.Trim and clean and purify,Neatly snip and prod and pry.Lord, I am weak-Life's much harder than it seems.I'm begging for Thy support,Please, O God, hold up my fort.Lord, I see Thee-In my soul I feel thy peace.Oftentimes I felt I'd die;With Thy strength I made it by.I got to share it with our branch president and his wife, and they liked it so much they had me email it to them lol. We also had a great devotional that night; the highlight was definitely getting to hear brother Mackay share about how he assigned himself to hometeach his future wife 😂Monday was also great, I got a lot of Japanese 🇯🇵 learning in, and worked on a bunch of language learning goals and stuff too.Tuesday we got to hear a devotional from Elder Bednar and it was amazing! He told us not to take notes about what he was saying as much as he wanted us to record what the spirit was informing us. My main takeaway is that there is so much evidence of the truth of the Book of Mormon being translated that it is factually undisputable that it cannot be not true, but that what is important is getting to have a testimony of it for ourselves, and about it's teachings. He also emphasized the importance of reading it in our lives, and that you should go from cover to cover reading, then reading by topic, and then reading and searching for patterns across the whole thing.Wednesday we learned a lot of Japanese food vocabulary and cultural etiquette, which was pretty fun. Apparently if you pass food to people with chopsticks and they grab it from you with their chopsticks it's a sign of death and you should never ever do it 😂Thursday we went over a bunch of vocabulary as well as did a lot of practice conversations and stuff along those lines.Friday we learned about asking people to share their questions and thoughts, and how to better ask questions ourselves. Today I also ate my first double-decker peanut butter banana sandwich of the mtc (possibly first ever I really don't remember) and it was kind of a big deal.This P-day / Saturday was pretty fun, although a lot of it was spent in the laundry room this time around: my companion's washing machine decided it wanted to be a bank vault instead of a washing machine, and we waited like 2 hours for a repairman to arrive and to fix it. Besides that I've just been writing emails and shining shoes all day. Almost really messed up my brown shoes but it somehow worked out in the end lol. Guess you shouldn't use black shoe polish on brown shoes, but as an artist I just couldn't accept that 🤷 so I semi-suffered the consequences.Can't think of much more to write atm (also time grows short lol) but as always I love you guys! Yes you! And you!Thanks for reading if you have :)-Elder Killian (earnestly hanging in there)Spiritual message??!! ❤️‍🔥Been keeping up on reading Alma, albeit a little slower this week. Here's a couple verses I liked thoughAlma 29:8-98 For behold, the Lord doth grant unto all nations, of their own nation and tongue, to teach his word, yea, in wisdom, all that he seeth fit that they should have; therefore we see that the Lord doth counsel in wisdom, according to that which is just and true. 9 I know that which the Lord hath commanded me, and I glory in it. I do not glory of myself, but I glory in that which the Lord hath commanded me; yea, and this is my glory, that perhaps I may be an instrument in the hands of God to bring some soul to repentance; and this is my joy.I really love these couple verses because they voice how I feel about my mission better than I could. Getting to teach in Japanese to the Japanese People is super exciting. While it’s definitely not easy, I'm so grateful to God for the opportunity to share of Him, His Son, and the Spirit.Love yall again, cya soon (-.-)Zzz・・・・(Fresh haircut btw)***to respond to KADEN, please email him at kaden.killian@missionary.org
summary: Email outlines weekly progress, shares poem, discusses devotional insights, details language learning.

subject: Weekly email #6 🤯
body: Gonna keep trying to go with the daily writing stuffSunday I was able to do a lot of personal study which was nice, and during the Go and Do experience I rewatched “the phone call” and a very old church video called “man's search for happiness”. It was fun to have a bit of nostalgia with the phone call, but beyond that I learned a lot about the value of lasting relationships and about how central the family is in the GospelMonday was also good, me and my companion had our first 25 minute HOA and it definitely didn't go perfectly, but it was decent. (IDK if I've even explained what an “HOA” [Helping Others Appointment] is, but in case I haven't it's basically someone whose native language is Japanese in this case, and is either acting or genuinely seeking to know more about the church (much less likely lol), and we basically practice teaching to them) Monday I also got a lot more Japanese practice in throughout the 6 hours of classes. We learned a lot about making and asking questions in Japanese, and it's crazy how flip flopped the sentences get when compared to English 😵‍💫Tuesday we had our General Authority devotional and we got to hear from Elder and Sister Bassett, which was really neat. I loved both of their talks, and I feel like my main takeaway from what I heard was that one of the best things we can use our time for as members of the church (especially at this age) is being a missionary. We are in the water, and we're trying to help others find for themselves how it feels, through baptism especially. Also got me thinking about the themesong for The Chosen 😂I also wrote another poem in the 5 minutes of journal time we had before our group devotional breakdown: (will have to send it next week sorry, short on time 😅)Wednesday was a good day (any day where I'm learning is a good day), but very exhausting, I think I might be catching a cold again and it was not very fun talking for such a long time.  ThursdayFelt a little worse on Thursday, but nothing crazy. We learned a lot of different sentence strengtheners, one of which (という) genuinely can't cohesively translate very well into English which makes it pretty tricky to learn 😅 We've pretty much learned all the pieces of sentence structure stuff but we are just now trying to incorporate it together.Friday was good, but I definitely woke up super stuffy and runny which was not very fun. The rest of the day was aight though.P-day/Saturday was good, but my voice was almost gone. We had one morning class and then had the rest of the day to shine shoes, do laundry, play volleyball and buy some cough drops (as well as some BYU creamery ice cream) super excited for Sunday and Tuesday, but I'm pretty doubtful as to my singing capabilities in the choir practices 😅 I was waaaay too loud and I'm pretty certain I will not be able to talk at all tomorrow…Well thanks again for this issue of Elder Killians issues. I'm hoping I'll feel a lot better next week, very exciting that we only have a couple weeks left at the MTC 👀 I cannot wait for Japan (or likely a reassignment lol)Love you guys!!!!!!!!!!!-Elder KillianSpiritual AfterthoughtAlma 3416 And thus mercy can satisfy the demands of justice, and encircles them in the arms of safety, while he that exercises no faith unto repentance is exposed to the whole law of the demands of justice; therefore only unto him that has faith unto repentance is brought about the great and eternal plan of redemption.Pretty to the point scripture, which I like. Faith is so emphasized in religion and is such a basic step that I feel like people sometimes forget just how crucial it is. having faith in the first place is a challenge, but I think that strengthening it rather than just letting it sit there is easier said than done. Keep at it!!Bye 👋Elder Killian Kaden.killian@missionary.org
summary: Grew through daily writing, rewatched old videos, practiced Japanese, attended HOA, listened to devotional, wrote poem, felt sick.

subject: Weekly Email #7 (wow)
body: First of all...(Promised poems)    Darkest of⭐️All the⭐️Time's slowed⭐️Down came⭐️Christ lights⭐️Up we⭐️Look to    Jesus(Read the first word of every line marked with a ⭐️ for a different experience)Gifts of the SpiritA man told me onceThat I had been blessed with a few gifts.At the time I had considered his speechAs a “throwaway line” and didn't really give it much thought.In my newfound knowledge I realize that I was quite the dunce.As I study now the wisdom I missed,My younger self (if I couldst) I wouldst beseech:“Please young man, consider the gifts that you've got.”Sunday (today) was great! I slept for probably 2 extra hours after breakfast cause I wasn't feeling well, but after that I went to sacrament meeting and it was good. Our Go and Do experience was pretty nice, I listened to chapters 36 and 37 of Alma on the Book of Mormon app (as per my father's request) and enjoyed the various voices for the prophets. I also watched a couple neat church videos, here's the link to both.Better Off on My Own?https://mediasrv.churchofjesuschrist.org/media-services/GA/type/6148607791001/hls.m3u8Treasure in Heaven: The John Tanner Storyhttps://mediasrv.churchofjesuschrist.org/media-services/GA/type/2738021620001/hls.m3u8After our evening devotional we got to share together as a district and that's always great 👍 Monday was good, although my voice was still recovering from being sick. We learned a lot about asking meaningful questions in both our first and second classes. In the second class we had a veeeeery level headed substitute, which was not a very good match for my throwaway humor and comments lol. If he wasn't gonna learn something profound from your comment you might as well have not shared at all 😂 Definitely learned sone important things about sharing in a meaningful way, but I do think he was slightly discounting the value of some general social aspects but hey to  each his own 🫠 Besides classes nothing super stood out todayTuesday we had another substitute, but she was much less strict and serious than the other sub (even though they are apparently married lol) so I feel like I was much less concerned about what to say which was a plus. We learned some sentence structure stuff for the first part of class, then shared testimonies and read scriptures for the last hour. I learned a lot spiritually and pertaining to Japanese. We also had a devotional from Brother and Sister Wright (Sister Wright is the first counselor of the primary general presidency), in which I mainly took away that a big part of my success as a missionary will be determined by what I do when I get home, which I hadn't really thought super deeply about. Wrote another poem afterwards too :)(Will send next week lol sorry)Wednesday was probably aight, but my head hurt pretty bad for most of the day and I really can't remember a ton. We did find out thar for our last week and a half ish we won't get to have our same teachers which everyone is pretty ticked about; I'm honestly fine with the idea just to get a new perspective from recently returned missionaries.Thursday was also probably good but I've still been pretty congested and coughing all day so everything is definitely a little skewed. Hopefully tomorrow I will finally be feeling better.Friday was fun, was our last class with Sister Brough though. We went over schedules and phone call etiquette In Japanese for classes today. We shared our testimonies in English at the end which was awesome, very sweet last moment to spend together.Saturday we went to the Provo City Center temple after morning class Which was awesome! I forgot just how beautiful it was inside, and it was super neat to see the new endowment presentation. The rest of the day I mostly Just ate snacks and talked with my family which was great. Felt a lot better today which was a relief, but I'd love to get all the way better by next week. We'll have to see 🤞I got my Visa on Friday which was nice, means I'm not being reassigned which I really wouldn't have minded (I'll go wherever the Lord needs me to go)I only have a week (and a few days) until I'm flying to Japan!! My Japanese is probably better than I would have expected, the gift of tongues Is coming in clutch 🔥Love you guys as always, bye!!Spiritual thoughtI'm really pushing the time limits that I have lol, so for now I'd like to just promise that if you are genuinely concerned about something and are open to the idea, Pray about it. God will work his hand, whether or not it's immediate. Its worked for me, I know it can work for you.

subject: Weekly email 8 🌋
body: SundayToday we had sacrament meeting, elders quorum, “go and do”, a devotional, and a special hour or so of some dude singing and playing the guitar (his last name was Deere, I can remember that much). It was a pretty busy day, and I was still pretty congested but I made it through it.MondayToday we had our last HOA which was a little emotional for me (pretty sure my companion could care less about being done 😂); I am very appreciative for everything that Izumi-san has helped us learn, regardless of If she's feigning not knowing about the church. We had an alright but pretty stale class with our first new instructor, and later we had Class with our other new teacher, which I think was a little more energetic at least. TuesdayToday I also went to the clinic to get prescribed some medicine, and me and my companion got to walk a block or two to a pharmacy for some. Afterwards we had one run of the mill afternoon class, but our Evening Devotional was with Elder Gong which was awesome. I totally forgot how much he loved telling dad jokes and I feel like I really connected over that 😂 He spoke a lot about not just being obedient but being eagerly obedient; obeying commandments out of a desire, not just because we've been asked to. WednesdayOn Wednesday we had the regular 7 hours of class, within which we practiced promising blessings for keeping and making commitment. We also filled out a bunch of surveys since we're almost at the end of our MTC stay. I also did a little drawing so I'll attach a few photos.Thursday we had the cafeteria shift, which I was absolutely vibing with, although That was probably not the popular opinion among my district lol. While we were behind the scenes preparing food I also saw some stairs leading into the ground; Pretty sure they have a dungeon back there 👀 Pretty much all of the latter end of this week we've been making paper cranes so I'll attach a few pictures.Friday we had a pretty normal two classes, but we said our last official goodbyes to Brother Marks. He gave us each a signed mission card from his mission, and we all got to sign a big Japanese flag that he had. In classes we are mostly just reviewing Stuff at this point, but we talked about mostly how to teach according to someone's needs rather than just a lesson plan. today I also finished Alma (albeit I listened to the audio) but I'd still like to go through and mark The stuff I really got a lot out of.Saturday (P-Day)Fun fact P day in Japanese is じゅうんびのひ which literally just means preparation day but it is pretty fun to say lol. Today I talked with fam, played an hour of spikeball in the very hot sun, an hour of volleyball in the gym, and shone my shoes. Pretty run of the mill P day but I definitely had fun, I think my shoes look pretty fun too, I've definitely been using a lot of black on brown 👞This will be my last email sent from America!! (For the next two ish years at least lol) I am super ready for Japan; there's so much I don't know and also so much that I know I won't understand, but that doesn't matter as long as I'm doing my best, and what the Lord would be having me do.Love you guys!!》Spiritual thought《As I've been reading through the war chapters of Alma, it's been interesting to draw parallels between the time of the Nephites and Lamanites. Alma 48 reads:8 Yea, he had been strengthening the armies of the Nephites, and erecting small forts, or places of resort; throwing up banks of earth round about to enclose his armies, and also building walls of stone to encircle them about, round about their cities and the borders of their lands; yea, all round about the land. 9 And in their weakest fortifications he did place the greater number of men; and thus he did fortify and strengthen the land which was possessed by the Nephites.Just as Moroni was strengthening the people where they were more weak, we also need to especially fortify ourselves where we are more prone to temptations or tuning out the Spirit. If you genuinely need help in an area God will make it happen if you are doing all you can-Elder KillianKaden.killian@missionary.org

subject: I'm in...
body:
Japan!

Weekly email #9

Sunday Today we had a departure devotional, which was pretty dang awesome. I took a few notes from Sunday, so I'll let myself do the talking instead of myself. (Screenshot below)

Monday Today We had our last class with one of our replacement teachers, and the class after that we had a Sub teacher too 😂 today I got most of my pre-packing, and mailed home some excess materials. I am pretty sure I've got plenty Of space for everything, it's just fitting it safely and efficiently that will be tough. Made some more cranes as well.

Tuesday Today was our last full day at the MTC

Wednesday/Thursday Today started at 1:11 AM 😱we traveled for a very very long time. Hard to believe we're finally leaving the MTC We flew for around 20 hours, and took trains for maybe an hour after getting to Japan.

Friday Today at the Mission home we did a bunch of learning about various parts of missionary life, as well as even a little streeting practice which was definitely a little nerve-racking but I know it'll just take practice.

Saturday Today we found out our trainers and our first areas, and went out and traveled to our respective places. Today we had dinner with Mai-san and Nao-san; they are curious about the church from what it seems, although we didn't talk a ton about it tonight. Mai-san was especially laughing constantly, it was pretty awesome. I can understand the general idea of most things they would say, but it's pretty overwhelming hearing from so many fluent speakers all at once. There's a handful of things that just fly over my head. I got to unpack a bit tonight, although ATM everything is pretty messy, and needs some work.

Sunday Today we got to go to a devotional With Elder Cook in a huge event center, there were tons of people! We got to sing in the choir although the low-key song got pretty messed up near the end 😱 after the devotional we did some personal study and then walked and rode busses and trains for ages to get to a member's house to watch fireworks And eat curry. It was mega pretty, it was definitely the craziest fireworks show I've seen And I don't even know if there was a big holiday 😄

Monday Today is my new P-Day, so sorry for the delay in my email! Today we called family, made some pancakes, and got to spectate a Super Smash bros tournament at the church building (although we did play skull King the whole time) afterwards I was jumping to catch a Frisbee but came down on one foot and probably sprained it or worse; It's pretty painful to do anything with it, so hopefully it will get better soon so I can ride the bike that I still need To buy 😏

This week has been pretty wild, and although a large portion of it was taken up just by traveling, a lot really did happen that was noteworthy. I don't have many brain cells left at this point, but thanks for sticking around to read these emails!

Love you!! -Elder Killian (キリアン長老)

Spiritual Message God loves us more than we can fathom. It's pretty simple but I think that we sometimes push it to the side; we literally just can't understand how much we are loved! If we did than I don't think we'd have anything to complain about!

Byeeeee

subject: Warm Fishy Pudding 🍮
body:
Hello!

Tuesday Today because of my foot (more explanation about the foot below) we mostly just stayed at the apartment and used Hellotalk and Facebook to try and find new friends. I've also been added into about 40 group chats (all of which I cannot read the names of lol) and we spent a lot of time sending short personal introductions. For some of today we actually walked and did a little shopping; we needed sheets, a pillow, some more soap for the apartment, an ankle brace and a few other things. It was pretty fun, but the walking part was not lol.

Wednesday Today we taught a lady named じ which is probably written as Xi but is pronounced as Jee. She has been reading the Book of Mormon with the missionaries for a while, and it was fun to read with her. She's had a tough time understanding some parts but we discussed a lot about the difficult stuff and it was great. We invited her to pray about it, and I hope she'll follow through on it! Tonight was basketball night, and while everyone was playing I talked for like an hour to a kid named Ryo. He is like 17 and he's going to Ensign college in Utah next year for basketball. He tore his ACL or something so we got to just chill on the sidelines. We talked a lot about music, and he was pretty blown away by all of the Japanese artists I like, he says a lot of them are like way famous in Japan. He's really nice, I hope stuff goes well for him in Utah!

Thursday For weekly planning my companion Elder Sonomura has a tradition where your first planning session you need to go and buy a corndog (American dog in Japanese アメリカンド一グ). Was actually pretty good, most of 7/11’s food is pretty solid. Today we taught Shin-san, who has also been taught by the missionaries for a decent while. He's definitely got some issues with smoking and depression but hopefully the gospel can be a source of relief. We talked about the Godhead for most of the time, and it was pretty neat. Tonight we walked 30 minutes to HAMA-SUSHI, a conveyor belt sushi place. I asked the 3 other missionaries I was with (companion included) to order me the strangest stuff they could find on the menu. why? I'm not quite sure, but I definitely tried a few new things including: Salty and burning hot fishy pudding, squid and sea urchin sushi, 3 sticky sushi rolls (nato, some kind of slimy pepper and some blended up tuna or something), and one other odd piece of sushi I don't remember. Was a fun experience though!

Friday Taught 2 lessons played Kahoot ate some Japanese lasagna

Saturday Today we did some housing (some is pretty generous, we knocked on maybe 8 doors) and a man answered and was genuinely interested in the church. He said we could come back and visit him to talk more about it soon, so that was a big miracle. Tonight was also sports night, and even though I was spectacting due to my injury most of the time, I played some ping-pong with one guy (shoutout Ben) and he was actually a genius. He played for like five years he said, and he could curve that ping pong ball wherever he wanted. I was astonished. There was also a guy who showed up to sports night who talked to me about anime and thinks I am a Japanese genius. He's not a member but he's expressed some interest, hopefully we will be able to teach him soon. He thinks I'm a “ ******* genius ” at speaking Japanese apparently 😂 we'll definitely have to teach him about using nice language lol, the English he does know is certainly a bit explicit 😂

Sunday At church I got to introduce myself (I actually asked the guy leading Sacrament meeting if I could lol) and shared a brief testimony about the importance of how we share and feel God's love. Afterwards we somehow got roped into the young men's class and I ended up trying to decipher a paper about scriptures with a boy named Souma. It didn't help that he also did not understand what we were trying to do, but it was fun to be trying my best for the sake of the Gospel lol. After classes were over, we also got roped into the ward choir, which was honestly hilarious. I do not know any musical vocabulary so a lot of it was listening to very friendly criticisms and points of improvement that I had no idea how to utilize 😂 Was still fun, and it sounded pretty good too. Definitely loved seeing that conductor lady trying to get all of the sopranos and altos to sing more “cutely” when like all of them were in their 60-70s 😂😂

P-day Today was a baller P-day and I didn't even get injured this time! (Last P I jumped to catch a Frisbee and landed on a guitar case and really messed up my ankle. (Finally feeling a little better, still can't move it side to side though) Anyways this P-day we did all sorts of stuff. First, in the morning we went to the church as usual to call our families, and very shortly after (sorry family!) we had to hurry over to a big building called Onikuru (pretty sure it roughly translates into demon coming) and volunteered for an hour helping teach kids some English. Not very heavy emphasis on teach because we probably spent half of the class singing and dancing in front of 2 girls who did not want to have anything to do with the class 😂 The lady in charge of the operation had a daughter who helped teach and she was super nice and actually pretty good at English too. It was great lol. After dancing, some lady on the first floor asked us very nicely if we could come and watch a police squad band performance, and it was awesome. They also gave us a bag with some safety messages and an assortment of random little toiletries for some reason lol. Low-key was grateful for the tissues, cause I'm catching a cold and we ran out of tissues at the apartment. After leaving Onikuru, we got lunch, then headed back to the apartment after grocery shopping and took a little nap. After we woke up, we went pretty far out of our way to shop for a camera. I have wanted one since I got here, and it was a relief to find one. Was definitely pretty pricey but there weren't a ton of options, and I'm pretty sure if I'm careful it will last me as long as I should need it. We ended our day with McDonald's which honestly tasted more like Wendy's than McDonald's (I'm a fan of Wendy's usually so it wasn't bad).

This week was crazy for sure, we taught at least one lesson pretty much every day, but it was very fulfilling!

Love you guys!!!! -Elder Killian キリアン長老 Ki-ri-a-n-chou-rou

Really enjoyed reading these verses in companion study today. If you find anything in here you like and wanna talk about with me, I'd love to email you when I can!

17 And it came to pass after I, Nephi, having heard all the words of my father, concerning the things which he saw in a vision, and also the things which he spake by the power of the Holy Ghost, which power he received by faith on the Son of God—and the Son of God was the Messiah who should come—I, Nephi, was desirous also that I might see, and hear, and know of these things, by the power of the Holy Ghost, which is the gift of God unto all those who diligently seek him, as well in times of old as in the time that he should manifest himself unto the children of men. 18 For he is the same yesterday, today, and forever; and the way is prepared for all men from the foundation of the world, if it so be that they repent and come unto him. 19 For he that diligently seeketh shall find; and the mysteries of God shall be unfolded unto them, by the power of the Holy Ghost, as well in these times as in times of old, and as well in times of old as in times to come; wherefore, the course of the Lord is one eternal round.

Bye bye :)
summary: Received email about Japanese amateur radio license info, will reply soon.

subject: This week 🍔🍩🏸💯
body: TuesdayToday was a little exhausting due to catching a cold (I shall never escape sickness lol) but it was still a good day. We had my district counsel meeting and we had to ride trains for like 30 minutes to get there; we shared a few stories and did a couple mogis to practice our teaching. After we were done, we went to eat at a restaurant all together; I got some shrimp Tempura and it was really good. On the way home I bought a couple of donuts for some reason lol. The first had anko (sweet bean paste) inside and I really enjoyed it. I'm glad, because I have heard a lot of foreigners do not lol. When we got back, we did studies for a while then went to the Fukuda’s home to have a meal and teach a short spiritual message. Brother and Sister Fukuda are like 70 or 80 and they are super nice; our dinner was very traditionally Japanese and consisted of crushed up Doritos in a bowl with various taco materials 😂 Definitely not what I was expecting. Was very tasty though~WednesdayToday Elder Berg and I taught the beginner Eikaiwa class which was very fun. A lot of the class was pretty sidetracked but it was probably still good English practice for the students lol. Afterwards we played Badminton (we--meaning everyone besides me because of my foot lol) but I did get to spectate and draw, which was fun. Also did a little experimenting with my new camera.ThursdayTsuji sanBasketballFridayOn Friday I don't remember a lot from the day, we probably taught a lesson or something but I'm writing this on P-Day so I'm unsure 😂 However, Friday night I do remember we ate hamburger steak (basically just hamburger patties lol) with cheese and rice and ketchup and it was amazing. I could only eat a couple of them, but it was great 🍔 This was for a YSA activity, and afterwards people just kind of sat around and talked. I sat with a dude named Yuuhi and talked for like an hour, he's super cool. He loves eating all kinds of bugs (cooked of course) and I get the feeling we are gonna eat some together soon 👀 his favorite things are camping and Harry Potter for some reason. He loves SciFi but his favorite movies and books are Harry Potter, it cracks me up 😂SaturdaySaturday we had a gyoza making party with the YSA that could come which were literally 3 people besides me, my companion, and or roomates, so that's 4 missionaries and 3 YSA lol. It took us like 2 hours to buy and prepare the gyoza as well as another hour of talking and eating. It was amazing. I ate probably 15 gyoza and I had to hold back so more people could eat lol.SundayWe went to a members son’s baptismBike get (got a bike and I am in pain from the seat)Meal at the IkawasMondayTrainZone Conference (will write more about this next week if I can, Lotta stuff happened)HirakataTuesday (This week's Pday because of Zone conference)Today was a crazy Pday. We went to the other area’s church to call family and meet up with our district (some of them anyways) to go to the MR. Donut museum and get some chicken with a member named Shingo. It was pretty fun and the donuts at the end were fire. There was one donut (I did not buy) that said “Mexican Meat” on it and I really don't understand what that means, but it's a little frightening. 😂 After our lengthy excursion (after which I talked with Shingo-San for like an hour) Elder Sonomura and I met up with someone I found on HelloTalk named Monica, and we talked and ate for an hour or two, was super fun. She speaks Pretty solid English but mostly spoke Japanese cause she said she was shy lol. We ate some Okonomiyaki, which was really good and I'm pretty sure my mom would have loved the one I ordered.Sorry a couple days were very briefly described, but I'm pretty short on time particularly due to how long it took us to get home from eating food with Monica-San (Pray for Elder Sonomura to be led spiritually but mostly directionally 😂😅)Love you guys!!-Elder KillianSpiritual messageVery brief today, but wherever you're at in life, I'd invite you to simply write down 3 things you're grateful for. If you can write down at least 1 thing a night you're grateful for, you might be surprised by the end of the week, or month 😁😁
summary: Sick Tuesday: meetings, train rides, donuts; Wednesday Eikaiwa class, Thursday P-Day, Friday YSA activity, tried new camera.

subject: This week I... did missionary work 😎
body: Alright first I would like to write a bit about how my first Zone conference went. I volunteered myself to do a brief training since we live with the ZL's so I spoke for around 5 minutes about how we can apply the 4th Article of Faith to our mission. I wasn't told I was gonna do it in Japanese (probably could have assumed I guess) so I pretty much winged it with my English notes. Definitely didn't share as much as I would have originally liked but it's OK. After our beginning trainings and stuff we ate some lunch, and did a bunch of mogis and stuff afterwards. It was pretty fun, and we definitely learned a lot, but the AP's gave us a pretty big goal of 10 meaningful contacts a day. Pretty crazy for how stuffs been going lately but I would like to meet it. At the end of Zone conference, I got… the SPIKEBALL NET 😍😍😍😍😍(shoutout District 22K, we played spikeball almost every morning at the MTC) Streeting is gonna be so easy now I just gotta play spikeball with Elder Sonomura every day it'll be great 👍  Wednesday Today Elder Ishizaka and I taught the beginner class, which was pretty loud. There was one pretty shy dude who showed up and made friends with a super short energetic lady and they were like cackling the whole time lol. When I was practicing with a dude named Toyama, and we were doing an A B (usually A asks questions, and B responds) english practice, and somehow we ended up both asking the questions 😂😂 We both thought it was pretty funny. There were also two pretty gangster looking dudes who showed up and one of them said his name was like Saint Louis the Third or something, dude was wild 😂Afterwards we played some badminton as usual (still mostly spectated btw) and talked to some people which was fun. Earlier in the day we went to find a street piano for Elder Sonomura to play, and we did find it but some lady was using it, so we waited for her to finish, and got a chance to talk to her. She was playing a bunch of pretty basic kids piano classics, and a couple were from Hawaii apparently, so Elder Sonomura talked to her about that 😁😁 She said she forgot her phone number (I believe that she actually did, she was a bit spacey, can't blame her though, she was 80 years old and like so energetic you wouldn't believe it) so hopefully we can meet her there again soon.ThursdayWe had basketball night, which was spectated by me 😂 not much to say there. We also had weekly planning and got American dogs again which are always a vibe. I also manned up and bought a whole quart of milk to drink which was quite lovely, probably enjoyed the milk more than the American dog 😂FridayThis morning we unfortunately missed the 12 week program meeting that we had scheduled, and had to do it the day after 😓 It ended up going well but I felt bad for Sister Stewart; my phone didn't have wifi so I didn’t see we had it scheduled. Today during our YSA activity (we ate Taco Rice) during the institute portion we got to teach Nao and Mai again. We showed them a video about Christ which may have left them with a lot of questions, but they really seem to like the Gospel which is great 👍Earlier that day we actually did a little streeting and ran into a lady who was super interested genuinely about the church and what we were saying,  which never happens 😂 Elder Sonomura says it's literally his first time that's happened when he was streeting so I must just bring good luck 😏SaturdayToday we started by going to the Mall with Nao and Motoi San, which was great. We spent probably like 3 hours there and bought a bunch of random stuff as well as ate at a Hawaiian restaurant which Elder Sonomura was thrilled about.Elder Ishizaka accidentally sent a video with me changing in the background to the Zone chat 😂 so he had to remove everyone from it and make a new one (technically they might still be able to see the video but it’s probably OK 😂😂Bus streeting: Streeting people on a bus, works great when they can't really walk away from you 😂 not really, but people tend to be more willing to talk in that situation, so we definitely got a few meaningful contacts. We also got some dinner with Brother Kadono who basically gave us intel on the inactive members as well as good places to street. I'm pretty sure he's 80 years old, and he's super cool.SundayWe did an AB lesson for a pretty recently baptized member before church, at church I gave my testimony for fun and basically told all the old people and children that even if they aren't missionaries they can still do missionary work. We had a lesson with this super cool dude from China who's family is all Korean and now lives in Japan and speaks like perfect Japanese and Korean too. His name is Yu, and he wears white gloves and he's like the most stylish person I've ever seen. He said we should go streeting together which is dope 💯 After that we had ward choir which is literally always hilarious to me 😂 It's like getting to be in the middle of a sitcom for free except everyone is dead serious about it. After church we actually went and did some spikeball finding and made friends with two high schoolers, which isn't like amazing but you gotta start from somewhere 😂 my companion asked if they had a phone number so we could play it again someday and they looked at each other and said nah 😂😂 Pretty sure we won't be exchanging contact info with high schoolers anymore lol.PdayThis morning we called family as usual (Always great, love you guys 😘) and afterwards got some lunch at a place called Sukiya (went there once before I don't remember if I mentioned it) and I accidentally ordered a kid's meal lol. After our food we went to the church and played assorted sports for around like 3 hours. I tried to do a bit more moving around, and I don't think it hurt much which was good 👍 we played volleyball for part of the time which I'm like thrilled about, loved that. When we were done, we went grocery shopping (lightly, the members send us so much food 😵) and managed to find a pretty solid tripod for social media post creation purposes. Also got some donuts at night with a member, the ZL's and a friend. Was very tasty and fun 😁Love love love you guys!! Hang in there! See ya soon! -Elder KillianSpiritual thoughtLiked this part of a conference talk, I really recommend it even if it's pretty old lol.“When all is said and done, the only real wealth of the Church is the faith of its people.”Questions and Answershttps://www.churchofjesuschrist.org/study/general-conference/1985/10/questions-and-answers?id=p14&lang=eng#p14
summary: First Zone conference volunteer training in Japanese, winged presentation, learned from APs' goal, played spikeball, taught beginner class, practiced A B with Elder Sonomura.

subject: Bye bye Ishizaka長老
body: Oct 22TuesdayToday was DCM, and afterwards a lovely exchange With Elder Wilson. Fun fact, it was actually his last exchange, and my first 😂 He gave me a lot of good advice about missionary work in general; we used a few different ways to reach out to people. We first did just a little streeting, then after went to a nearby station and did some sign streeting. Usually it doesn't go well from my experience, but like 10 seconds after I held up the sign, a lady walked right over to us, and is gonna bring her boyfriend to Eikaiwa in Okamachi (the area that Elder Wilson is from). Was a huge miracle! Later that night we also probably deposited around 100-150 flyers into people's mailboxes lol. Was very fun 😁 we also had our transfer video, which didn't change much besides Ishizaka長老 transferring to Akashi, which is sad 😢 But it'll be ok, we get Kyoumen長老 in his place; he's a riot.WednesdayToday before Eikaiwa, a dude named Sanchew came to the church to make music with Sonomura長老, as I drew on the sidelines lol. He was super nice, and his voice is extremely high 😂 he brought a guitar and was singing up a storm. He actually stayed at the church building for like 5 hours probably lol. We had Eikaiwa with him, and afterwards he even stayed for a decent while during Badminton. Was awesome! In Eikaiwa we talked almost only about food; Elder Berg has the advanced class structured basically as a big conversation, so we basically just talked about random foods in English for an hour lol.ThursdayToday we were planning to go to a festival for a bunch of the morning, but when we got there, oddly nobody was there. Turns out Sonomura長老 had the wrong month 😂 Anyways, I spent a lot of the day with Ishizaka長老, as he needed to impart his final wisdom to me before he transferred. I asked if I could call a bunch of inactive members numbers, and he taught me a bit, then let me do the rest 😁 It was a bit nerve-racking but great practice! We also did a lot of travel, getting from Ibaraki to Hirakata is an hour no matter how you get there lol. When we got back to Ibaraki, Ishizaka長老 and I taught a lesson to Mahinda兄弟, a new member in our ward. He's maybe the nicest dude I’ve met. There are low-key a lot of people I want to say that about, but it’s still true 😂 Later that night we had basketball as usual, and I was able to share some of my art while others played basketball; still fun. Afterwards, we taught a lesson, idk what it was about or who it was with, but I had it written down so I'm pretty sure it happened 😂 I could just check the “Area Book” but I've gotta write, so for now just pretend it was a super touching story 👍👍FridayWe met with Mahinda兄弟 again, and this time he bought us all donuts, again super nice dude. Later we ate again at the Fukudas, this time it was okonomiaki, but I was actually so close to throwing up from how much I ate. They would not stop making food 💀 Later I somehow was feeling ok, and we met again with Rui. He's again super nice, and feels like an American friend, but he's just gotta get to more church and activities, He's still a member but just thinks the other church members here are weird 😂 We will fix this 👍👍👍 😂 Anyways, he also told us he works for JT, which is apparently Japan Tobacco or something 😂 he says he doesn't tell his boss about his beliefs or anything or he'd probably get fired lol. He still works there because it pays well lol. That night at the Gathering Place, I pretty much taught Nao a lesson all by myself while everyone else taught a big lesson a few chairs away from us lol. Shes not a member, but says she wants to be a senior missionary in Utah 😂😂 love the passion, let's just move forward then lol. After we got home, we played our last game of Catan with Ishizaka長老, pretty sure Berg長老 won 😢SaturdayToday we got some more Indo Curry with President Sano’s son. I got the spicest that they had, and it was perfectly fine lol. I gotta find some real spicy stuff someday 😂 We had a fun meeting at the church for our “ward conference eve”. Later we got to visit Moriguchi san’s apartment, her kids are the cutest ever 😂😂SundayChurch taught primary for about 5 mins,afterwards had lunch with Moriguchi and Toyama San. Toyama only made it to the luncheon part, but it was awesome; he got to talk a bunch with the members! Also got to say hi to Yu兄弟 again, this time he told me he specifically wants to be a captain in Germany so he can pilot a German made ship. Love this guy.PdayToday we went shopping for most of the day lol. First we went to a cards/game shop, which was very neat, also kind of crazy how cheap some old games were (and how expensive some others were lol). We tried to visit a cool big statue but it was unfortunately closed 😕 was still fun to bike there though! Near the place of the statue there was a massive mall, so we went and looked around, was very fun. Not a bunch else to say unfortunately lol.Sorry some stuff isn't super detailed, I'm typing like a menace ATM 😂 Love you guys!!!I mean it!! I will try to get back to yall next Pday, sorry 😅Bye! -キリアン長老Today's spiritual message is simple; think of something/someone you are grateful for, and think about why you are grateful for it/them. Pray about it, and let God know you are appreciative, and I promise that you will be able to feel that he hears that. It's true :)Bye!Elder Killian Kaden.killian@missionary.org
summary: Oct 22: DCM with Elder Wilson, fun fact; Oct 23: Music session at church with Sanchew, Oct 24: Festival planning failure with Ishiz

subject: Elder Killian's Uber Lengthy Email Edition Deluxe
body:
(Still probably not even as long as one of Elder Wheeler's normal weekly emails, and this is for 2 weeks!! Bro has got a gift)

Tuesday Today, we went to Osaka Umeda to meet up with Mai for a lesson. It was awesome, and we basically invited her to change her work schedule so she can go to sacrament meetings on Sundays. It's a big ask, but it seems like she should be able to. Afterwards, we went to a few different art stores (a dangerous place for me) and I got a couple fun pens to give to Nao, since she hurt her back recently 😢 I did also of course buy some art stuff 😂. Afterwards we just did a lot of the usual missionary work, but it was good 😁

Wednesday Today we went to a humongous park in Hirakata, and streeted there for a decent while. We met a lady with a flute, and a lady with a dog in a stroller. The lady with the flute was super nice, but not very interested in anything gospel related; the lady with the dog was very nice, maybe a liiiitttle bit crazy 😂 She was very full of energy. Turns out she's actually been to a Christian church once as a kid, and kind of likes the ideas surrounding Christianity. Hopefully she will call our phone, cause she didn't have any contact info to give us! 😵😂 Today Sonomura長老 and I tallied up some of our travel for reimbursements; I only got one sheet filled out and it was already so much 😂 Today we also got some cheap halloween costume stuff from a yard sale type thing, and got some good information about Hirakata from the people running it. That night we dressed up (lightly lol, I had a cane and a hat, and Sonomura長老 wore a Hawaiian shirt with some ties tied to his hands lol; He was going for a 3-headed look) for Eikaiwa. It was super fun (and funny), we had a blast by roleplaying trick or treating with some doors in the church Hallway. When Toyama came up to my door, he said super stern and serious “trick. Or. Treat.” And in reply I told him “Go away” 😂 We both just laughed so hard 😂😂. It was great 😂

Thursday Sign streeting Basketball night Eikaiwa Asked a bunch of places if we could hang up our Eikaiwa posters, one place that said yes was the Train Station 😂😂

Friday We spent almost the whole day doing our weekly planning! It was great, but extremely draining 😅 It was also very rainy, and I was very wet and cold riding my bike 😢 (Did get a pizzamon btw, made my day bearable 😂😂)

Saturday and Sunday Over the weekend we had a huge stake conference, in which Sonomura 長老 and I sung in the choir. We had a 2 hour practice on Saturday, and another 1 hour practice on Sunday. It was so exhausting 😂 I think we sounded pretty good though! 👍👍 On Sunday we found a spider in the apartment that somehow escaped from brother Marks and made its way to Ibaraki. We let him chill so he can eat the cockroaches but I wouldn't mind not seeing him again 😅😂 Today, Mitani shimai (very nice lady from the ward) gave me a 5 pack of curry soup for my birthday which I cannot wait to try!

Pday Card shop, game shop, dinner With yuuhi at hamazushi (low key think its better than sushiro, hot take 😵😂) Yuuhi loves reading manga, and his favorite manga is WELCOME TO DEMON SCHOOL IRUMA-kun 🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥 I haven't actually read the manga but its definitely my favorite anime, he even gave me a sticker from it 😍😍 I was kind of mind blown 💥 oh yeah, to end the day off with a bang, I was going down a staircase 2 at a time (as usual), and because the steps were longer than usual ((they were very long)) I landed sideways on my left foot 🤦‍♂️ yeah its sprained now too 😂 My right foot was finally feeling good enough to start trying more movement, but for now it's back to little to nothing. Hopefully it'll heal alright 🙏

My birthday! (Tuesday) Today we had our DCM, beforehand I actually got to quickly call my family as well; they had sent a small package with my cousin when she went to Japan. I somehow got a chance to get it before my birthday 😂 It was awesome 😁 DCM also went well, we did a couple of trainings and then mogi'd some streeting at the end. I don't know if I've ever Actually Explained, but “mogi” is how We say “roleplay” Essentially. There's probably a more specific explanation but its good enough for me 😂. Afterwards DCM we went together to Seizeria, my first time~ It was pretty good, and would have been very cheap if the pizza wasn't pretty small; definitely ordered 2 Entrées. Later that day, Sonomura長老 and I went to our church building and met Nao and Mai to prepare for a study session with an almost high schoolers in our ward. Shes just having a stressful time at school trying to make some big decisions, so Nao and Mai wanted to help out. Before the study, Nao and Mai bestowed upon me 2 presents, as well as a chocolate cake/pie (Not sure) and a happy birthday song. They are so sweet 😢 We then proceeded to the study session, which I think was good. Not Sure I understood any of the Japanese math vocabulary though 🤔 That night while we were doing online contacts At the church, I got delivered Another cake by a ward member (like a super fancy one 😂 It even had like cool crispy chocolate inside of it), and a little later another cake (although technically a microwave cake mix 😉) with a few other treats (all dark chocolate, Not sure where they got the idea I love dark chocolate 😂 I do enjoy it but it was funny to see all of it. To end the day, Sonomura長老 and I went off in search of a pizzamon From a convenience store, and played a game of Pokémon as well. Pretty crazy birthday 😂

Wednesday Today was lovely Eikaiwa, Went well as usual 😁

Thursday Met with someone I talked with through Hellotalk, His name is Sou, his Birthday is actually on the same day as me 😂😂 was a crazy coincidence. We also did our weekly planning, which was really good. That night we had Basketball, it was good, but I definitely still just watched 😂

Friday Met with someone Sonomura長老 talked with through Tandem; his name was Sho. He was also super chill, and he was super Appreciative when we gave him a book of Mormon. He said he's gonna read it, I recommended he start with the intro, then go wherever he likes from there. Super Excited to hear from him! Tonight was the Gathering, Nao didn't want to hear a lesson from Sonomura長老 but she said she likes me so I ended up teaching 😂😅 She's like half joking and half serious, I don't think she understands a lot from Sonomura長老’s teaching for whatever reason 😂

Saturday Today We did some service at the same park we went to a month Ago, I went around with Shingo Kyoudai And picked up garbage from all kinds of places. It was fun, and good language practice as well; Shingo Kyoudai talks super fast 💀 I can get the gist of most things but a lot of words I know, he says so fast that I don't know them anymore 😂 Also had dinner at bishops house, his wife's mom taught us about calligraphy. Apparently she's like a big deal in Japan as far as calligraphy goes, it was super neat!

Sunday At church today I gave Yu Kyoudai a bunch of fantasy novel recommendations but I'm not sure any of them are translated into Korean… After Church we went with Toyamaさん and Yuukoさん to a shrine absolutely filled to the brim with Daruma. you Might have seen one before, but I'll make sure to attach a photo of one. At the end of our excursion, Sonomura長老 gave Toyamaさん a book of Mormon, pretty sure he's down to give it a read, glad he's moving forward 😁 Tonight we also played Hanafura, which seems fun but I lost the second round pretty much to just luck So I've been writing instead 😢

Pday (Monday) Today we went back to Kyoto And I actually git to take some cool pictures, butttt they are still on my camera so I can't exactly show Them off ATM. We went To that famous bamboo forest, some neat old Japanese houses and gardens, as well as went to a monkey mountain. The monkeys were dope, and slightly cheered me up about being ticked off about walking so much (and hiking) ((was not thrilled with my feet being in their current State)). Pretty Solid P-day, would have been great if I was more happy the whole time 😂 partly my B, but I am always learning.

Spiritual Thought/Challenge Yeah its not a thought sorry, this week I'd like you to share something uplifting with someone you've interacted with recently. You don't need to share something specific to the Gospel, and the person you share it with doesn't even have to be having a hard time, but I believe That this is a good thing to do 😁 Good luck! If you have a good experience I'd love to hear from you about it 😊

You guys are the best! I love each and every one of you!! I can't wait to see you all soon 😁😁❤️❤️

キリアン長老

subject: A peek into the mind of Elder Killian
body: The warm winter weather is throwing me offIf a Japanese person said to me "it's cold" I would scoff 😄Coming from the dry and icy Utah wintersHearing this weather is "comparable" would leave me in splinters.The news is out. Elder Mackay has transferred.He thought he'd be leaving, and that wish was indeed answered 😂I'm staying here in the Marugame branchUnrelated, I'm still upset I can't find ranch 😢 (to eat with pizza of course)As suggested by the title aboveThis email will be full of rhythm and love ;)I'm a poet by nature; I rhyme what I think.My wish is, today, that this poetry won't stink 😅At least not to the extent of the stench on the porchThat lurks and lingers before the great scorch.The scorch of course meaning the landfill's bright fire. 🔥If I said there weren't 3 full trash bags sitting on our balcony... well, I'd be a liar 😁I hope that these stanzas will give you a hunchAs to the condition of our apartment after lunch...A bowl in the sink, a plate on a chair-Which fortunately I convinced Elder Mackay to take care (of).Yet alas, now he's gone. And I don't mean to be mean...But with the new Elder Martinson this apartment will probably be much much more clean 😂One funny detail from my time with Elder MackayWas the time in the schedule he'd create for his... lacky 😓It's not quite as bad as it sounds, I assureBut the "tidy time" in our schedule sure passed by in a blur 😂As I spent the hour washing the windows with care,I'd glance over to see him asleep in his chair 😂But what can I say, I'm a charitable guy ;)If I'm the only one in the kitchen, [as long as there aren't onions] I won't cryThough... as a bit of revenge to add to the heap,I admit that I'd let him stay there asleep 😴 😂All in all this last transfer has been quite the blast!My time with Elder Mackay went by way too fast 🏃‍♂️I've done lots of work! But occasionally shiver...When I think of accidentally ordering a large serving of... liver 🐖Love you guys! Hope you enjoyed my poem ❤️ it was pretty fun to write 😂 might try to do this style of email every now and then. I'll try to give some more details as to what's actually going on in the area next week! A few things we did were: Celebrate the 50th anniversary of the Branch, eat some pretty expensive chicken, play ping pong, play some board games, biked like 4 hours one day, travel to pick up some Jojos Bizzare Adventure cups and Keychains from a dude, and schedule to meet up with a lady at a museum. Lots of fun stuff is happening, and I'm hoping that this transfer there's a good chance to help our friends progress! Looking forward to Martinson長老, keep him in your prayers! We need all the help we can get 😁You are all awesome! Love you! I'll try to respond when I can. Send me an email if you've got time! I'll definitely respond by the end of the year 🙃キリアン長老Kaden.killian@missionary.org
summary: Arrived warm Japan, sprained foot, transferred to new branch.

subject: Gold 🪙
body: TuesdayToday we did some more media finding with Jimoty, actually getting some good responses lately! We will meet at a museum in a couple days with one lady, and another lady exchanged lines! We sent some photos of our art to each other, and she'd like to meet up and draw together sometime. We also did around an hour of housing/attempted streeting. We didn't really run into anyone while we streeted, but we had a couple meaningful contacts while we housed an apartment complex. One lady listened to a short lesson, even if she wasn't interested in meeting again, and a nice guy talked to us for a bit, even though he said he didn't think he could make it to any of our events due to being of another religion. I'm still not super confident when housing and streeting; I feel like I haven't done a lot of it in the past couple transfers. Hoping to get a lot of good work done with Martinson長老!WednesdayDid some more housing, although it wasn't super successful. We got to talk to a few people and give out some flyers, but nobody was very interested. It was still a good effort though! (I know of at least one person who likes that word 🙃)Thursday⚠️INCOMING BLOCK OF TEXT⚠️Today was insanity, let's begin.We had to be up and at em’ right from the start of the day. Martinson長老 and I had to go to the hospital to figure out some medication for him. Going to a clinic and getting my foot checked out already required quite the stretch of vocabulary, so as you can imagine we requested the help of a member for translation. His name is Brother Nakamura, and he speaks pretty incredible English! He helped us figure stuff out for about 2 hours 🤯 It was a lot to take in, but it was a neat experience. There was also one super sweet lady who worked there who tried to use some English to help us out. She was so nice, and I wish that we'd had a flyer for our Eikaiwa class to give to her! In the future we will probably need to go back, so I'll make sure I'm ready! After our lengthy morning excursion, it was finally time for our museum visit with a young lady named Ami from Jimoty! The plan was to get some lunch together and then go to the museum, but she was running a little late, so Martinson長老 and I biked over to the ocean to look at the pretty landscape (seascape??) Anyways, while we were over there, we saw a lady with a trash bag and tongs picking up garbage, so we brought some we had noticed over to her. She told us she was 80 years old, was super appreciative, and after we had deposited some trash she told us to wait there and went to her car. She came back with a framed picture of the sunset, and some fancy Japanese sweets! She had us each take one treat from the package, and proceeded to go and hand out these treats to everyone in the near vicinity; 2 dudes in a truck, some construction worker, it was awesome lol. She didn't have any sort of contact info she could give us, but it was a very sweet interaction. We offered to help volunteer picking up trash, but she thinks we'd need to be Japanese; she did say that our hearts are Japanese though, so that's still a win 😂 After this semi-lengthy interaction, we had to book it over to the restaurant to meet with Ami. Fortunately we still got there first! Ami pulled up in a pretty big SUV, loves snowboarding like every week, has a motorcycle, and has dyed Blonde hair with sunglasses. Pretty fashionable if you ask me 🆒️ We ate some pretty tasty noodles, and then headed across the street to visit the museum. It had a bunch of pretty wild Spanish artists’ work, I definitely appreciated it but Martinson長老 left feeling probably a bit disturbed. It was his first time going to a museum, hopefully we can visit another few and change his perception of museums as a whole 😂 Ami seemed to enjoy the museum, and as we headed out the door, some random dude riding a bike stopped in front us and asked if we were missionaries for Marugame. He wasn't actually a member, but we convinced him to pull up to church on Sunday (He came!) Apparently he'd met with the sister missionaries before, and has a friend who goes to church every week! Super random, and Ami probably thought it was even more random/weird, but it was chill. After that strange interaction, we went thrifting with Ami, and proceeded to McDonald's to practice English, chat, and share a little spiritual message. Ami didn't seem to have a problem with the religious talk, and is mega interested in doing Eikaiwa with us! High hopes for her!!SaturdayThis is super random, but I have a small tool called a “sim popper” aka sim card remover that I use to remove the SD card or sim card from my phone. It looks a lot like a little paper clip bent into a silly shape. For reference, I keep it in a pocket inside of my wallet. Anyways, today I was walking outside and realized something like a pebble or some dirt got stuck inside my shoe, and when I shook it out, my sim popper fell out of the shoe onto my hand! It somehow managed to find its way out of my wallet in my pocket, down through my pants through a hole in my pocket, and finally slide its way in between my sock and my shoe. I can't believe that I didn't just lose it in that encounter 😂 From now on I'm going to keep it in a safer spot 🙃🤯 Today we visited a friend referred to our area with the sister missionaries, and he is super excited to meet and play games with us soon, super sweet guy.SundayHad a dope lesson with one friend named Bitou, Sister Takao Helped us out and shared her experiences too. We talked about Baptism and the Holy Ghost, it was super sweet, and I think we made great progress with him! Tomoya also pulled up to church, even if he only stayed for the first half. He's got such a funny relationship with the members, they are probably a bit forceful with trying to get him to come to church, but their hearts are in the right spot 😂 Today was a 10/10 Sunday, super good time with members, great talks, all around good time.MondayFun Pday, we ate Shabu Shabu with Ikeuchi兄弟 (he is always talking about how we should invest in gold, pretty sure he's made an abundance of money off of it), went to ToysRUs (still a thing in Japan btw), got a haircut (Martinson長老 only), got groceries, and wrote this email. 😀 Spiritual MessageSuper short, one sentence today…When you find your vision clouded by doubt and fear, look upon your Redeemer for a warmth and comfort that you can't find elsewhere.Love you guys so much!!!!!!!!!❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️ELDER KILLIANキリアン長老Kaden.killian@missionary.org

subject: Hi honey it's Elder Killian 🧡
body:
The title doesn't have any relevance, just popped into my mind 😂

Tuesday Today we had in person DCM (District Council Meeting) and talked a lot about how to teach our friends. We role-played one of our own friends and got taught by another missionary, then switched. It was interesting trying to make an invitation to someone I don't really know about. Definitely good practice though!

Wednesday and Thursday We didn't have anything super stand out from these two days. We did some housing as usual, and didn't have any amazing responses. One angry old man shouted at us: “what are you doing? Go home already” in the least polite Japanese you can use lol. I thought it was funny but Martinson長老 has wanted to go back and beat him up for the past few days 😂😅

Friday Today we went to a community center to look at local events and plan out the day, and some crazy stuff started happening. First of all, a guy just walked up to us inside and started speaking in pretty decent English. He was super chill, loved talking with us, and kept walking away and coming back. He was definitely a little spacey, but a really sweet guy. Later we found out he also met with the sister missionaries randomly too! Hopefully we can get a chance to meet and teach him a lesson! Also while we were sitting down, we heard a couple high schoolers muttering about us and giving us the side eye from in front of an elevator. One of them started waving over at us and smiling as the elevator door was closing, and then her friend pressed the “hold door” button and totally humiliated her 😂😂😂 She was yelling at her friend as the door started closing again lol she was so ticked. I was laughing about it for the rest of the day 😂 Unrelated, but later when we were doing some housing we found a new person with interest! His name is Etou, and he was super interested in 2 foreigners from a church who aren't just “telling him outright to join a religion.” We are going to meet with him next week to share more about our church! Keep him in your prayers 🙏 he sent us some super long text messages so we think he's got some good potential!

Saturday Today we had our game night and our Eikaiwa. Unfortunately only a couple friends showed up to the game night but our Eikaiwa was massive, maybe around 20 people. We split into two groups, and further split in order to achieve a good amount of talking. It was super fun, but also pretty exhausting haha.

Sunday Almost all of our friends made it to church today! Unfortunately the second hour was way too in depth; poor Tatsuya and Tomoya could not keep up (of course me neither lol). Next time we are definitely just gonna have a lesson with them separately. We spent a lot of time talking with President Simmons (our branch president) (president of the MARUGAME branch) (M-A-R-U-G-A-M-E) and he shared some of his missionary approaches when he was in Japan. We learned a lot, and hopefully are going to go and try out some new ideas! Later we worked on a new Eikaiwa flyer, and figured out a bit of our Pday schedule. Pretty solid Sunday!

Monday (Pday) Today was our last day to hang out with Tomoyaさん、we went to the top of a mountain up a gondola lift type thing, was pretty dope. There were 1000 buddhist statues, and we got some wild pictures. I'll try and attach a few. Afterwards we went to the beach, unfortunately it was cut short by needing to get back to the church and have a lesson with Yocchan. The Matamuras helped us this time around, it was good for gauging our understanding. I think we might need to nail in a few important principles before we see a lot more progress. Going well though! Tomorrow is Zone conference, hopefully I'll have a lot to write about!

Spiritual Message Today we read these two scriptures with Yocchan, he really thought they stood out.

Moroni 7:21-22 21 And now I come to that faith, of which I said I would speak; and I will tell you the way whereby ye may lay hold on every good thing. 22 For behold, God knowing all things, being from everlasting to everlasting, behold, he sent angels to minister unto the children of men, to make manifest concerning the coming of Christ; and in Christ there should come every good thing.

There is a way to find every good thing, and that is by turning unto Christ. We can find good things in all sorts of ways but as we turn unto Christ for guidance, its going to be a lot easier, and the good will be so much greater! 😊

I love you guys so much! Whether you're family, friends or anywhere in between I appreciate and love you all!

Elder Killian キリアン長老 kaden.killian@missionary.org

subject: Re: Study Time - D&C 14:1-7
body:
I want to let you know I'm getting on this, I still need to properly get my stuff out and get studying along with these last 2 emails, but I will share my thoughts with you when I do. Your emails have helped me to start my day off more spiritually, I am very grateful for that. I am still trying to take it more seriously, but hopefully I can get caught up in the next day or so. I love you and your efforts! I can tell that you love this gospel and are feasting upon the word. The way you think may drive others insane /s but I believe That deeply pondering and studying the scriptures and words of the prophets is a Christlike attribute. I hope that I can emulate that same enthusiasm when I reach your steep age! 😉 I don't know if I will ever be truly "ready" for a family and the joys and challenges that come with it, but I can try my best to be even a little prepared for that day. I am grateful for the time you sacrifice every day to support our family and my mission! I will try to make the most of it not just for that sake, but for all of those whose lives can be changed by the Gospel of light and life!

I love you Dad! Keep up the good work chicken UAP telepathy master 🐔

Elder Killian (crazy that you were called that a long time ago too!)
