subject: 🚨(not) Emergency Email🚨Please Read
body:
Not an emergency, but sorry for the wait. It's been a very busy last couple of P-days, and it's been difficult to find time to sit down and write. Let's dive in! (First part is the poetry email I forgot to send/finish from last week, after that is the next couple weeks)

*note* this is a very long email so skip to the last few paragraphs if you’re looking for an uplifting thought, the rest is random encounters and some mid poetry 🙏👍👍

🌟EMAIL START🌟

It's poetry Email round 2 let's go! Some stanzas with the Missionary you know 😎 I can rhyme with ease, the bars come to mind, You'd be hard pressed to find another-of-the-kind.

As I write my email I sit in a chair As my companion (over Zoom) his thoughts will share. I'm talking like Yoda, I know, I'm sorry. I'll try to write so you don't have to worry 🙃

Let's cut to the chase, this week was sure busy; The amount of things we did made me dizzy. Though, of course, this is the work I enjoy! Sharing the Gospel makes me a happy boy 😂

This week we had Zone conference as well! Role playing with members and friends rang a bell! Ever since then I've thought more deeply About how I can ramp up my service more steeply (?)

After our conference we had a great chance To go on exchanges and make an advance. This time around I was with Elder Strong; It's an understatement to say that we both get along 😎

We tried to knock on apartment doors But when housing in Japan, when it rains, it POURS. A response from each apartment complex sent us away “preaching at this building is 100% Da-Me” (unallowed) Unfazed, however, we left with heads high. We weren't even sad when they didn't say “goodbye” 😆

Now I'll provide a rapid-fire summary of things that we've done Because there's a lot lot to write in this format and after writing and writing for so long it's getting to be a little bit less fun 😂😵

Jimoty meeting, purchasing tie clips Visiting garage sale, making many trips. Inviting a lady to our Eikaiwa went pretty well! We still haven't seen her there but I can tell… (That she has interest in attending 😆)

On exchanges with Elder Strong We were stared at by a Starbucks employee She thought that we'd get along Or at least that's how it seemed to me 😂

A random fun fact, I'm trying to cook! Turns out tasty food can be quite the hook.

Met with another new friend named Kengo-san! He's cool and funny and also likes Pizzamon! (Probably 👼)

One night we had to get home in the dark, The bike ride was an hour! Unfortunately, though, my light wouldn't spark, and the situation turned sour.

But alas! I had a stroke of luck! I remembered a flashlight I had in my backpack. I used a plastic bag to ensure it was stuck Right on my handlebar! (I admit it was wack)

My poetry energy has come to hault. Forgive me. Yep.

We had a few cool things happen in week 2 of this email!

We had a missionwide devotional which was awesome! I learned a lot that I would like to try and apply in my daily work. One thing that stood out to me was to make sure I'm entirely exhausted and used up by the end of each day. It's a high bar, but it shows devotion and effort! God loves effort!

For district Pday we went to an Island by ferry (brought our bikes too), rode around the island and looked around for sea glass/starfish on a beach. It was pretty neat, but if we didn't try to go and catch the sunset on the other side of the Island, we would have been stuck there forever. basically we rode to the other side of the island to look around, and to try and see the sunset we had to go back to the place where we rode the ferry, and as we were passing by a lady told us the past ship home was coming in 5 minutes, so we just went back rather than view the sunset from the beach 🙈💀. It was a close one lol 😂

FUNNY THINGS: (Shoutout to Sister Crockett's emails)

From a few branch activities there is usually leftover food or drinks that the missionaries are welcome to take. I have been trying to make sure I'm staying nice and hydrated, so I've been taking some of these big bottled containers of spring water and chugging through them in a couple days. Not long ago, I was in the kitchen and a member was curious if I'd seen the special "sacrament water” in the fridge lately. 😟 It turns out in my great thirst that I drank and threw away a fancy bottle of specially filtered water that they have been using in the branch for like 10 years 😂 Nobody was upset or anything, cause the bottle wasn't even labeled or anything, but I unintentionally ruined a fancy tradition that's been going on for a decade 😂😂😂 Hey the water tasted great btw

We went to a lady's house to do service and she needed a piece of wood to be smaller to fit in a trash bag, so I put it against a curb and pretty much exploded it in half and she freaked out for a second and got all mad at me saying I almost killed someone, it was a loud explosion so it's understandable, things were fine in the end. Once I showed her I was safely exploding the wood she let me finish the job lol.

I made a cardboard dragon hand-puppet! We are going to try and use it to find people on the streets. Gotta use those random talents to help bring people to Christ 😤😎

I got an electric guitar 🎸 I an going to try and find an Amp. It was only 30 bucks or I wouldn't have caved I swear. My fingers are sore but I have a good feeling about “Electric guitar finding.” I bet we can find new friends with this new idea!

We were having food at a member’s house with the sister missionaries, and one of their friends was sitting behind Martinson長老 and Aquino姉妹 (one of the sister missionaries) and Started giving her a shoulder massage, then switched to Martinson長老 😂 It was so funny, he didn't have any bad intentions but she definitely froze up there for a second lol.

The same dude who gave the unsolicited massage (his name is Okubo-san) keeps on saying he wants to be like Martinson Harris (one of the Church's early leaders and kickstarters). We ask him why and he says he wishes he had lots of money haha 🤑 He has a baptismal date so his heart is in the right place no matter how it sounds lol.

OK, not sure how this next segment is going to go, but I am in the mood to write a short story. I'm just gonna go for it, maybe it will make up for the past 2 weeks with no emails 🙈 Or maybe it will make you wish I hadn't sent it for another week we will see lol.

📕Story Time!📕

As I sat at my desk writing a letter to Geoffrey, a dark flash of movement from outside of my apartment window caught my eye, followed by the sound of a horn and presumably a car slamming into something. Alarmed by the noise and the strange movement, I arose and made my way to the curtains, peering through the blinds for some context as to what I had seen and heard. In the road 5 floors beneath my window lay a muddied white personage. Dozens of small feather-like shapes appeared to be scattered around the figure, some floating in a large puddle a few feet from the strange “person.”

As I scanned for signs of wreckage from the car accident, uncannily I could see no damaged vehicles or surrounding scenery. There were a few parked cars nearby, but no signs of a crash. After gazing thoughtfully at the unrecognizable form below, I decided to continue writing my letter. I walked back to my desk, sat down, and picked up where I had left off, thinking of how to write about this strange occurrence as well. I spent around 3 minutes writing before I noticed the unusual stillness of the apartment. Usually I could hear traffic or muffled conversation from the neighbors upstairs, but it was oddly quiet.

A sort of heaviness fell upon me, and I again marched to the window to inspect the street below. The same sprawled white form lay on the side of the road, still unrecognizable due to the person’s positioning. The form began to shift, and lifting their chest from the asphalt, what turned out to be a woman with short white hair turned her attention upwards towards my window. As her lips turned to a smile, the windows in front of me exploded outwards due to some power unbeknownst to me, and my room was filled instantly with chilly city air.

I exclaimed in my surprise, and stumbled backwards, terrified by what had happened. Through the shattered glass I saw as the smiling face of the woman from below appeared. As our eyes met, the weight I had felt earlier increased six-fold, I find myself in order to regain my stance. On whom upon further inspection was some sort of feathered and fair-skinned humanoid I addressed with a shaky “W-who are you?”, I was met with a frown and the words “I see…”

📕Story Over📕

Pday of this week (week of sending email for real) Today I bought a guitar Amp that you can put batteries in and use with headphones (not actually an Amp built into a speaker). It is pretty cool to hear my electric guitar being all electricky but I have yet for other people to hear it as well lol. I can at minimum get some guitar practice in with it sounding cool at least. Besides guitaring around we went to KFC, hung out at the church, and something super strange happened…

We were sitting around when we heard the church doorbell. It's the first time I've actually heard it lol. Martinson長老 and I were the only ones at the church, so we jumped up to go and get the door. There was a girl standing outside, probably in her late teens early twenties, and we asked if we could help her. She said she had heard about the church from an acquaintance, and wanted to come and hear some talks to learn about the church 💥💥💥🤯🤯 The fact that we were there at the church to help her out was a huge miracle. We exchanged contact information with her, and we are really hoping that we can meet and teach her or see her on Sunday for Sacrament Meeting. She also biked from like the city over, which is like 30-40 minute bike ride with Japanese short legs. We still don't know what she needs or what drew her to the church but we are excited to find out! Will keep you guys updated on her! Her name is Aoi.

I believe that as missionaries (or anyone for that matter) we are NOT working alone. God is always preparing people to receive the gospel in small ways that we may not even begin to comprehend. God gives us blessings and interactions that can help us to find light in our lives. God also provides us with unique challenges to stretch us and help us to grow in many ways. I believe that God loves us each deeply, and that we can feel his love through studying the words of the prophets, learning of the Savior, prayer, or even as simply as interacting with your family and friends.

3 Nephi 15:12

12 Ye are my disciples; and ye are a light unto this people, who are a remnant of the house of Joseph.

In this instance, Jesus is speaking unto his 12 Apostles. However, I know that as we act as disciples this message applies to us as well. We are a light unto God's people. Please, choose to turn outwards, and set aside the many distractions of this day and age, even for a few minutes. Reach out to someone you can see that needs your support, advice, love or service. If you can't think of anyone, go out and find someone! I believe that we all have the ability and potential to be a great light unto those around us. I would encourage each of you to try in some way this week to be a light to someone else. You would be surprised just how much turning outwards can fill your own bucket 🪣a.k.a. lift your own spirits/burdens.

I love you guys! I am not a perfect person, not even a perfect missionary. I have things I am working to heal and improve. I am devoted to this work and I am working to increase that devotion. I hope that each of you can sense that you have a place in my heart, and more importantly in Christ's! Keep your chin up! Be a light!

Elder Killian キリアン長老 🩶🩶🩶🩶

Companion low resolution/energy 😎 (He took the pic not me)

subject: FW: What do you do when you think everything's going to change but not a lot does?
body: -------- Original message --------From: Kaden Killian Date: 3/18/25 8:02 AM (GMT-07:00) To: killianfamily Subject: What do you do when you think everything's going to change but not a lot does? It might seem natural to feel hopeless, or discouraged.That's not what God wants us to feel, though! He wants us to be hopeful, and encouraged!The start of this week wasn't what I was hoping it would look like. There are a lot of things that have been better than last week, so there's no reason to surrender myself to the negative thoughts that would beguile me. It has been frustrating not seeing a lot of change, however. I trust that as I give my energy to do his will, I can be encouraged and hopeful in any circumstance. I am going to try and guide my actions to be in sync with what God would have me do, with an increased drive!The week slowly passed byHere’re some Interesting things that happened:Went to Bishop's house for dinner, he shared an ancient family tradition that ended with me having oil smeared all over my face 🤦‍♂️Stopped by an art store! I talked with a sweet lady inside, and when she saw my church name tag she grabbed onto my arm and started telling me about how she had visited America before! She was so sweet a d we need to go back and visit her soon. She had a certain light about her. She also gave me a discount when I bought a tube of paint 😉We were able to do an hour and a half of Housing at an apartment complex! Doesn't sound like a huge feat but last week we did actually 0 hours of finding so it was a big step! We didn't find anyone interested but there were a few solid interactions 😆Picked up a selfie stick tripod thing for 300 yen from a Jimoty person. Will invite them to our Eikaiwa through the website next time we are at the church!We had DCM this week too. It was Magbago長老’s first time leading one! I think he did a great job. We had a fun Mogi and talked about the difference between listening and hearing. I'm excited for our next DCM! He is wanting to use me for a lot of it which sounds fun too. After DCM we went and ate Ramen as a district. Was fun!On Sunday we talked a lot after church with a member named Otani兄弟 about ice cream and art museum stuff. It was fun, and a nice way to spend part of our Sunday.We had a chance to eat at an Udon shop with one of our uninterested in gospel friends. I don't think anyone has high hopes for him as far as gospel stuff, but I think he's got great potential, and he wants to go eat with us again!It's time for that one random thing that's always in my emails: this time I'm feeling Like a few haiku should hit the spot 😌#1“Life is Meaningless,”Satan wouldst have you believe.Ponder not those words.#2“Love one another”Commanded our Redeemer.“Turn yourself outwards”#3Clouds are mighty thinYou'd be surprised at just howClose we are to God#4It's late, I will sleep.I love all of you so much!Elder Killian

subject: I bought some Rosary Beads 📿
body:
Here are some brief thoughts on this week.

This week had its highs and fair share of lows. This week was also not a good week for finding. We had a few big events that took up what could have been finding time, although I am not confident it would have happened anyways. We have a couple new goals set that will hopefully help us to get out of the apartment earlier, and start planning for the rest of the day. I can’t say I was a bright beacon of sunshine this last week. I didn’t expect things to feel so lonely even having a companion with me all the time. I think that I underestimated how much my interactions with other people affect my day/week positively. Maybe I can express my concerns and we can work together better as a companionship for that. I am kind of sick of being in a seemingly endless cycle, but with that in mind, I know I can put forth a heightened effort to get out of the cycle. I will strengthen my prayers, my studies, and strive to act and teach more as the Savior! I recognize the way things are written it seems like it was a pretty dark week. There were a few really great things that happened though, so don’t linger on it 🫣😅

On Tuesday after meeting a Jimoty person to get a tripod selfie stick (super handy) we had a chance to try and visit a few less active members. One of them was not living in the place we thought they were, but we were able to say hi to the wife of one other member. She said he was at work, and we were even able to leave a note for him.

This week we had Zone conference. It was fun to go and say hi to Sonomura長老、Mackay長老 and Dietrich長老。I got to participate in a Mogi (roleplay) with a Japanese member, and it was fun to hear her thoughts on things. Not gonna lie I didn't really know what we were supposed to be going for, but I just kinda winged it. Wasn't a bad experience 👍 Today I received a package from Nao and Mai back in Ibaraki. It was so sweet of them to send me a gift, but the best part was definitely the handwritten notes in English by Nao, and Japanese by Mai. Inside of the package were some chocolates with a variety of fruity flavors 👅🍌🍓🍍🫐 and I thought I would share them around with some missionaries. Well, one of the other missionaries at zone conference saw the chocolates, and shared how he and some friends ate some before only to realize that they had green tea in them. Lol. That would be a problem BUT I read the back, couldn’t find any green tea in the ingredients, and I showed it to Sano会長 and he said he ate some the other day and they were fine XD. So things were fine in the end. One more fun thing was at Zone conference we had a challenge to read the Book of Mormon all the way through TWICE in TWO TRANSFERS 😶 I’m not off to a great start, but I know I can make it work if I really put my mind to it.

Big highlight this week was getting to meet with a new Japanese Learning Referral. His name is Romeo, and he's from Nepal. he's super cool, and a really spiritually inclined dude. Without us bringing anything up, one of the first things he asked us was who God was to us. We talked together for a little while about how we've seen God in our lives, and afterwards got a chance to practice some Japanese together as well. He's actually really good at speaking Japanese already, so we are gonna have to make it a more challenging class than it usually is. We introduced him to The Book of Mormon (could not have been any less smooth but that's ok) and we've Got high hopes. When he was first calling us to schedule meeting up, he kept on asking how to join our church 😂 So he's got a willing heart already. Super excited to meet with him again this next week.

We stopped by a Filipino appreciation festival by the beach for like half an hour on Saturday. We only caught the end of it, but we talked with a man in charge of a charity organization and a girl from the Philippines who has been living in Japan for about 3 years. I wanted to get something to show support for the charity, so I bought some rosary beads 📿 made in the Philipines. Pretty dope. I removed the Cross from the brads when I got back to the apartment so now I can wear them without getting any dirty looks from members or JW’s. We did get housed by Jehovah’s Witnesses a couple weeks ago, don't know if I mentioned that lol. We didn't get a chance to Bible bash (don't do that) but they talked to us for a couple minutes. Interesting to think we're not the only ones going out and talking to people about Religion in Japan. All the more motivation to get out and do finding, so that the only faces people around here recognize aren't just the JWs 🤷‍♂️ Not that it's a competition, but there's plenty of forces working against us, so we should try and make sure we're active.

Church this week was awesome. I got a chance to say the closing prayer, and we participated in the youth class after Sacrament meeting. We learned about what we should be prioritizing, it was a pretty useful lesson.

Still haven't gotten a reimbursement form from 2 of my past comps so I sent this image^ to them 😁

Fun note, Magbago長老 is biking better the more we do it. He is much less close to running into people or buildings lately which is great 👍

This week to try and do something to bond with him I decided to get a few packs of Magic cards so we could play together. Probably his favorite game, even if it's not it's definitely the one he talks about the most. I accidentally asked the clerk at the store for 2 of each of the 3 types of packs I chose instead of 1 🤪 He spent a solid minute searching around the shelves for all of them so I didn't want to tell him to put back half of them. It wasn't crazy expensive but I was hoping to be a bit more thrifty this week. Hopefully my companion will be happy that I have more cards for him to play against though.

That about exhausts my writing for today. I will try writing a poem now.

How can you expect to take a fresh breath When you spend your time sitting by garbage? If you’re never mindful of what's around you You likely won't increase your yardage.

I kind of wrote this because there are some trash bags sitting by our front door which actually stink, but it's important to be mindful of what we put into our lives, and what kinds of things we spend time with and around. I hope that there is a lesson to be found in the random poetry that occasionally spews from my brain 🧠

I love you guys so much! I hope that next week I will be able to write a 150% positive email. It's possible but really difficult and exhausting, so wish me luck 😂😆 I haven't replied to many personal emails in a while, I'm sorry. I'll try to work on that this next week. Hang in there!

Elder Killian kaden.killian@missionary.org 1000011310.jpg 1000010992.jpg
summary: Week had highs and lows; found few, felt lonely with companion. Set new goals, plans for change. Recognized need for prayer and study. Visited less active members, participated in Mog

subject: I saw a big bumblebee
body:
Reply to Elder Killian: kaden.killian@missionary.org

This week was a week to remember, hopefully it can be a sort of turning point for me and my companion. Let's talk about it!

After such a hopeful start to my email, the first few days this week were inversely quite a slump. Tuesday and Wednesday I don't think we even left the apartment, save leaving to teach Eikaiwa. The more I just let things brew inside of me and the more I told myself “you shouldn't have to be the one to say anything,” the less actually got done. I tried to convince myself that getting up and ready for the day (relatively on time) was enough, and that Magbago長老 could play his part. It doesn't seem like a terrible thing at first glance but I didn’t have the intention of building up my companion, or even encouraging him a little bit, which was probably what he needed to get jump started. It's sometimes really hard to be that voice for someone else, it really irks me sometimes but I am trying to learn to just suck it up and try to do what the Savior would in that scenario, even if it feels like the opposite of what I want to do.

Eikaiwa was pretty fun as it usually is, but I can't really remember anything that stuck out from it. Eikaiwa is in general one of my favorite things to teach 😆 A couple times teaching it with my present companion, though, there have been a few English tips or principles that aren’t written in our Teaching books that Magbago長老 will teach… It’s really awkward when something is just straight up wrong or does not exist in English and I have to like stop him in the middle of it and try and make sure everyone knows the right way to say it. Its really tough when he is so confident that he’s right with it too 🙈😂 To be fair there are plenty of things I don't know about English, Japanese and don't get me started on Cebuano 😆 so it all balances out

Thursday we also didn't really do anything until the afternoon; we met up with Shikoさん from Eikaiwa to practice Electric guitar together, he's insane. On the walk up to the church there was a kid laying facedown on the road, we thought he was meeting his maker or something, but as we started getting closer he rolled over and sat up. We told him we thought he was dead as we walked by and he might have smiled or something lol. OK back to Guitar practice. I'm pretty much sitting in front of a rock star. A bit more about his background; he's played the guitar since he was 14 (Pretty sure he's in his late 50's-60's) and he's never even like been in a band or anything. He is still single, he said when he was younger he was never Really interested in dating and all that romance 😂 It's kind of sad because I feel like all of his talent and stuff is just going to fade away with him, especially if he doesnt stop smoking 🚭 He's already had cancer twice, maybe we can be encouraging to him for his own health’s sake. I gotta carry on his legacy 😢 Anyways, he taught me some basic warm-up exercises and told me to go through them every day. To play the C Major scale, there are a handful of patterns that you can use, but we just went over the simplest one. He showed me the others, including the ichiban muzukashii (most difficult). The most difficult pattern was actually pretty easy for me as it involves going down the length of one string playing notes in sequence, which is like the only way I actually can play guitar at the moment 😂 I played through it and he was surprised/weirded out that I could do it so easily, he keeps on asking how long I've played and is always surprised when I tell him just around a month. 😂 The other C Major scales are actually hard for me though, so I've been practicing the first one a little bit each day. After meeting with Shikoさん、Suehiro姉妹 came to the church and I gave her my audition for what I would play on Easter Sunday. She was actually ok with the guitar, I just had to make sure it sounded as acoustic as possible 😂 She gave me a few suggestions for more commonly known songs, and we settled on “I Know that My Redeemer Lives”. She also sat down and Played the piano along with me, and we decided it would be best if we played together on Sunday 🔥🔥

Today we had our exchanges with the Takamatsu Elders, the good ol’ ZL’s. It was legendary. Let’s hop in. The ZLs showed up pretty early so things got started right from the morning. Me and Lighthall長老 were together, so we headed up to the church to get our day planned out. I had a media team meeting and also practiced guitar a bit for my performance, and we took a train with the other elders to RinkuTown to do our finding. We basically did Housing and some streeting for around 5 hours, it was super productive. We weren’t able to give away any Books of Mormon, but Lighthall長老 had a bunch of super meaningful interactions, including one new GREEN DOT. We were walking around trying to visit member dots on our map, and while walking away from an apartment where nobody answered the door, we saw a lady in a nearby park playing some sort of Croquet and decided to walk up and ask how she was doing. Turns out she was playing “Ground Golf” And it has a sort of Croquet mallet that looks like a huge putter. We came up to her and she immediately started speaking to us in English. Her name is Setusko. She asked if we were missionaries, and she told us that she had seen missionaries before, and when we asked her what she talked about with them she said “nothing.” lol. We talked more with her and it turns out she used to live in California like 40 years ago. (She is in her 70’s, always hard to believe that Japanese people are so energetic when they get that old) Her English is seriously so natural, it's crazy. She actually reads the Bible often, and asked us to explain it from Genesis to Revelations lol. Lighthall長老 gave a brief sort of overview of things, and we even introduced the Book of Mormon. She has a bunch of different questions about things, but her knowledge of Gospel principles and vocabulary is insane. It's crazy to believe that she’s just been learning all this on her own, I can’t wait to learn more about her background. She wouldn’t take the Book of Mormon or exchange contact info right then (she didn't have her phone) but she says she goes to that park almost every day of the week, and we told her we would come at 3:00 next Tuesday to talk with her and study the Bible together. Huge miracle that we ran into her, especially as she was just starting to clean up her Ground Golf equipment. After meeting with Setsuko, we stopped a few different people and asked for a bit of background as to their views on Religion, and if they think it has anything to do with how they feel happiness in life. We ran into: An old lady walking her dog, a cool dude with sunglasses, a lady who read a verse from the Book of Mormon with us, and a cool lady from Sri Lanka (who might be interested in our Eikaiwa.) On our way back home after all of this craziness, some dude frantically ran up to us asking how to get to Wakayama, and I think that Lighthall長老 directed him to a train going the opposite direction and requiring a special ticket. He sprinted over to it before its door closed and he was off. Pray that he does not see my face one day and be super mad that his evening was potentially completely ruined 💀🫣

Last crazy thing from our Exchanges was that Mackay長老 and Magbago長老 put our friend Romeo on baptismal date! He was still not feeling great because his knees hurt, so the two of them went and visited his apartment to meet with him. I obviously don’t know all the details because I wasn’t there, but it sounded great… Unfortunately a few days later we got a text from him saying that he still wants to experience change in his life but he doesn’t feel the need to commit to any religion, and that he can still personally have a relationship with God and through Meditation. This is a big bummer but it's also great progress that he agreed to a baptismal date at first! He still wants to meet and talk about God, so I'll keep y'all updated. After meeting with Romeo Magbago長老 and Mackay長老 also spent like an hour trying to find Magbago長老’s lost wallet (it was found lets go) Tonight that sweet member brought us food again (this time grilled pineapple) 🍍

On the morning after our exchange, we got up and headed up to the church to have our zoom Eikwaiwa with Aime (spoiler alert, she wasn’t able to do it), and also to see off the other two Elders back to Wakayama. On the way some dude passed by us on a moped and shouted to us “Donald Trump lives forever” which was surprising to say the least, especially in Japan. Afterwards, I got some time to practice my hymn with Sister Suehiro, and it was pretty great. We had to try and balance the volume with the “nice sound” of the guitar and what we ended up doing was putting a wireless microphone in front of one of my amps. It worked perfectly! After that, Brother Suehiro came to the church after his Saturday morning golf and brought us some Mcdonalds. It was nice to sit down and eat and talk with them. Later we went to the beach to do some planning, ended up just walking along the beach and skipping some rocks and shells and decided to find somewhere more in the shade to do our weekly planning. We found a nice little rugged park bench in a semi-shady spot and talked for a while and made a new action plan for our missionary work. I am much more confident in what we can do next week, I am excited to see how things go! After our planning we had to get back to the church for a mogi lesson with Brother Kazuto, and it was like the best Mogi lesson I’ve ever been in. We did an ok lesson at first but he gave some super meaningful feedback, I even whipped out my notes and took some while he was talking lol. It was lit. Last meaningful thing from the day was helping a member set up chairs while Magbago長老 had to use the bathroom. One dude in the ward was pulling some weeds out front and started setting up chairs so I helped him get them all set up.

Easter Sunday! At church I got to perform with Sister Suehiro and it was great! We played the first verse together and everyone joined us to sing the next two verses. We went to the church pretty early to meet and practice with her, and it was all worth it in the end :D We got a lot of nice compliments from the members and we also got another mogi lesson set up for this next week. After church I got to have some good Book of Mormon catch up time while my companion did follow ups with the rest of the district. We had a Sunday zone devotional as usual, and that was also great. I cut Magbago長老’s hair too, I think I did a pretty good job but you will have to be the judge lol.

Monday Poem:

I went to the mall And I bought a few cards I went to the barber And they cut a few yards I went to the Daiso And I got a soft cushion I went to the music shop And I bet you can't think of anything that rhymes with cushion either 😮‍💨 (Pushin’, Smushin’, Swooshin’)

I don't have time for anything deep today, but with Easter being this last Sunday I'd continue to invite y'all to ponder The meaning of Christ's Atonement and resurrection to you. How is your life changed through the Savior? General Conference will also be a great way to ponder Him, so I'd invite you to read or reread or watch General Conference to help!

I love you guys! Your prayers are likely the only thing keeping me going some days. A mission is tough for real. Peace out ✌️

Elder Killian

(I attached the sneaky audio I took of my guitar performance, it's pretty basic but I hope you enjoy it 😁)
summary: Kaden saw a big bumblebee, but the rest of the message is unclear due to poor audio quality.

subject: Performance
summary: This message appears to be a duplicate of the first one, with the same unclear audio quality.

subject: I'm a Rockstar now 🎸
body:
Reply to: kaden.killian@missionary.org

Tuesday Today we were able to meet up again with Setsuko! We showed up at the park to talk and play Ground Golf and she showed up with a fresh haircut. She cuts her hair with scissors herself using two mirrors to see the back, she's awesome. She said we were both cute with our haircuts too. Before we played she recalled some of her memories she had of her two younger and very naughty brothers. When they were kids, their mom got a phone call from the school that the sons went to, and apparently one of them had broken all the windows of the school. 😈💀 One of them also sprayed a fire extinguisher off of the top floor. That's a whole new level of rambunctious. The funniest story she told us (in her opinion) was when one of her brothers was 4 years old, he jumped straight into a pond trying to catch a butterfly with his net. Setsuko acted this out with a big jump which definitely surprised me considering she's 75 (I think). She also has arthritis so I don't really know how she's moving around so well, she's super energetic and I'm all here for it. We also got to exchange Line with her, so now we have a solid contact method!! We didn't get to talk much about gospel stuff but it was a good chance to get to know her and her background better. Afterwards we did some housing and I used the flower method 🌸🌻🌹🏵🌷🥀🌼🌺💠💐 Basically we ring the doorbell at the house of someone with a lot of pretty flowers or something and I'll ask if it's OK for me to take a picture of them. Today we tried it and got to talk with some lady in her front yard for a solid 5-10 minutes! She showed us all her different flowers and their names, and we even talked a bit about our missions. We invited her to Eikaiwa and it seems like she had a little bit of interest, she's practiced English by listening to the radio before! Hopefully we get to meet her again!

We visited art store lady again! I need to get her name memorized, I heard it once but totally forgot. She was pretty busy helping a guy get some art stuff for a news broadcast (crazy) but she saw we had an interest in calligraphy and tried to help us get set up with some free classes with some random teacher. I also picked out some watercolor paper to try, and when she saw me trying to look for more coins to pay for it, she grabbed my hand and took only the money I had (it was less than the paper) and sent me out of the store with my paper 😭😊 shes so sweet but I literally want to like support the store. We will definitely visit again! We had Eikaiwa today, and before it started 2 random people (I think past students from like years ago) showed up and wanted to say hi to us for a little while. We talked a bit about church stuff and a bit about Eikaiwa but it was super random. The two were maybe a couple (not sure) but they were for certain previously dance partners 💃🕺They were in their 70’s and weren't able to stay for long but it was nice to meet them. We played ping pong before Eikaiwa with one lady and she and I went back and forth for like 100 pings and pongs without missing a hit. It was like the spirit of ping pong was so strong 👻 Eikaiwa was fun, afterwards Shiko showed us his acoustic-electric guitar. It was super cool, and it blows my mind just how light they are compared to electric guitars lol. Today Setsuko also sent us a bunch of voice memos which are so cute and always in English. She told one of her friends about me and Magbago長老 and she wants to meet with us! From what Setsuko said she doesn't have much of a gospel interest maybe, but Setsuko would like to talk about our beliefs all together with her. This will happen in 2 weeks, let's go!

Today we had exchanges with the Gobo elders, and I can say with surety that Gobo is the most Inaka (countryside) part of the mission. There's hardly anyone there. I was partnered up with Downs長老 and like 10 Percent of the doors we knocked even answered. One guy answered in a towel and had just gotten out of the bath. There was no reason he had to answer the door like that but it was awesome, props to him. Me and Matsuhisa長老 also played some Pokémon that night, and the game was like 2 hours long 😱 I ended up losing but I also chose not to just kill him in the first couple rounds so I feel validated. He also gave me much praise for my Japanese speaking, as a native he thinks I'm like cracked at Japanese. It's a very sweet sentiment, especially from another missionary who has likely seen all kinds of other missionaries and their own levels of Japanese. God has really blessed me with an increased ability to speak and listen in Japanese as I have tried to align my will to His.

Today we had 3 Jimoty pickups, and because my comp didn't have money ATM we biked to meet up with the 3 people. One of them was a bunch of assorted tools for 200¥, one of them was a couple guitar pickups for 1000¥, and another was a pair of 4kilogram exercise weights. Can you guess which one we picked up first?? The weights 💀 Can you guess How far we biked today? 30 Miles 💀💀💀 The whole time I carried around those weights and it was wild. Not sure I've biked a longer distance in one day In my whole life. Also having the weights made it significantly tougher. We crossed 4 cities in between meeting with all of those people. As usual we invited them all to our Eikaiwa classes and I left them positive reviews on Jimoty. It was crazy for real. I also got real crisped up from the sun, I will attach a picture. While biking home we tried to visit a house I’d been attempting to knock at least twice before. This time they answered the doorbell but immediately said no thanks even while I was saying how I loved their disney themed fence. Tragic. We also tried to say hi to a member by the church and she answered but we didn't exactly bring anything for her and she didn't have time for a longer visit so we gave a bit of an awkward greeting and left lol.

Sunday Surprise At church we had a recently baptized member named Daniel show up to Sacrament meeting. He's been living in Japan for around 8 months and he's from Paraguay. We are hopefully gonna hang out with him on Pday! He lives pretty far and travels by bike so he hasn't gotten to attend church before now since coming to Japan. What’s crazy is that he was taught and invited to baptism by a Sister Killian 😮 Here's something crazy. A day before we met Daniel, a sister missionary named Dossett姉妹 ♥️ sent me a Facebook profile for an Elder Kade Killian who was called to serve in Japan 3 ish years ago. We met Daniel and it was Elder Kade Killian's sister who served in Paraguay and taught Daniel about the gospel. Insane divine inspiration from Dossett姉妹. Today me and Magbago played one of the card games he likes called Union Arena and it was pretty fun.

Random missed note from last week, during exchanges met a high-school girl on her porch and talked a bunch about religion and what eternal life sounded like to her, and she even said that she imagines it would be living forever married 👀

Pday was miserably rainy, we weren't able to hang out with Daniel because of it. We did bike a little ways to get groceries though. I bought a foot pedal thingy for my guitar and another CD. Not at the grocery store if that wasn't apparent Lol. We have plans to meet with Jiro tonight, I'll have to talk about how it goes next week 😁 (it went well, I'm writing this late and after we met with him. We Invited him to meet up with the missionaries in Australia and he seems like he's excited to!)

Spiritual thought This week has been a week of huge progress as a companionship. Getting out and doing Missionary work feels so good. Things aren't perfect, and there's definitely times where we aren't using our time 100 percent perfectly but it's way better than it has been. I can promise to each of you reading this that no matter what difficulties you are facing, God knows your trials and struggles. I have really tried to see things from his perspective recently, Maybe holding my tongue against my companion or waking him up if I don't want to 😂 (not happening all that often fortunately) or following a prompting from the spirit. Things really seem much better as you are striving to Follow God's plan. It's different in each of our lives, so I'd invite you to pray and sincerely ask God what His plan looks like for you. Maybe even ask what you can do. I'm sure you'll be surprised by the response you get, I definitely have been.

I love each and every one of you! I'm grateful to be serving the Lord. I hope that my email can be a light to some one of my friends out there 😁

Elder Killian

subject: Teach Me to Walk in the Light
body:
Tuesday Today we had DCM. It was pretty chill, we got to talk with the district and hang out for like an hour before it started. Matsuhisa長老 and I talked about guitar, he has Played the Electric guitar for a couple years, I let him jam out a bit on mine. The DCM itself was pretty cool, Magbago長老 got two returned missionaries to do mogis with two companionship in our district, while we did a mogi with the sister missionary trio. Afterwards we went to lunch all together, and I got some Okonomiyaki, which is just so good. Its like a big savory pancake with vegetables and meat cooked into it. You prepare it yourself on a built-in stovetop in front of you, and cover It in some awesome sauce. It was great. While trying to take a picture of another companionship I accidentally got a photo of myself which was funny, I'll put it on the email. Immediately after the meal, me and Magbago長老 rushed to get our bikes headed up a hill to a member's house for a mogi lesson (and lunch which I didn't know about). We got there mostly on time, and proceeded to have a second lunch. I think I survived pretty well, but I did ask if I could take home a little bit so I wouldn't be sick. Dono family was very sweet and gave me a little takeout box. We then had The mogi, and dessert afterwards. Super good time and I didn't feel mega sick. Later we had time for studies and preparing some cards to pass out streeting/housing but we were crunched on time and didn't get to pass out anything sadly. We do have some resources for the future though. At the church we met for another guitar practice session with Shiko and this time he brought a couple cool Picks to give me as a present. One of them is just for display, but the other is to use. He recommended I use it with other guitar strings though. Note, I need to try and involve my companion better with these guitar practice sessions. I don't really know how to best make it work with the 3 of us but its kind of sad if it's just a 1 on 1 type thing with me and Shiko. Other than that it was a pretty good busy day.

Wednesday Today was Eikaiwa, and we got to talk for like 30 seconds about axolotls which in Japanese Is ウーパールーパー (oopaloopa) It's literally such a funny name it makes me so happy. Before our normal Eikaiwa we had a 1 on 1 Eikaiwa with Aime, a likely less active member technically living in our Ward boundaries. She's super busy with work, so we practiced some work scenarios, and how she could apologize to customers in English as per her request. It was great, and she even said the closing prayer for us. I hope it was meaningful for her. I was also practicing guitar today and while setting up my amps and stuff it appeared that my brand new Amp that I got on PDay (with cool buttons you can press with your feet while you are standing playing guitar) was broken. I just felt a wave of dread come over me as I realized it wouldn't turn on anymore. I immediately got on my knees and prayed about it. After I prayed, I kept messing with stuff, changing batteries, that kind of thing. Even when it seemed like nothing would work. Eventually I changed some stuff, tried pressing the button to turn it on in a different way, and it worked. I got right back down and said a prayer of gratitude. It was a big deal for me, and it meant a lot to have Heavenly Help when it seems like all I can do is break the things that I buy 😄😅 As we were waiting for Eikaiwa students to show up, Shiko came a little early and gave me one of his old chord books to practice with for the guitar. Such a sweet dude, it's hard to believe sometimes. We found out he first came to Eikaiwa in 2009 😲 so he's been coming for 16 years. He's probably seen so many missionaries come and go, I don't think he's ever been taught by them either. We also found out he's been coming all this time because it’s a way for him to escape from Japanese life, he feels like he's trapped sometimes. This is a great insert for the gospel, pray That things continue to go well with him 🔥 After Eikaiwa we headed right to the train station and spent the night with the Sakai elders, the loudest apartment ever bro I'm dead. I don't understand how its not universally known not to yell in an apartment building at 10-11 at night bro 😂💀 I had an ok sleep, crazy dreams as usual.

Thursday This morning I got up at 6AM to shower and get ready for leaving to Zone conference/mission tour (it was like kind of half and half) . I delivered two letters I'd written for Nao and for Mai to the Ibaraki elders to give to them. We heard a lot from President and Sister Esplin, as well as our mission president and his wife (Love the Sanos!) Afterwards we had some lunch and even made a little Origami. I got an Easter package from my family 😁 and I searched the Ibaraki church for my spikeball net. It was nowhere to be seen. I asked the Elders there and they said they asked the members and they had no idea about it. The only other place I think it could be is the Elders apartment, but I didn't get a chance to look there before I left 🌀🌀🌀🌀🌀 I am Gonna try and get a hold of the Elders soon and have them search the apartment or something 😮‍💨 Pretty lame. It was fun to be in Ibaraki with Ishizaka長老 and Sonomura長老 though. I Would have liked to see any of the members or my friends but nobody was there for the Zone thing, so I will have to wait until I am transferred back 😏

Friday 17 Perhaps thou shalt say: The man has brought upon himself his misery; therefore I will stay my hand, and will not give unto him of my food, nor impart unto him of my substance that he may not suffer, for his punishments are just— 18 But I say unto you, O man, whosoever doeth this the same hath great cause to repent; and except he repenteth of that which he hath done he perisheth forever, and hath no interest in the kingdom of God. 19 For behold, are we not all beggars? Do we not all depend upon the same Being, even God, for all the substance which we have, for both food and raiment, and for gold, and for silver, and for all the riches which we have of every kind? 20 And behold, even at this time, ye have been calling on his name, and begging for a remission of your sins. And has he suffered that ye have begged in vain? Nay; he has poured out his Spirit upon you, and has caused that your hearts should be filled with joy, and has caused that your mouths should be stopped that ye could not find utterance, so exceedingly great was your joy. 21 And now, if God, who has created you, on whom you are dependent for your lives and for all that ye have and are, doth grant unto you whatsoever ye ask that is right, in faith, believing that ye shall receive, O then, how ye ought to impart of the substance that ye have one to another.

This is from Mosiah Chapter 4 from the Book of Mormon, and as I read it tonight I felt that I wasn't taking things with My companion as seriously As I should. Today was a bit of backtracking as far as daily planning and getting out and doing Missionary work, and I found myself wasting time. I did attempt to make some flatbread and shared a little with Magbago長老, I kind of felt like I should make some bread and share it. (Before I read the scriptures btw) It was like the most American meal I've had here, Flatbread Breakfast sandwich for dinner. Not gonna lie it slapped. Anyways beyond the sandwich stuff I think I need to step up my game as far as supporting and interacting with my companion. Doesn't matter where he's at (doing worse or better than me), that shouldn't change my efforts to help him out. I am gonna try the rest of this week to be more humble and like the Savior.

Saturday Today we had a crazy Stake sports festival thing that was 5 hours long. It happened to be in our area so we just had to bike to it, but it was crazy. Scavenger hunt, Tug of War, Relay race, you name it. It was super Fun, and I got to say hi to a couple people I met in Ibaraki even 😁After the sports thing I got a bit of guitar practice in and we did some Weekly Planning which was actually super good. It was nice to have a chance

Sunday Today was fast Sunday, so We had the fast and testimony meeting, and I went up to share a few scriptures I read that I learned from. I also made a couple jokes like “If I give my testimony today I don't need to give a talk next Sunday, right?" That kind of thing lol. It was a good Sunday, filled with DL followups and other good stuff.

Pday Today was lit. We went to the mall, which was busier Than we've ever seen it. Crazy 🤪 I got a few packs of Magic and a little deck for a game called Union Arena so I could play more with Magbago長老. Afterwards we checked out a thrift store I’d seen on Google maps and it was awesome 🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥🔥 they had a bunch of CDs for 100¥ each, and I got like 5 I recognized. I was talking with the lady there and she was super sweet, She even gave me a recommendation for music and a CD carrier free of charge?? What?? So chill. We gave her a flyer with the location of our church and told her she was welcome to join our Eikaiwa class, or if she could give the flyer To a friend with interest. Definitely going back, I didn't even get to look at the guitars 👀 Today at the church Shiko came pretty early to practice guitar so Him and I played guitar and talked for like 2 and a half hours while Magbago長老 called his family. He brought a huge Amp that could have 2 guitars attached at once. He also showed me some cool books About Chinese monsters and creatures. Pretty wild. I also learned the C minor pentatonic scale which is super useful. (I think it's called that lol). Tonight me and Magbago長老 played Union Arena And even right after opening my deck and not knowing any of the cards I almost beat him, like we both thought we had lost several times, If I'd played things slightly differently at the end I totally could have won lol. He was freaking out over how close it was even with my no-knowledge Of my cards lol.

Longer email this time. Hope it's not too much (I know it's not for my family but still lol)

I love you guys. I'm doing alright, still got some challenges but it's been a good mission so far 😁 I'm so excited for what's next

Elder Killian

i did not buy the titanic cd sorry
summary: DCM meeting, guitar jam, tried okonomiyaki, mogi lesson, study prep, guitar gift.

subject: this week: untitled
body:
please reply to: kaden.killian@missionary.org

Tuesday Interviews with Sano会長 and DCM! It was awesome. Because we were in Wakayama for DCM, we were able to go over to eat dinner at a Filipino member's house. Her name (or at least what we call her is V姉妹(V shimai)) I ended up sitting by their water cooler and V姉妹 was laughing to tears about how it was a trap and now I have to get water for everyone when they need refills. I was also laughing so much to the point of tears just because of how funny she thought this simple thing was. It was a riot. She made a bunch of different food, one thing was like crossections of pig leg or something; the texture made it so hard to eat, I needed to chew it with something else in my mouth (I didn't by the way lol). I only had a third then gave the rest to my companion. Super sweet family. When we were back home in our apartment that night a member showed up with food at our apartment at almost midnight, it was nuts. He's not 100% with it I don't think, but he's super kind to bring by food.

Wednesday we stopped by the art store lady again, this time we were able to put some of our Eikaiwa flyers in her shop! She also sent me out of the store with more art supplies but made me not pay for it 😭😭 she's Impossible bro I literally wanna help out her store. She's so sweet, we invited her to our Eikaiwa, gonna try and invite her to church next time we meet. Also they are closed on Sundays so there's a good chance she can!!! Tonight we tried out a game night instead of Eikaiwa, and everyone loved it but Shiko lol. He can get over it though 😂😂 We played a bit of poker together so hopefully that helped him enjoy it a bit lol. He really lives Eikaiwa. It went pretty well in general, we are going to try it on the first Wednesday of every month going forward.

Thursday Today was awesome. We got to meet with Setsuko and her friend Asako to play Ground Golf! Sister Dono agreed to join us for the lesson, but she really just got roped into playing Ground Golf with us lol. I could tell she wasn't maybe thrilled lol. afterwards though, she was able to talk a lot about the Church with Setsuko. She also explained a bit of the role that women play in God's work. It was brief, but really neat. Hopefully we can have Asako more involved next chance we have a lesson. Before the Ground Golf Magbago長老 and I went to Wendy's and actually got some food. Low-key ⬇️🗝 mega mid, way better in America. On the way home from Ground Golf, we stopped by a member's house and left a note since they weren't there. Later that day they actually called and said thanks! 🙏 Tonight was our last dinner outing with Jiro, he is going to Australia! We went to an Udon shop, it was super tasty. Jiro asked us some weird questions in the car ride too which was fun lol. Probably don't wanna write them in my email lol. We figured out more of where he'll be in Australia so we can refer him better. We exchanged contact info and sent him off with a hug.

Friday We got up pretty early this morning to go all the way down to Gobo to participate in an Eikaiwa at a high school?! It was crazy. We walked almost 2 hours from the station to the school, it was a long walk. We got there and I felt like I was in an anime. It was weird to be in a Japanese school. It was fun to talk with the students, but some of them were less than enthusiastic lol. Looking forward to next month’s class (if I'm still around). I also met a guy at church fixing the Air conditioning, When I asked him if he knew a Brother Killian, he said yeah, I do? And I told him he's my uncle and his jaw just dropped lol. He also happened to see us when we went to get Udon too lol. He’s super awesome 😁

Saturday We had our first Eikaiwa class in Ozaki! Unfortunately there were only a few previous participants that showed up, and there weren't any kids that came for the youth class 😂 Before now we haven't exactly had many chances to get the word out so there's still hope 😁 today we also got some tasty Okonomiyaki at a shop called Mugen (endless). It was gas.

On Sunday I gave my talk! It was fabulous. I thought it went well. Below is what I read from and translated in Japanese. It's pretty basic but it was tricky on the fly haha.

My talk

By Kaden Killian

(Explain how I always title my talks that)

Today I have been asked to talk missionary work

I have been a missionary for less than a year

Just like my introduction paper says, I have served in ibaraki, Marugame, and now in Sennan.

I haven't been a missionary for very long, but I have already learned so much. I believe that everyone of you guys can learn through missionary work as well

I think that everyone can grow from a mission. There are new experiences, you meet new people, and you even get to live with a lot of different companions throughout your mission. I have learned so much from my past and present companions.

Like I said, I think everyone probably will grow from a mission, but there are a lot of things that we can do that will change how much we grow.

For me, I have found that I have learned and grown the most by turning outside of myself, and looking to those around me. This could be friends, ward members, and even my companions.

Basically, by turning outwards we are becoming more like the Savior, and we are doing Missionary work.

I want to talk a little bit about what the meaning of missionary work is. I think that missionary work is teaching others like the Savior would. This of course can be about the gospel, but what else did Jesus Christ do besides just teaching about the gospel?

Let's read some scriptures to find out.

3rd Nephi 11:9 he reached out And it came to pass that he stretched forth his hand…

3rd Nephi 17:7 he healed …Bring them hither and I will heal them, for I have compassion upon you; my bowels are filled with mercy.

Mark 8:6 he fed 6 And he commanded the people to sit down on the ground: and he took the seven loaves, and gave thanks, and brake, and gave to his disciples to set before them; and they did set them before the people

These are a few things that we can do to do missionary work. I would invite each of you to try and see what other things that Jesus did in the scriptures during your own studies.

I'd like to recount one of my experiences I had back in Marugame. One time I was by the beach with my companion Elder Martinson; While we were over there, we saw a lady picking up trash. I noticed some trash nearby, and decided to go over and help her out. She was very happy, and we were able to introduce ourselves. She gave us some treats, then went to find nearby workers to give treats to as well!

She reached out, she fed, and she healed (with treats).

This lady set a good example for me and my companion. She did not know us, or the workers, and she was in a place that she did not know. I think that we are the same way.

In my mission, as I have followed the example of this lady and the example of the Savior, I have seen miracles and success. As we prioritize knowing and interacting with those around us, we are following the example of the Savior. We can find comfort and reassurance as we follow God’s plan for us.

I believe that Jesus is our Savior. I have really felt his love. I have also felt love from Heavenly father. I love you all, and I want to follow Christ's example. I believe that even though I am imperfect, I can feel peace as I try my best to be a good missionary.

I say these things in the name of Jesus Christ, Amen.

Also gonna wrap up my email here. Hope you guys are all doing well, I love hearing from you all so don't hesitate to send me an email or 3 or 4 😉😂 Love you guys!!! Things are going better lately, increased efforts and humility make all the difference.

Elder Killian
summary: Arrived in Japan, interviewed Sano and DCM, ate dinner at Filipino member's house, received unexpected food from family, tried game night instead of Eikaiwa.

subject: Magbago out, Sofoul is in
body: Reply to: Kaden.killian@missionary.org It's been quite a while. So I wrote a lot to make up for it. I don't know if anyone but my family will enjoy reading this much random stuff about my experiences but thanks to anyone who does 😂P-day Today we looked around at the store where I've gotten CDs before, and I (can you guess??) bought a few CDs. Afterwards we got some groceries and spent most of our afternoon at the Church. Later for dinner we met with Shin Chan, and he took us to eat Shabu-Shabu. We talked a bit about his past experiences with the church, and it was cool to hear. He went to BYU and lived at a member's house. To kind of repay them, he went with them to sacrament meeting a bit. He says he really liked singing the hymns. He lives most of the time in Germany I think working for an airline. He speaks German better than Japanese at this point, and his English is great too. We invited him to church these past two weeks but he has been either busy or sick, so couldn’t make it. Hopefully we can stay in good contact with him still, he's a super generous and funny guy. He also loooves Eikaiwa. He's going back to Germany next Wednesday, but we are gonna meet next Tuesday to say goodbye. He visits a lot so I will probably get to meet him again.TuesdayToday we passed out flyers for our Ozaki Eikaiwa and it was great! While we were streeting, we saw a house (literally the house across the road from the community center we teach Eikaiwa at) with some big beautiful paintings drying or on display. Of course we went up and rang the doorbell, and a woman came out and talked with us! Apparently her husband made the paintings, and he likes putting them out. He wasn't there but we said we'd love to talk to him about art. Maybe we can go out plein air painting together 👀👀 Anyways we told the lady about our Eikaiwa, and she took a flyer and is going to show it to all her friends! She said she even has an interest in learning English too. She said we can park our bikes by the side of her house when we distribute flyers; she was so nice. Big miracle. Back at the church we met up with Shiko for some guitar practice, and he helped to lower the strings on my guitar to make it generally more balanced. It's like way easier to play and bend the strings too. I accidentally started learning on Hard Mode without even knowing 💀WednesdayToday was Eikaiwa, which went pretty normally, but before Eikaiwa we did a lot of cool things. We were able to pass out flyers again near the station in Ozaki, and we stopped to talk with a lady watering her flowers. She was super sweet and she took like 10 flyers to give out to her kids and friends. Really cool! We also visited art store lady again, her name is Watanabeさん this time we were able to sit down with her in the store and have a lesson! We heard about her background; she's kind of bound by her marriage to Buddhism but she says she's Christian at heart. One of her school teachers growing up was Catholic, and she kind of went with that until she was married. She's too busy taking care of her husband to do much outside of work, and she said she can't attend our church meetings. She is able to talk with us though, so we’re gonna try and have a lesson with her every week! Super awesome!Thursday and Friday weren't much to talk about, just a day kind of stuck at the apartment, and a day of SKK. SKK was really good though, I think we were able to talk about a lot of good things. Hopefully we can move forward with even more strength and effort!SaturdayToday we had Eikaiwa, which was dampened by the rain to say the least. There were only a few people who actually showed up. Tonight we had another crazy dinner at Bishop's house. Tonight's dinner was a whole ride. Firstly, a returned Japanese missionary from 20-30 years ago came to visit! Her bean area was actually Sennan. Super cool. She's high maintenance and kind of gives off miss piggy vibes with her personality 😂 it was so funny to watch the bishop and his wife interact with her. We had tacos today, not just tacorice but semi legit tacos. Somehow I think I managed to eat more than I did that one time I threw up outside their house, but because I was sitting down I was OK And had some time to process things. I ate a bunch of bishops special chili with beans and cheese 😂, 3 big Soft tacos, a slice of chocolate cake, a cup of ice cream, and some pudding Magbago長老 brought. It was intense. I also brought my guitar so I could play a hymn for the bishop, it was fun. On the way out I noticed a slug sliding it's way up the inside part of the front door. I took it off and put it on my finger and showed it to the Bishop's wife and she bolted into another room like the second she saw it lol. She hates slugs apparently 😂 I took it out for her. I'm so kind. 𓆑SundayToday was a good Sunday. I felt grateful as I took the sacrament. During the lesson we joined the youth class (Just one girl and the teacher) and I used the Bishop (who was also participating) as a slight reference (photo below). Besides Church, today was mostly DL followups and some semi-distracted media finding. I need to try and be more focused lately 👓MondayToday we had our last Pday together as a companionship. It was pretty good! We started off the day by visiting Aeon mall as usual, getting some food and some cards along the way. We also visited my favorite thrift shop/estate sale place to check out a cool guitar I'd seen before. 👀 Turns out someone had already bought it and it was just sitting in the back 😭😂 no worries though, probably a good thing I couldn't buy it. Later around lunch time we visited a Filipino member named Sister Allen? Aren? No clue. Anyways we showed up at her house with a member (on time) and she wasn't even there 😂 Only her 10 year old son (probably 10-12?) answered the door and had us sit outside until she showed up lol. Magbago長老 brought her a cool pudding that he has been working hard to make. We talked a little with her, and somehow ended up getting sent off to play sports with her son just the 3 of us at a park. We played dodgeball, tag, and baseball. I felt like a small child again, it was surprisingly refreshing to put on a glove and throw some pitches. We were wasted after we were done 😂 Photo below (we look happy not wasted at least) Lastly I met a kid smoking at the convenience store, basically said what's up and now we're chill. He has seen us walking around before, if I meet him again he's got interest in English so I bet he’d come to Eikaiwa. after the comvenience store dude we got dinner at a cool Okonomiyaki and other Japanese food place, and are now pretty good friends with the owner I think! I wanna give them an Eikaiwa flyer next chance we get, they might even be willing to hang it up for us 👀TuesdayToday was Guitar Practice with Shiko again, although only for around an hour. Shiko taught me some more tips for practicing, lately it's been making more sense to me. (I say that but there was one pattern or something that I just COULD NOT understand lol 😂😂, Shiko goes pretty fast so its hard to tell what I should do to imitate his motions). After guitar practice we went out with Shinchan for dinner, his last time to meet with us before he goes back to Germany again. This time we went to a super cool place where you bread and fry a bunch of food on skewers at your own table. It was good but like that cannot be healthy 😂 we also got to hear more about Shinchan's background, and how he got to be so familiar with the church. We talked about a bunch of commandments and different stuff too, it was a good conversation. We kind of challenged him to meet up with the German missionaries if He can! We will see how it goes 😆WednesdayToday we visited Watanabeさん at the art store! We sat down and talked for like an hour, and she even showed us some cool art from Katsuhiro Hokusai, a famous wood block printmaker. As we were leaving and Magbago長老 was saying his goodbyes, he was given a cool golden rooster statue (For his birth year, year of the chicken) by Watanabeさん！It looked pretty expensive so he was super excited lol. We also had Eikaiwa today which was fun and funny as usual. Good times!ThursdayToday we met up with a sick referral named Sumi! She also works near the airport, so we met with her near Aeon to practice Japanese and get to know her. It was super cool, we were able to teach her about how we believe that Heavenly Father, Jesus Christ and the Spirit are 3 separate beings. We are gonna meet with her again in two weeks! Before Meeting with Sumi we were doing some housing and streeting and stopped by a place for a drink. Basically the walls were covered in art and Hawaiian vibe stuff it was sick. We talked with the owner and he knows about our church. Definitely not getting the vibe he's interested but he likes English stuff so we can connect with him through that, and also the fact that the place is a music venue sort of too, so my love for guitar can also play a role. They were also kind enough to pin up our Eikaiwa flyer in their shop! So chill! Definitely going back. Btw also exchanged stickers with the owner, pretty sick.FridayToday as we attempted to visit a member in IzumiSano, a lady noticed us and stopped to tell us that where she lived was a good place to do missionary work because people aren't super strong in their Buddhist faith. Crazy. One of her friends from a while ago is a member of our church so she knows a bit about our church. We got her name but not any other info from her, hopefully we can see her again doing stuff in that area!SaturdayToday was transfers. Me and Magbago長老 said goodbye. We traveled all the way to Kobe and I waited alone (waiting for new comp) for around 45 minutes at the station. It was actually great. Not saying that missionaries shouldn't work in pairs but it felt pretty wild, almost like I was detached from the mission for a bit. I used this time to get some snacks for my new companion when he arrived, and as I was walking away from the 7-11 I felt like I should stop by two foreigners in front of a building. They were a couple waiting for the wife's brother to pick them up. I said hi, and just that I felt like I should talk to them. It was such a cool experience. They are from Australia, and are doing something with the brother in Japan. Anyways I probably could have brought up something more spiritual than just saying I was a missionary for my church, but it was a super neat experience nonetheless. I felt such great love for them and everyone else I saw while I was waiting. Then, I met up with my new companion, Sofoul長老! We came to Japan at the same time, so it'll be fun to see what missionary work methods we can come up with! Getting home was less than ideal lol. We rode the two hour train home, simple. Then Sofoul長老 realized he had the keys to his last companion’s apartment 😂 So we had to ride 2 hours back. Then, we had the ride home from that. On the Osaka Loop train line, we somehow ended up riding the entire loop accidentally instead of transferring, once we figured that out we had to ride it all the way to our stop again lol. After that, on our way back our train shut down due to the weather, and we had to wait like half an hour at the station. After we got onto a new train that showed up, it didn't stop at our station and went like 20 minutes past it. Then, we got out and went the other way and finally got back to the apartment lol. Crazy Day.Sunday was busy! We were at the church for 4 hours (including church) before we could go home for lunch, which we didn't do after leaving church lol. The Dono family was nice enough to drive us to the city hall to look at the art exhibition that Watanabeさん from the art store has been hyping us up for for the past couple months. It was super cool, and we got to meet with some fellow artists 😎 They offered me a sign up for the next year's art show; I might be able to leave some art with Watanabeさん and she can enter it for me, but who knows. Pretty fun! The rest of the day was mostly more apartment cleaning and DL followups. Sofoul長老 is quite particular about having a perfectly clean apartment which is great! I'm pretty carbon neutral as far as apartment messiness goes, (aside from my own desk 😂) so the cleanliness of the apartment is generally as clean as my companion keeps things. Besides that Sofoul長老 likes playing the guitar, biking, sports and his girlfriend back home 💁‍♀️ He seems like a chill guy, I'm hoping he warms up to me more as I get to know him lol. Cool miracle from today, we called Sagawa (the shipping company that we use to move our stuff between areas) to set up a time to drop off Sofoul長老’s stuff on Monday, and we randomly got a call from the guy later the same day saying he could drop off the stuff today because he was driving nearby. It was so nice and gave us so many options for Monday rather than being stuck without a bike. Super chill guy 😎 Pday MondayToday was pretty wild for a Pday. Very exhausted. It can mostly be summed up by saying we went and had a beach barbecue with a member named Inoue Kyoudai. He's a pretty chill guy and often stops by our apartment with almost or expired food he gets from a market or something for cheap. Anyways we spent like 6 or more hours with him driving to the beach, setting up the Barbecue stuff, cooking, and even stopping by a place for a haircut for Sofoul長老 😆 Pretry crazy, took foreeeeever but was pretty fun. I think Inoue兄弟 is having a pretty big faith crisis ATM so I'm gonna see if we can help him through the members. He comes to church every week and loves the missionaries (probably members too) but doesn't see much of the reason for anything we believe in anymore 😭 I will also communicate this with my companion cause I don't think he got much of it, at least to the extent Inoue兄弟 expressed things. Afterwards we did some shopping, and I even got a couple Allen wrenches and new guitar strings along the way. Pretty crazy first Pday for the transfer, I'm excited to try finding with a new companion this week!Spiritual thought? 👀These past two weeks have been pretty crazy. There have been a lot of things I've seen and learned from. There has been a challenge for the mission to read through the Book of Mormon twice in two transfers, and I still haven't finished it once. It kind of sucks hearing everyone talk about it and everyone having such high expectations of me when I'm doing so poorly at it. Ever since I left the MTC I just really haven't had a good plan for my personal study in the morning. Things have actually been looking a bit better recently, but it's not been amazing as a whole. I don't know if I can say that I've been trying my best or not, but I've genuinely been trying really hard, even if I can't read like 6 chapters a day (what I'd need to read to stay on track). There are a lot of things I've been worrying about but even through it I have learned and felt so much from even the small bits I have read from the Book of Mormon. It's such a fascinating book. One thing that stood out to me was Jacob 5. In this chapter, a prophet named Zenos gives an allegory of an Olive tree. It's a super long chapter in the Book of Mormon, but while I read it I like to think about the way that the people who lived back when it was written would have seen it. When I think about how much people back then relied on oil and on trees for their livelihood, as well as the worth of even a single tree, it helps me to get a better perspective of things. I would invite anyone reading this to try and view someone else's challenges or situation from that person's point of view. If you can do that, I promise that you will notice more opportunities to help them, and you will generally be a more outward and less self centered person. I still need to work on this too. If you have any neat experience with this please let me know, or even just send me an email to say hi if you want 😆I love all you guys so much. A mission is difficult and rewarding. I appreciate your prayers and keep you in mine ❤️Elder Killianキリアン長老
summary: Responded, wrote backlog, bought CDs, shopped, attended Church, met Shin Chan, invited for goodbye Tuesday.

subject: Less WiFi more Nephi
body:
Reply to: Kaden.killian@missionary.org

This week was pretty chill, and imma start things out with a note before we jump in. Ever since this last transfer started I've had some pretty awful anxious feelings in the mornings. Its like theres a big weight Suspended over me. I've prayed about this earnestly and the last couple days (Saturday and Sunday) I've felt much better. If things are still feeling really bad I will consult one of the mission Health people about it, but I want to say that God Hears our prayers and eases our burdens according to our faith and willingness. I know that he loves each and every one of you.

Tuesday Today we decided to try and visit a member and then do housing nearby. On the bike ride to the lady's house, we were Riding along the side of the road when My companion (in front of me) suddenly swerved to the left to go behind a guarding rail. I didn’t have enough time to go after him, and I obviously couldn't swerve to the right or I'd get hit by a car, so I tried going left and my tires skidded and my Knee went straight into the divider 👀 luckily It was like sheet metal and kind of curved, but it was still a big impact. My knee was OK but pretty banged up. (I made a little sketch of what I ran into) Anyways we kept going to the member’s place, and she was actually home! It turns out she showed up to our Eikaiwa last Saturday not knowing it was canceled 😱 I hope not a lot of people showed up 💀 anyways she was super sweet and I don't think their family has been to church in a little while, and she seemed a bit hesitant talking with us. She is going to a stake beach cleaning thing This next Saturday though, which is great! We unfortunately can’t go because of Eikaiwa but it sounds like a good opportunity! After visiting this lady, we started to walk around and do a bit of housing. We found a place to stop our bikes, and went to say hi to a lady in her front yard. Then the crazy things started happening lol. We were talking with her about religion and stuff, and she said that its hard for her to believe there is a God with war going on across the world. She also told us about how she was one time Working on the flowers in front of her house, when two Jehovah Witnesses came up and asked what she was doing. I don’t entirely understand everything she was saying, but I think it was something along the lines of she should just get rid of or kill the injured flowers? Idk anyways she thought it was pretty mean of them, and she was even crying about it (and also about seeing war and stuff on TV). Then this random guy walked by and said Hi, and ended up being a part of the conversation lol. He lives near the lady (Nao-Chan) and his name is Jin-Chan. We talked with the two of them for almost an hour out in front of their house about all kinds of things.
