Jin-Chan has apparently met with Missionaries in the past and told us that it is just so much easier and more comfortable to practice English with Mormons lol. His English is actually pretty great. Sofoul長老 tried giving a book of Mormon to Nao-Chan before we left, and she basically said because she isn't going to read it, there's no meaning in the gesture. Hopefully we Can go back and visit again and maybe she will change her mind 🤔 Throughout this wild conversation she also gave us some home-dried and flavored seaweed, as well as some cool citrus drinks. What a sick lady. Before Jin-Chan left he told us that we can talk again if we see him around that area, and Nao-Chan is ok with us coming back to visit her. Around the end of our wild conversation we also got to meet Nao-Chan's husband, who was getting ready to head to work. He was also super nice, it would be cool if we can meet with them again. Anyways, after that we headed over to the seaside to try and find somewhere I could sit down for a little bit. We saw two fishermen sitting by a shed, and asked if they knew of a place to sit nearby, and they offered that we go and sit down with them 😂 We were talking with the two guys when another one showed up and one of the first two left. Definitely a bizarre interaction but it's cool to meet with people in all circumstances of life. The original Fisherman that stayed talked about some wild stuff but also invited us to a place where everyone that comes can have a meal for free. It happened to be the very next day, so we agreed to go! (Also it's a once a month event so it's pretty awesome we met the guy right then) we eventually had to leave but it was cool talking 😆 We ended off the day by heading to the church to practice guitar with Shiko which was fun as usual.

Wednesday Today we had a bit of time for streeting around Ozaki and talked with a sweet Old Lady. She said that walking around the block and talking with Old people like her was doing a public service lol. She was very kind. We also took some time to write some more note cards that we can leave in Mailboxes as we house. I always try to write every one differently, so I’m not super fast lol. I believe that this Extra effort might be what someone needs to see to make the step and call our number. Today we visited the art store again and talked for a while with Watanabeさん and one of her friends who teaches a bunch of art classes. Elder Sofoul and I shared a bunch of cool photos (from me, art I had made, from him, floral arrangements his girlfriend had made) with the two of them. I showed them the picture I made of Jesus's Tomb after his resurrection and explained to them a bit of that story, it was really neat. They said that even before they knew what the drawing was that they could feel something looking at it. Maybe I can make some more gospel art to share with them, it seems like a good way to connect spiritually. Before Eikaiwa tonight, we tried going to the “everyone Eats together” type place that the two fishermen had recommended. It was so awesome! It was mostly kids that were serving food around to a bunch of people in a big pavilion. We both got given a tray of food, and it was actually really good. We talked with two ladies and they both are going to come to our Eikaiwa, and also likely bring their kids! They exchanges Lines with us too 😎. We also saw one of the Fishermen that we met before and Said Hi to him, and I somehow got him to exchange Line info with me too, even though he didn't understand how his phone really worked at all lol. It was sick. He texted me a few days later and invited us to the next two events, over the next two months. Super awesome. Afterwards we hurried back to the Church to set up for Eikaiwa as well as play ping pong with Yasuedaさん、one of our regulars At Eikaiwa. Eikaiwa went well, and after that we headed back to the apartment for the night.

Thursday Today was awesome! We had a chance to meet with a returned missionary, Pentreath長老, at the Bishop's House! It was super chill talking with him, especially considering we were actually disappointed when we realized we couldn't ask him about friends in the area because he went home, but we unexpectedly got a chance to talk with him and got a lot of good info!! The food was great as usual, and I again had to be very cautious not to make myself throw up lol. We spent some time today also contacting some Grey dots and one of them said he'd like to meet up with us for a lesson! We will meet with him next week 🙏 Today was also SKK (weekly planning) and we got a good chance to talk about or friends and what we wanna do with the area. Good times. Lastly we had a super cool lesson with Yasuedaさん and Dono姉妹 joining us to help share her experiences. We talked about the book of Mormon and a bit about the Godhead. Yasuedaさん doesn't want to join our church but maybe her heart will open as she continues to hear our Lessons and messages. We also played a bit of ping pong together too 😆

Friday and Saturday Friday was a district leader meeting in Kobe, and during that time I got to have mini exchanges with Pistorius長老. He's a super humble Elder, I vibe with him. We did some studies in a park whilst getting assailed by mosquitos, and tried a bit of streeting. We only really talked with one cool old dude but he was very friendly and probably has a good impression of the missionaries now which is great. After that we went to meet Up with one of the Elders’ Friends “Lime.” his name probably doesn't mean lime but you pronounced it like that so it's kinda cool. He's big into fashion. We went to Denny's With him, and Denny's is lowkey a super boujee restaurant in Japan. It was kinda pricey and it had fancy music playing lol. Pretty chill mini exchange. The next day we met 4 chill lads at a park and danced with them. I also tripped and fell into one of their bikes and lost aura 😭 We gave them each a flyer to our Eikaiwa and they followed Sofoul長老’s Instagram 😎 Today we did a bunch of housing as well, it wasn't super effective but it feels great to be doing so much each day. Saturday morning was also Eikaiwa, and 5 new people came!!! Super awesome. It was kind of awkward and mostly just introducing the class but it was so fire to have more people there. Afterwards we went to KFC so Sofoul長老 could get a free biscuit with a coupon. Later we went to Panda Express and went to the Lego store (to say hi to someone who works there that we met before, not so that Elder Sofoul could buy a lego F1 car) and even got to say hi to Lime who actually worked nearby 😂 Good times

Sunday Today was Fast and Testimony meeting, and it was a good spiritual time. During Second hour I made some cool art in our lesson I will attach a photo 📸 after that we had our Missionary work coordination meeting, which was great. We got to talk about the huge success with Eikaiwa, how we attempted visiting members, and also made some plans to visit more members with members 😆 throughout these things were also followups with the district and cleaning of the apartment, which is almost to the point where its probably the mission's cleanest apartment, its getting there. Later we met back up at the church to have a dinnereon (luncheon but later) for the soon to be Elder Otani. There was a bunch of cool food, and at the end everyone went around in a circle and gave their final advice to him. It was pretty sweet to see the impact someone can have in their own ward. All in all, great Sunday! (Today Bishop also told us about a phrase Less WiFi, More Nephi. Apparently it means like focus less on your phone and more on being like Nephi Or reading The Book of Mormon lol)

Pday For Pday we had a much more laid back series of events than we did last time with the Barbecue and what not. We visited that one thrift shop I always visit, Hobby-Off, a home improvement Store, and we did our shopping. Highlight was definitely calling with my family and playing Boggle. Aside from Pday stuff, we had an Eikaiwa Video call with Aime, and spent a little time making Origami We can give to members. Sofoul長老 made an X-wing with Facebook instructions, it was dope. Not a super crazy Pday, but it was a good time.

Love you guys so much. I will try and send some time responding to people this next week, I've been bad about that. I appreciate all That you do for me.

Elder Killian
summary: Recovered from sprained foot, dealt with anxious feelings, bike accident.

subject: Weekly Emailn't
body: Please reply to: Kaden.killian@missionary.org Hello everyone, thank you for Joining me for this weekly review. Not! I am going to go over everything from this week and this last week in my next email. For today I have a few random things from Elder Killian's brain to share 🧠Spiritual message/updateThis past week has been great, and I've only felt anxious a couple times and only for a few minutes at a time. There are a couple reasons I believe this is the case.1. PrayerEver since I've started experiencing these somewhat anxious feelings I've immediately taken to the knees to pray about it to my Heavenly Father. I trust that He hears my prayers and is concerned about my well-being. I also believe that the Heavenly Help we are prescribed is correlated somehow to our faith and open-ness with God. I haven't felt immediate relief from these feelings in the moment, but steadily I have noticed a positive change. I attribute this to Heavenly Father, and also in part to my trust that I place in Him.2. Anxious vs Anxiously EngagedDoctrine and Covenants chapter 58 reads:27 Verily I say, men should be anxiously engaged in a good cause, and do many things of their own free will, and bring to pass much righteousness; 28 For the power is in them, wherein they are agents unto themselves. And inasmuch as men do good they shall in nowise lose their reward.We sometimes can't 100 percent control our emotions or our circumstances, but there are things that we can control, as we are "agents unto [ourselves.]" I have had an increased focus on this kind of mindset these past couple of weeks, and it's made all the difference. As I focus on putting all of my efforts into bringing others closer to the Savior and showing love unto my brothers and sisters in Japan, I have slowly seen the anxious feelings I've felt get pushed to the side, or even covered up by the bright and rejoicable experiences I have seen through that increased focus.I am very grateful for what God and the Savior have done to ease my burdens. I promise that as You (whoever you may be, member of the Church or not) walk forward with trust that you are being watched over, and acting in Righteousness, you will be blessed and see a great change in your lives. We all need to change, no matter where we are at. I am still trying to change each day, failing, repenting, and slowly crawling forward. Some days feel like I'm being dragged backwards! But I have the desire to move forward, and thanks to the Savior sometimes that's even enough.Poetry1. Never-woulda-thoughtPokemon is LitMy companion is BrainwashedIt's pretty funny 😂2. Crime SeenStem cells peek around the corner;The left side of my brain has gotThe right side of my brain in a choke-hold and if I said that the left side wasn't being bold I would be denying I'm a foreigner, which would therefore be a lie that I'd just told. The reason there's an argument is that the left side really meant to argue just for the sake of it, and it didn't sit right with the right side of my brain. "It wouldn't be right" just to leave those words suspended in the air so the right side of my brain upended the chair of the left side (who wasn't on the right side at the time) which (if I'm right) provoked the scene and the image that we've just imagined right here. I've got nothing left to say 'sides this whole thing just so happened to be a stroke of luck or a stroke of genius. On second thought it might be right to say it was just a stroke.3. Ceramic FeathersIf a bird of ceramic took flightIt would have to be due to a trickWith "feathers of masonry?" Yeah, rightI refuse to imagine they'd stickIn this moment just nowI realized... something grandAs I furrow my browIn disbelief andClose off my mind to the idea of this eventIt's quite obvious now that things just won't go how I'd meantIf we limit ourselves from the get-go I fearThat aside from this "brick bird" much greater, much more important things will veerOff courseAstrayInto lost placesFar awaySo make the decision to stretch your perspectiveThere's no need to scrutinize like a detectiveThink openly and take all your measuresTo fly far away on your ceramic feathersConclusion!Thanks for tuning in. I had fun writing each part of this, and I hope it will have some meaning with whoever reads this. Love you guys!!Elder Killian
summary: Arrived in Japan, reviewed week's progress, shared spiritual insights.

subject: These 3 weeks
body:
Please reply to: kaden.killian@missionary.org

This last two weeks (now three weeks 🙈😳) have gone by very quickly. It's very hard to believe that we are (more than) half way through the transfer already. This time I'm gonna try and write a somewhat shorter summary of each day. (Spoiler I didn't really do this lol)

Tuesday Today was rainy; however, we took the chance to go out of our way and do some housing. We ended up talking with a cool lady who, as we introduced our church, began to remember various experiences she's had with our church in the past. She even said she has a Book of Mormon in her house, she just lost it. We need to go back and talk with her more! She even said we look like Jesus, it was pretty cool

Wednesday Today we had exchanges with the ZLs, so I got to hang out with Mackay長老 all day 🎉🎉 We revisited that cool train restaurant and got to talk with a guy in there who is a choir teacher who also speaks some pretty solid English. Sometime we are going to try and visit their choir class on Wednesday morning. Afterwards we went to visit Hiroko, and talked to her for a while. It was nice for her to say hi to Mackay長老. We did some housing and tried to visit some members but it wasn't super fruitful. Still good efforts though. One crazy thing that we witnessed was a duck in the middle of the road getting eaten by a snake; a Japanese guy drove by, saw it, got out of his car and kicked at the snake, eventually grabbing both animals and tossing them away from each other. It was pretty wild. Later was Eikaiwa which was also fun!

Thursday Today we met with Sumi for another lesson! It went well, and she's going to read the Introduction to the Book of Mormon before we meet again. For lunch we attempted to go to a cool indo curry place, but google maps led us to another that was less desirable. It was at a little mall, and while we were there we got a few donuts too. Later we attempted visiting members and housing, and on the way home we saw a guy working in a field. Sofoul長老 asked if there was anything we could do to help, and he agreed to meet us in the same spot the next day. 🎉

Friday Today was Zone conference! It was fun to go and catch up with some previous companions, and I also enjoyed getting to tell a joke I made about a vacuum to everyone. I even brought a vacuum into my interview with Sano会長, it was fun. I also got to spend some time talking with a member from Wakayama named Morimoto Kyoudai. He's super nice. After we got back from Zone conference, we were able to meet up with that guy from the field, and we helped him trim some weeds in a bean field. It was cool, and we even exchanged lines with him.

Saturday This morning we had our Eikaiwa, which went well. Afterwards we stopped by a nearby place for some lunch, and talked with the owner a bit. It was chill, hopefully we can keep having good experiences with him. Today we also picked up a pair of cheap binoculars from a lady on Jimoty, and we actually got to talk with her and introduce ourselves. She wasn't super interested or anything but it was a good example of how we can have meaningful interactions through Jimoty lol. Another cool thing we did was stop by a place called “children's freedom place” roughly translated. It was some kind of festival where kids can play without rules or that kind of thing. There was a bunch of cardboard and I took the liberty of making some cardboard weapons for the kids. It was lit. Also there was a super sweet little girl who drew a picture of me and my companion on a piece of wood 🪵 to end off the day, Otani兄弟 took us out to get sushi even though it was one of his last days with his son before he leaves for the MTC. He said that his son was at home watching movies and tv before he can’t anymore 😂

Sunday Today Sakurako came to church! She's a referral from Abeno, and she's 20. She seemed to have had a good time, but she says she didn't understand much. She has the desire to understand though, so it's awesome. Today it was pretty rainy, and we ended up getting driven around by Miyagawa兄弟 and had prayers with like 4 or 5 different families in the ward. It was a cool experience. Afterwards we even got to have some super tasty pasta from Miyagawa姉妹。So good 🍝

Pday Pday was an interesting mix of things. Got to call my family, lowkey often my highlight of the week lol, and afterwards we biked In the rain to go shopping at Aeon. It was fun, and I got like 2 crepes. Tastyyyyy. Later we got picked up by the Donos to drive and visit a less active member named Sister Hayashi. She loves the church and would like to go, but can't because of how far it is and because of her health. We shared a spiritual message and she seemed to really like us. We will definitely try to go again!

Tuesday Today we got to get driven by Hirakawa兄弟 to visit a member named Ito兄弟。We basically helped throw out a bunch of old junk and trash that was in front of his house. We had to wait for a guy with a truck to come and pick it all up, so we got to talk with him for a while and admire a bunch of cool old things like film and 10 yen bills in a display case. He also apparently has a bunch of cool tape recorders which I think is super lit. Later today we got to play guitar with Shiko which was fun as usual, he taught us a cool pattern on the guitar, I've been trying to practice it lately.

Wednesday Today we visited Hiroko and had a pretty lengthy conversation. We were able to talk a little bit about Jesus, but it wasn't quite a "lesson.” She was super sweet though and gave my companion a cool stone inkwell for free! I also got a discount on a couple water brushes 🙃 We also attempted to visit some more members, and as we did that we were able to do more housing as well. Later that day was Eikaiwa, and Sakurako came! I shared a spiritual message at the end, I think everyone appreciated it!

Thursday Today was exchanges with the Gobo Elders. They came up to Sennan, and I was partnered with Matsuhisa長老, a 27 year old Japanese missionary who is going home at the end of next transfer. It was super fun to get a chance to talk with him all day. We were hoping to set some goals for our exchange, but we got kidnapped by a member and did a bunch of crazy stuff! (Not really kidnapped but we didn't have much say lol) We ended up going to some interesting Health machine testing meeting thing. Basically everyone goes and sits on pads that are electrically charged somehow. I think it like vibrates your blood, It was a strange sensation. Anyways the member thought that the people giving the lecture were sharing a good message about health, and we could learn how to better teach from watching and listening. There was also a popular song that I knew and sang along to with Matsuhisa長老 which was fun. Afterwards we drove to Brother Ito's house and helped install light fixtures all around his house. We also shoved a huge old tub in the back of the member (Brother Hirakawa’s) car and drove it to a nonmember so he could use it to collect rainwater. Crazy stuff. Before the service stuff we also got to visit an inactive member and a couple friends. It was nice to get around the ward, even though we didn't have like any time for finding together 💀 We also played a game of Pokémon together at the end of the day which I could have won but didn't 🙃 I'm a nice guy what can I say （＾＿－）≡★

Friday Today we had our weekly planning at the library we last had our lesson with Sumi at. It was pretty lit. There was also an art gallery or something open and I went and checked it out while my companion was doing language study. I ended up talking with a few of the artists from the gallery for like an hour and a half. It would have been the right thing to do to get my companion and drag him along, but I was kind of sucked into things. I can do better with that in the future. Afterwards we went and tried to visit more members, and also I finally took Elder Sofoul to get the best Indo curry in the area. It was so good. I was so deathly full though, I took that whole lunch hour to eat everything.

Saturday Today was pretty wild. It was rainy like all day, we had our Eikaiwa in the morning and even had a couple new students show up. For the rest of the day we had our studies, and also basically chilled with the bishop at his house for like 3 hours 😂 His wife had just left for a trip so he was joking about how he was finally free. We got to talk a lot about members who he thinks we should visit, and got good information about members whose houses we attempted visiting. We alllllso watched a movie 🤔🤔🤔🤔 It was called “The Wagon Master” and was from like the 1950s I think. It was in English and had Japanese subtitles. It was actually made not by the church but was about Mormons traveling to the west and their encounters. It was pretty tame and had a church connection so I don't think there was any problem with it, but definitely a rare encounter on the mission lol. Osako Bishop is also really good with English and often surprises us with awkwardly natural phrases like, “Sister Tanigawa called me up.” That one caught us off guard. We also shared a laugh over Sofoul長老 not knowing about pioneer day. Bishop said “are you even Kaiin"? Kaiin meaning member. It was actually hilarious 😂

Sunday Today we had an awesome Sacrament meeting and to celebrate Father's day we even got to eat some brownies. For the lesson portion of church I got to help interpret a lesson on the law of chastity for two teens visiting from Hawaii. It felt like I was teaching a health class, it was awkward lol. I did my best though 👍

Pday Today was district Pday! It was super fun. We all traveled to a place called Rinku Town which is a huge shopping outlet by the airport. We got Panda Express for lunch, and walked around some of the shops. Afterwards we headed over to a cool beach area and played spikeball, beach bochiball (no idea how to spell that) with some rocks, and chilled in the sun. Mackay長老 and I faced off against Sofoul長老 and Lighthall長老, we put up a good fight. Mackay長老 and I lost 2 of 3 rounds, but it's ok because it was due to him not me 😋 Just kidding 🙃 It was actually pretty close all of the rounds. Everyone took the train to get there but me and Sofoul長老 just biked because it was our area. It was like an hour both ways, but really fun. After Pday time we tried to visit Romeo's house, and we were able to drop off some chocolate even though he wasn't home. Solid Pday! (one not solid thing was me spending like 10 dollars on a claw machine 💀 I was so close to getting the prize like 3 times and I just used all of my coins I had for it. I am making a promise to never play a crane game again. We will see how it holds up.)

Tuesday Today we had DCM! Afterwards we went with the Gobo Elders to get lunch (the sisters had to go back home for an appointment) and a super nice lady there paid for our food. It was actually pretty crazy. Fortunately everything was super cheap but it was so nice. Later that day we were able to see a huge miracle as we gave a Book of Mormon to a guy named Kawakami San. He says he is going to read it, and it seemed like our message really resonated with him. We gave him our number, so we are praying for a phone call! We will try and visit his place again sometime. Later we also got to do some wooden sword training with Shiko. My arms were sore for a couple days afterwards but it was fun 🙃

Wednesday Today we had Eikaiwa, did some housing, and we also had another lesson with Yasueda San. She is taking our reading invitations pretty seriously, but doesn't generally seem enthralled at the moment. I believe that as she continues to read the Book of Mormon she will be blessed with an understanding and reassurance.

Thursday Today we had SKK, which was pretty average as far as Weekly planning sessions go. We were able to meet up with someone who wanted to sell some pokemon cards to Sofoul長老, but it was a pretty minimal interaction. Little bit of a bummer. On the train back from the pickup we met a cool guy named Isaac from Israel. He was lowkey so funny. Just imagine the chillest California surfer dude and turn him into a short man with a big beard and also a Chef. When asked about things he was aspiring to do he just always would respond with “Yeah man we'll see how it goes.” He lives in Wakayama so we told him to talk with Missionaries if he ever sees them.

Friday Today we went to Gobo for Eikaiwa at the high school. It was super fun and the missionaries have a really good relationship with the school. We even got to teach one of the teachers a bit about our beliefs with the law of Chastity and the word of wisdom. He's pretty admiring of that. We also got a chance to eat at a member-owned and run Michelin star restaurant. It was insane. It wasn't even all that expensive but it was all like the highest quality food I've had. I'll attach a couple pictures, I had Shrimp mushroom pizza and Gelato. Doubleyou Day.

Saturday Today we did some housing, visited Hiroko (and talked with one of her customers), and even got to spend the evening with the Suehiro family. It was a great day!

Sunday Sacrament meeting, 2nd hour and DCS all went as usual, pretty awesome. We had the ward birthday party thing today where we celebrate everyone's birthdays for the month. It was fun and cute to see everyone's pictures as babies or kids (yes they show these pictures in front of everyone on the projector lol). After church we were able to have a lesson with Sakurako! Tamada姉妹 participated In the lesson too. It was really neat, and it was a good chance to answer Sakurako's questions, as well as teach her more about the Gospel. She said that life is rough, and that's why she is learning more about our church. I am super happy that we get to help her learn more about the church!

Pday Most of today was spent at the church and looking through some cards at Hard-Off before doing our shopping for the week. Pretty average Pday but still a good time. Definitely loved getting to talk with my family.

This was a very quick 3 weeks, and I'm sure not a very quick read for this email, I apologize 😆 I will try and make things more interesting in my next email.

I love you guys. Thank you for your emails, for your support, and for being my family and friends. I look forward to greeting you when I return from this mission, although I wouldn't change my current situation if I could. I am grateful to be a missionary. It's exhausting sometimes, but that doesn't really matter 🙃😁 This is the best thing I can be doing at this moment in time.

Love you all!

Elder Killian
summary: Rainy Tuesday: Met helpful Japanese lady; Wednesday: Exchanges with ZLs, visited choir teacher; Thursday: Lesson with Sumi, lunch at less desirable curry place.

subject: Ah, Genki Daze.
body: Email title: there's a member in this branch named Hisamune長老, I think I've mentioned him but he's literally so funny. We needed his help printing out flyers, and we needed his email to send him a copy of them. Rather than his name or anything normal, his email was "ah_genkidaze," @gmail.com I think. It kind of means Like "Ah, let's take it easy" or something. It's literally so funny 😭😂This week's email is gonna be moderately long. I've had a lot of time to write on trains this week, so buckle up!TuesdayToday was literally filled with Miracles. I'm so grateful for God's hand in my life, and I saw it demonstrated to me in a few big ways today. Yesterday (pday) We headed up to Tottori to stay the night for DCM today. It's a pretty messy apartment, really makes you grateful for your own situation lol. Throughout the night I woke up like 10 times because I was uncomfortable, and also had to kick a bunch of dust or crumbs off of my futon. It was less than desirable, but when I got up in the morning, I felt rejuvenated! First thing that God showed me today! Next, we headed out to walk to the church for DCM. Alas, the curse of companions with poor sense of direction strikes again. The church was supposed to be a 5 minute walk, but we somehow walked almost 20 minutes past it 😅 and everyone had to wait for us to start. I really should have just looked at a map before we left, but it was OK in the end lol. DCM was pretty fun, but the trio of sister missionaries in our district is definitely going through some conflicts lol. Keep them in your prayers! Haha. Afterwards we went and got some Indo curry, and I tried some lychee berries. Pretty interesting. We had to leave in a hurry to catch our trains, poor Nakazawa長老 was dying in the heat 😂 While waiting at a station for our next train, Nakazawa長老 had to go to the bathroom, so I was sitting alone waiting on a bench in the station. I hear a voice say “hello?” This foreigner sits down by me and we introduce ourselves to each other! His name was Berihun, and he's from Ethiopia! He's been living in Japan for like 3-4 years Studying to be a veterinarian researcher. Super cool. I was able to talk with him about religion, and he told me he reads from the Bible and is Christian. I introduced the Book of Mormon, and testified of how Jesus taught and loves his Children all over the world, and how his teachings to the American people are written in the Book of Mormon. He seemed very interested, and when I gave him an English copy of the book, he took it very excitedly and said he's gonna read it! I was able to exchange information with him, and he happens to live like a block away from the church in Tottori! He wants to meet with the missionaries and is also excited to practice Japanese with them. Huge miracle!!!!!! ⚠️⚠️⚠️📢🚨🚨 I just wish bro lived in our area! 😂 The Lord is preparing people all over the world to receive the restored gospel, and Berihun is a great example of this! I hope I can meet him again someday 😁 OK one last miracle from today. On the way back home riding many trains, I realized that the key to my bike wasn't in my pocket like it usually is. If I didn't have it things were going to be so rough when we got back home, considering it's an hour and a half walk to the apartment. I realized this, calmed down, and trusted that God would provide a way. I said a prayer, and afterwards reached right into my backpack and pulled out my key. Super grateful for God's help 🙏 I promise that we are loved and watched over, and that God shows this to us in a variety of ways.WednesdayToday we didn't really have any outside finding time, but we made some good preparations and taught Kids Eikaiwa! Well, I'd like to say that “we” taught it, but my companion ended up taking like a 30 minute phone call in the middle of it so I handled things by myself (OK so we didn't teach but we assisted 😁😂) I am at first super awkward teaching little kids stuff but once I got into it and jumped around and clapped my hands it got better lol. Be as a child and you can teach the children 🔥Thursday🌟Elder Killian deep dive🌟This morning for part of my personal study I watched a video addressed to newly called missionaries, inviting them to think about what kind of a person they want to be when they return home. Now that I'm halfway through my mission, I can't go back and change the first year, but this next year is in my control; I want to make the most of it. Here's a few things I’m going to try and implement for future me:Financial AwarenessPrioritizingGoal SettingDaily Scripture StudyHumilityUnderstanding of Both PartiesUsing Technology EfficientlyGospel ArtAlways Placing Others’ Needs Above My WantsI have already learned so much from a mission, it's crazy to ponder. I feel like I am a much more humble and understanding person than before my mission, I'm so grateful for the experiences that have led me to the “Me” I am today. However, if I feel that I've changed a lot from just 1 year of a mission, I know I'll be surprised by what challenges and growth opportunities I see in these next months. I would absolutely recommend anyone reading this contemplating a mission to think about what they are looking to improve about themselves. While the focus of a mission should be outward, there are just so many things that you can learn about yourself from a mission. The challenges and effort are absolutely worth it. I encourage you to pray about a mission, and seek to know if it is right for you. A couple dreamsI had a pretty freaky dream this Tuesday … on the underside of my right forearm I have an abnormally long strand of peach fuzz, about an inch or two long. Even when I plucked it off once, I'm pretty sure it just grew right back. (This is a real life explanation lol) Anyways, in this dream instead of being a strand of hair, it was my nerves sticking out of my arm. Like all throughout my dream my nerves in my arm kept getting like pushed out, and whenever they brushed against anything it was like hitting your funny bone. I could kind of slide them back into my arm if I was careful, but through the dream they just kept sliding out 💀 very weird and disturbingI had another dream on Thursday night. I remember at one point in this dream I was trying to explain my first dream to someone, which was really weird lol. I think this dream mostly took place in my house. One highlight was that my youngest brother, Kameron, was trying to convince us that we needed to watch like 2½ hours of new Bluey episodes with him. There was also some point in my dream with pokemon cards, and also a pretty big robotic spider, which my mom was controlling. I think my mom might have also been partially a robot or something lol. Interest dreams 😂FridayI've come to a pretty interesting realization. This Week my head has been seriously hurting quite a bit. I've taken medicine and have Been drinking lots of water, but it doesn't seem to be affecting anything very much. I think I have an idea as to what might be wrong though 👀 I'm pretty sure I have a minor concussion 😮 Looking back on things my head has been hurting since that 70 year old guy smacked me in the side of the head, I lowkey think it's from that 😂 We are gonna try and call the medical coordinator tomorrow but a concussion would match up with my symptoms I think. Besides this interesting revelation, we were able to meet at the Okada family's house and practice music! This time we walked up to the second floor, and into a super cool room filled with all kinds of cool music stuff. We were able to practice our songs for the concert together, it was super fun! I even got to hook my guitar up to a big Amp 😎😎😎😎🎸🎸🎸🎸🎸🎸⚡️⚡️⚡️⚡️⚡️It was awesome. I held back from playing some hard rock stuff but It would have sounded so cool 😭😂SaturdayToday was also a bit slow. My head was really hurting today, but we were able to talk with the mission medical coordinator. His name is Elder Brown, and he's a riot. He doesn't think I have a serious concussion since I'm not throwing up and my vision isn't impaired, but he thinks it might be a minor concussion. If things get any worse I'm supposed to go to a clinic. For now I’m just gonna keep trying medicine and hanging in there. Today we actually had a really cool experience giving a priesthood blessing for the sick and afflicted to a non member. There's a lady named Tsukiyama san and she has a broken leg. It makes it really hard for her to run her store, and Hattori会長 suggested we visit her together. Nakazawa長老 had the awesome idea to ask her if we could give her a blessing. She actually agreed when asked by Hattori会長! We were able to go to her store, and we blessed her to be able to heal quickly and according to her faith. It was a really neat experience. I made an art letter for her and Nakazawa長老 made some Origami. She was super grateful, and reassured by the blessing! aside from that, there wasn't anything big that stood out from today besides getting some ice cream from a drugstore 😎🆒️SundayLast night I dreamed that I was playing piano over at my grandma's house and ended up startling her. She was happy to see me though! I was on a video call with my mom and my aunt at the same time too. They wanted me to show them grandma, so I panned around my phone to show them the room and she had left so they couldn't see her, and I commented saying she was a “part-time” grandma lol. I thought it was pretty funny. At some other point in my dream I was viewing my mom's video call and she was fighting off a bunch of feral raccoons in a backyard, it was pretty awesome lol. One other odd quote from my dream too. There was some father in the dream (not my father) and he was talking about his American kids saying that even though they know only English, that when he whispers about then in Japanese they can tell what he says like it was English. Something like that. I also thought it was pretty funny in the dream 😅😂As for the real life side of things, this Sunday is pretty crazy already as I'm writing things. We got up and used our morning mostly for getting ready for zone conference, and then went to church, prepared to leave soon after. Seems like the curse of “companions with no sense of direction” is back again this transfer 😭😂. We need to travel all the way to Yonago For our Zone Conference, but we ended up being on the wrong platform and missed our train, which only sets us back about 3 HOURS 💀 lol. Waiting 3 hours in the semi-heat at a station is no fun. I could probably get mad at my companion and it would be valid, but it's already in the past, so no use making a big deal about things 🙃 it is a long time to wait though, not mentioning the 4+ hours of travel we have afterwards. It's about using this time as efficiently as we can. We'll see how it goes 😅😂Poetry season~~~~If it were youWaiting 3 hours for a trainIf it were you, you might burst a veinAlas, I am humble, I am niceIf it were you, you wouldn't think twice 😜Universally sillyA fascinating thing that I have noticed, from all my time in JapanIs that language don't matter, comedy will shatterAnd laughter affects every man.I've made but a few thousand puns, and yet, I know I can gladly smileWhen, my jokes, others hear, and they grin ear to earIt will raise up my soul for a while.HaikusThe train is comingPlease stand back, behind the lineThank you for your time“Take a chip to yourTooth,” just what does that entail?Does a cookie count?The hard edge of aBuilding, against the blue skyThat is what I spyClouds are pretty weirdLike, how does that water just,Chill around up there??動物は「ワンワン」以外うるさいなお花火を見たいって言う忙しい土星の環が元妻のものだった？I hope that my poetry was bearable 😂Here's an update on our Sunday travels. We managed to catch a different train after waiting only around an hour and a half or so, but waiting an hour at one station, and being on another long train… We're not gonna be able to make it For our interviews with Sano会長 😢 We will just have to wait for tomorrow. However, while waiting we struck up a conversation with a guy, and he was nice enough to sit down and talk with us for like 3 hours on our train ride to Yonago! His favorite band is Man With a Mission, it's also one of my favorites! His name is Nagaoka San. He's pretty chill, and we exchanged our LINE with him. Hopefully he gets a chance to meet up with Missionaries in the future!!!Tonight we got to spend the night at the Zone Leaders’ apartment (Beloved Dietrich長老 and Lao長老) and the two of them were nice enough to give us some curry. I ate mine with a massive spoon 🥄 Lao長老 is super musically talented and was even nice enough to teach me a bit about chords, so I can level up my guitar skills. Super cool companionship, love both of them so much! We have an exchange with them next week so it'll be lit! We are definitely gonna play Magic the Gathering too 😎Zone Conference (Monday)Today was a pretty unique zone conference experience! It was kind of a youth missionary training experience, and at the start we got assigned individually to about 2-3 youth to be their companions for the day. I ended up with Elder Kenshin, Elder Inoue, and Elder Matsumoto. (Not really Elders haha) the 3 of them were really neat, but I only got a picture with Elder Inoue at the end 😭 We had a bunch of cool activities and Role Play lessons. It was interesting to think about how I would bear my testimony and teach a lesson when I was 15 or so. It would be hard. Elder Matsumoto I think was around 15 or 16 and it was his responsibility to do a lot of sharing and teaching. He's a bit of a quiet guy but he hung in there and gave it his best. I tried to encourage him, and at the end I shared in front of everyone how he really brought the spirit to our lesson. I hope that he grows up to be an awesome Missionary, he sure is now 😁 I had my interview with Sano会長 today which was fun. I asked him what his favorite dessert was and one of the foods he said was his wife's lemon scones. He seemed surprised I haven't tried any, but I still haven't really served all that close to Kobe yet 😂 I asked Sano姉妹 about it after the interview and I'm pretty sure she wrote down my name, she's definitely gonna try and get me some lemon scones 😂🍋 Today I got two awesome packages from my family. A new spikeball net!!! (I will not be entrusting this to any other Elders lol) and a bunch of snacks and notes and fun things 🙃 I'm so grateful to have such a caring family. I'm also grateful for you! Yes, you, whichever one of my friends or neighbors or family you are. I love you!After zone conference we got geared up to travel back home. Seems like it's gonna be a long ways, and pretty expensive. Hopefully we don't have any missed train incidents this time around 😭😂ConclusionThis week has been pretty crazy. It's been super fun though. I've also had a good chance to write my thoughts and evaluate things. I hope that we can have more finding time this next week, but it's also been important strengthening the members that live here as much as we can. I know that God loves each of us, and that Jesus Christ loves us too. He is our Savior! Isn't that a huge miracle?! One last miracle is that I have people like you reading my email. I'm so blessed to have met so many amazing people in my short 20 years I've been alive. Thank you for all that you mean to and have done for me. Love you 💜!Elder KillianKaden.killian@missionary.org
summary: Arrived Japan, met funny investigator with "ah_genkidaze" email, grateful for divine interventions today.

subject: Dead bug
body: Good Morning! And welcome to my email 😉 This week we did a bunch of cool stuff, so let's jump in! Tuesday (Pday)Today was a pretty good Pday, lets jump in. Today I got to talk with my family which was awesome! We played a word game called Boggle together, and shared some laughs and smiles. I am so grateful for such an awesome family! After wrapping up the call, Elder Nakazawa長老 and I headed off to find some sort of elbow pads for him so he can practice better with his nunchucks 😂😁 The first store we looked at didn't have anything that would work, but Nakazawa長老 did manage to find a new pair of earbuds haha. Next we stopped by a fried chicken shop to get some lunch. It was super tasty, I got some chicken with grated plum on top 🔥 I even got a few pickles on the side, all different kinds. One of them was like a dill pickle I think! I love dill pickles but I can’t seem to find many in Japan. Anyways it was great! Next we went back to the toy store, and I asked if they had a specific card game in stock, which they did not. The owner was nice enough to look it up for me, and told me to keep checking in and it might be there soon. In the meantime I bought a couple packs of Magic the Gathering cards, nothing was super good for what I'm trying to make currently, but it's no problem. I guess besides that it was a pretty uninteresting Pday I guess lol 😂 That night we did have Eikaiwa, so we prepped a bit beforehand. I ended up teaching a lot about school and some associated vocabulary; it was pretty fun talking with Katayama san and Keiko san. They are both in the 50-80 age range, but are both really good at speaking in English. It's pretty fun teaching them both! Afterwards I shared a spiritual message about how even though we are surrounded by millions of people, each of us are special, loved, and recognized by our Heavenly Father. I felt the Spirit as I shared my testimony. WednesdayToday we were able to print off some flyers for the music concert in a little while, and we passed them out for maybe an hour near the end of the day. We were hoping to do it earlier but there was like nobody on the streets. Veeeery hot. We also tried to stop by a less active members house but it ended up being a completely different members house, her name is Morimoto姉妹。It was pretty surprising having her open the door and seeing her standing there. She invited us in, and gave us some super fancy herb enhanced water. I ended up being able to talk with her about some herbs and stuff and told her about how my grandma had mint leaves in her backyard, and that it was nostalgic. She also has a ton of Doterra essential oils that I saw on a shelf and enjoyed talking with her a bit about them, and how my mom loves them. It was really nice to talk, and she expressed gratitude for our help with the kids Eikaiwa class last week. She's been very stressed about it, and didn't really have a say in being in charge of it from what it sounds like. She said because nobody is forcing her to do it, she's been thinking to herself about how she feels it's kind of useless and when she should stop it. She thinks that through our help it can continue to go well, and she's encouraged! We shared a scripture with her before we left, and offered to come back and help weed around Her house the next day! So grateful!ThursdayToday we attempted for go visit and give a blessing to Koumoto兄弟 who suddenly got put in the hospital for dehydration, but when We got there (Hattori会長 was with us too) the people at the front desk said if we aren't his family we can't visit him. Bummer, but afterwards we were able to drive and visit an inactive member who's coming to our music night. He was really nice and even knows some pretty solid English. He might end up coming to our Eikaiwa again in the future! Later that day we went over to Morimoto姉妹’s house to do some weeding. It's been a long time since I've helped anyone weed, it reminded me of helping in my family's backyard garden. It was super hot! Morimoto姉妹 also showed us All of the cool herbs she is growing, she definitely had a handful I recognize. After we finished weeding (just for the day, she wants us to come back) she gave us a super pretty salad, which was actually delicious. Like definitely up there with my mom's Thai Chicken Salad. She also sent us away with two frozen pizzas and some furugura which was appreciated!! After that we headed straight for the community center for my Jkaiwa class. This time there was a teacher I'd never met before, who is about the same age as my parents. There was also a guy named Sou from Korea, but his Japanese is native level. We essentially just had a fun conversation about all kinds of random things. Sou mocked Fujita san (the teacher) about his lack of music knowledge and for enjoying the sound of his tires and the engine when he drives, it was hilarious 😂 we were even able to talk a bit about missionary life, and I shared how my best friend Mason is serving in Korea. Both Sou and Fujita san seem to think we're working pretty hard, it was nice. When we got home we looked again for frogs, then got ready for bed. I don't know if this was today or another day, but we were able to play Magic the Gathering again together one night. Anyways I had a pretty fast start compared to Nakazawa長老, and could have won the game a bunch in the beginning but just didn't attack So that he could still try and do things. Anyways, he ended up destroying everything that was in play (sending It all to the trash) and immediately after used a card to play everything that just Got trashed under his control 😂 it was insane 😭😂FridayToday was a big day of planning and practicing. We started off the day with a pretty short Zoom meeting for the Media Team, and had our language study afterwards. We got lunch at the convenience store by the church, and I ate a sandwich that randomly tasted like soft tacos. Later we met with Hattori会長、Nishiokaさん、and Kawai兄弟 for singing practice. It ended up going on for like 2 hours because people showed up late but it was good. Ok so when Kawai兄弟 first arrived, I'd never met him before. I think he's a pretty inactive member, or maybe busy. Anyways he's sung at Carnegie Hall before, he's big about singing. OK, so he walks in the door, and Hattori会長 busts out his harmonica, and they perform a song called Santa Lucia, And let me tell you that Kawai兄弟 probably has the loudest voice I've ever heard anyone use singing. It was a crazy experience, some pretty awesome vibrato. He's also like one of the only people I've met in Japan with a full beard. A couple hours later we had game night, and we played Uno and spikeball (just Me and my companion, everyone else was too old so they just enjoyed watching 😂). Uno definitely had some funny moments. I have had a few crazy starting hands the last couple times I've played, I’ll attach a few pics. At the end I shared a past experience and testimony, which I will do my best to write here:When I was a kid, I loved playing soccer. My favorite part to play was the goalie, mostly because I got to touch the ball with my hands, drop kick it as far as I could, and didn't have to run around as much 😆 Anyways, there was one day where I was pretty spaced out, and while (I thought) my team was on the other side of the field I was crouched down, picking dandelions or eating some grass, when suddenly ai heard some shouting and… WHOOSH! The ball Was kicked straight into the goal! I didn’t even see the other team coming! In our lives we all have different chances or opportunities that are placed in our path by a loving God. However, if we're Sitting around eating grass, we aren't going to even recognize the chances before they go past us! I would like to invite whoever is reading this to look for the opportunities that might even be right in front of your face; I'm sure that everyone has different opportunities at different times, but a few good examples could be attending church, reading the scriptures, daily prayer, lending your time or hand to someone else in need. I challenge you to look at things from an eternal perspective! I promise that as you make That effort you will be happier and have an increase of peace and reassurance day to day. 😊 In the name of Jesus Christ, Amen!SaturdayToday we started off the day by heading to a library to volunteer reading English books for kids. Really simple picture books, but it was fun. There was a nice lady there in charge named Oobayashiさん and she was impressed my paper airplane making skills lol. I actually came up with a new/modified design on the spot, it was pretty cool. For reference there were some numbered stickers on the ground along the main entrance of the library, and Oobayashiさん explained they were for marking a paper airplane competition lol. Anyways she was pretty blown away by the paper airplane I made, and says gonna use it herself in the competition later today lol. We then stopped by an art exhibition (while doing our language study) and talked with a lady who owns an art store that we might be able to help volunteer at! Super cool! Afterwards we got some super tasty Korean Fried Chicken (KFC) ?? 😂 and glanced at some kind of samurai movie that was playing from a projector in the store lol. Nakamura長老 seemed to think it was pretty lit. Later we did A bit of music Practice and kind of fell into a slump of Facebook finding at the church. We did manage to wrap up the day with a solid conclusion to our SKK (週間計画会), however. One random thing from today was we went to a drugstore to get some snacks to give to a member, and I've Noticed one of the cashiers the past few times we've been. He's super tall, probably in his late 40s or 50s, and I've noticed him getting trained on how to run the front register. I think it's super cute that this big giant that's probably older (and definitely much taller) than the other workers is getting instructed in such a humble situation. He's really nice, and today he was the one who checked out me and my companion for our snacks. While I was waiting for my companion, I said a prayer, asking that we'd get a chance to meet with him outside of his work environment. I hope that we can run into him sometime! He seems like he could be our Friend!SundayToday after church we had some watermelon, I surprised everyone by eating the rind along with the rest of the watermelon. I had to tell the members that it isn't an American thing, but rather that I'm just weird 😂 While we were eating, I placed a dead cicada on Nakazawa長老’s arm and he got so scared 😂 I didn't think he'd be bothered because he loves bugs And animals and stuff but afterwards he told me the thing that creeps Him out the most is dead bugs lol 😂 Later we went to go to the Okada Family's house and practiced music with Matsuokaさん, Hattori会長 and Nishiokaさん。It was pretty good, and I only almost fell asleep once 👍😂 Fun fact I thought all of the Okada family were members but none of them are 😱😱😂 Okada姉妹/さん apparently just attends church because we need someone to play the piano for Sacrament Meeting, and there's nobody else who can do it. Their Whole family is so sweet and friendly with the members though, I had just figured they were part of the Branch 😆 When we got back to the church we had some studies, and before heading home we put a bunch of flyers in people's mailbox; our musical event is in less than a week! Really hoping we can get a few more people to show up! While we were walking back to the church to get our Bikes, we said hi to an old lady walking with a flashlight. She was holding a trashbag and trying to figure out where to throw out her garbage, and I guess she thought we were garbage men because she was asking us all these questions about it lol. We weren't able to give her any flyer or invitation which kinda stinks, but I am hoping we can meet her again, she was pretty friendly!💭Dreams💭Had a dream that someone said I was their other half Dreamed that I had an electric scooter And did a cool trick with it and surprised my friend, and somehow my DL was also involved in my dreamDreamed about a bunch of weird stuff but I only remember there was a floating butterfly like a balloon, but the string for the balloon was spiders silk, and it was attached to the tip of my shoe. I also performed on the electric guitar for a big crowd, it was kind of awesome. I think I get the hype now 🔥📝Poetry📝I love my fam’lyThey are all so special toMe. I'm so grateful.My mind, it wandersI think I'm pretty focusedBut still. Hahaha.Salty rice with egg onTop of the world when I eatchicken invaders 🐥🐤🐣桜の子草から飛んで木になった秋の意味ってなんだろうわっからんTake the edge of thePaper, and hold it overThe flame. Burn it, All.ウンチってうんこでしょう違いって？💩She's purple latelyOr maybe she's always beenHard to say for sure 🟪I find myself turning while I try to sleepTurning into the kind of person that the present "me” wants to beAs long as that “me” is the one that God, you, and my Mom want, I'm probably OKBut I don't know if that's who the present “me” is trying to beIf I try too hard to change to be what the present “me” views as important, or coolVisuallyPhysicallyEmotionallyWhat if I turn into someone elseGood thing this is just poetryIf not, maybe there'd be cause for worryIt's only a half-way concernEven if that's not what you discern 👽I hope you've enjoyed my poetry ✨️ and my email in general. It's always fun to write, and I hope it can be inspiring, silly, or a light in anyone's lives. I love you. I really do! You are each valued and esteemed by yours truly 🙌 I wish I could do a better job at showing you each personally, it's really hard, especially when mostly everyone reading this lives back near my hometown. I will try and write responses when I get the chance. I love reading all of your mail, your messages inspire me to keep going. I love you! Thank you!Love from the loving and ✨️lovely✨️ beloved Elder KillianHahaha lowkey scariest picture I've ever taken of myself 😭😭😂😂😂 POV me sending out this email
summary: Arrived Japan, talked with family via Boggle, found earbuds, bought Magic cards, taught Eikaiwa lesson.

subject: It's a new Dawn, it's a new Day, it's a new Life
body:
And I'm feeling good 😎

NOTE I've actually been pretty good about writing every day so it's a pretty long one this time. Some of you will be grateful 💜 and some of you will probably not be 😂 but either way- I hope you can take something meaningful away from my experiences in Japan!

Pday Today we did our usual rounds at the game shop, and the owner let me know that there are people that get together to play Magic the Gathering at a community center, the same one I've been taking Japanese classes at! I think it might be a good chance to talk with some hopefully somewhat younger people which we have not seen many of so far. This reminds me of something interesting that I've noticed from my experiences in Japan. With any sort of Gospel or even Eikaiwa approach, trying to initiate conversation with strangers is not impossible, but very improbable. Most people that I've tried to talk with will turn me away, with no sympathy. However; if there is even a small conversation between the two of us, the ice seems to melt right away. It's still likely that they won't be interested in what I'm trying to offer them, but they are much more warm and understanding. I think that this might be a good opportunity to put myself into a scenario where conversation is implied, and even a small relationship can be formed through a simple interest. We will see if this merits any success! I'll definitely write about it if anything notable takes place 😁 Anyways, back to Pday! We went back to the art store to check out some markers for Nakazawa長老, and I was able to talk a bit more with the owner of the art store. I want to do something nice for her, but I'm not sure what. I will try and make some art to give her the next time we see her! We had lunch At KFC (very disappointed with How few French fries I got) and did some shopping which was lowkey kinda draining. I'm pretty exhausted From this last week. I’m hoping I feel more energized tomorrow. Another fun thing was I found an Aerosmith CD with Like a bunch of their biggest hits and it was 110 yen which was liiiiit! Gonna try and continue my streak of only buying cheap CDs 🔥🔥 Still hoping to find more Sukimaswitch CDs while I'm here in Japan. My favorite song by Sukimaswitch is probably “Genome.” Even if you don't understand Japanese, no worries! Back in America when I listened to it I didn't either 😂😂 Later tonight we were hoping to have our family home evening activity at the church, but nobody showed up! 😭 We decided after 30 minutes of waiting to go to the Conbini to print out more flyers to use this week, and when we got back to the church a car was there!! It was Nishioka San! We went and greeted her and she'd brought some Chicken Katsu for us to eat together, but it was super awkward because we aren't allowed to meet with women with just the 3 of us, and nobody else could show up. She ended up just leaving us with the food, and in exchange we sent her away with a bag of some snacks. It was super sweet of her, but it sure stinks we couldn't have a lesson with her! The food she made was super good too 👌 pretty wacky end to the Pday, but a good day.

Tuesday Last night I had some weird dreams. There is like a super heavy vibe attached to them even though I can't remember any of what happened. BTW this was after I woke up, did some studies, them slept for like 30 minutes. This morning we had DCM (District Council meeting) which was pretty fun. It was super hard to hear because besides us everyone was in person, so the audio from the video call was really choppy. We still had a good time though, and when we had our Mogis (roleplay scenarios) we broke out into 1 on 1 video calls so that was nice at least. It's weird being a somewhat old missionary now; I am the person being taught in Mogis mostly now. Kind of nice ngl 😂 I just remembered! When we got to the church for DCM, we realized that Nakazawa長老 left the keys to the church back at our apartment, so we had to go back and get them, we ended up being a bit late 😂 Another instance where I probably could have been ticked off (especially in like 100°F weather lol) but I think I've done a pretty good job at just letting things go lately. If I think about it, getting angry will only make me feel bad and my companion feel worse about forgetting something. There's no need to get mad over little things, and sometimes even big Things! 😁 After a lovely DCM, we made some lunch at the apartment, I cooked up probably the best Fried Rice I've ever made but I got it all over the stove and floor 😂 It was pretty good 😆 Still want to be better at making food 🍳 After lunch, we had a bit of study time, then biked in the heat to an art store to talk with a lady we met at the art gallery last week. She wasn't there, but we were able to talk with one of her coworkers, who informed us of when we should come back to talk with the other lady. She was super nice, and we were able to show each other all some art that we had made. Pretty wholesome moment! I shared about how I used to work at an art store and taught kids classes there. She seemed pretty impressed, and it seems like we might be able to do some kind of service there! I think the lady from the art gallery wanted me to teach a Copic marker Class, we will see how it goes! They were also nice enough to take a poster for our music concert and hang it up in their shop for us! Artists’ connections run deep 🔻🕳 Later we headed back to the church for Eikaiwa. We were really hoping to have a lesson with Nishioka San beforehand, but all the students were like 20 minutes early and we couldn't just leave them alone 😭 Bad timing but we weren't able to have a lesson. I was able to share a testimony at the end of Eikaiwa though. I talked about how we can show humility through prayer, and how the examples of others (namely my mom!) can help us to follow the right path. BTW in the advanced class with me and Matsuoka San and Katayama san it was mad depressing. I tried to talk about Family reunions and Katayama san wasn't very interested lol. Things ended up going more in the direction of global warming and how humanity is on track to perish 😅 pretty sure Matsuoka san was actually crying, it was brutal. Katayama san wouldn't help me steer the topic to something more positive, he's a big talker. I hope my message at the end shared a bit about peace though. I don't think thar everything is going to be sunshine and rainbows, there's a lot of pretty sad and awful stuff happening all over the world. I do believe that Jesus will come to bring light and a hope that couldn't exist without him though. I know of his love. It amazes me to think of the Love that our Heavenly Father and Jesus Christ feel for each of us. Imagine the person you are closest with, and the love you feel towards them. Then imagine having a love far stronger than that for billions of people, probably more. It sounds exhausting!! 😆😂 I think you would have to be in a Godly state to be able to feel something quite as strong as that amount of love for others, but there is still love that we can feel and share in our imperfect human state! I invite you to give more of your time to others. I have some amazing friends and family 💜 who have shown me such a good example of love towards others. Thank you guys!

Wednesday This morning I woke up at 4:30 and couldn't really get back to sleep until around 7 (after I'm supposed to be up lol) and slept until like 9 again. During those two hours I had some wild dreams. Me and some other people were visiting a sort of info desk in a museum or something, and we kept on leaving and going back for some reason. As a service offered by the place, there were toys and card games we could use, so it was mostly for that I guess lol. There was also somehow Like a dollar store built into the place, and from the store I bought some weird wooden shapes On Keychains and gave them to some tourists (I thought they were pretty boring, literally just like wooden cylinders on Keychain, but they were dying over how cute they were lol), who had me show them where from. In the same area there were more keychains and some plastic bags with a bunch of interesting metal shapes and stuff inside, which were like less than a dollar, which I was impressed by. After a bit more looking in the store I found some Magic the Gathering cards lol. They were super old and there were some super cool ones. I think they were priced individually though, and I did not want to spend that much money on them. I didn't, but I seriously considered just stealing them in my dream lol. Another funny appearance was my old Companion Magbago長老. Apparently the place we were at was having a sort of Magic competition, and I was put on the bracket to battle him. I was super excited, but I just couldn't find my deck anywhere to fight him with. I was actually so frustrated! 😂 I had to calm myself down, and say a prayer, even though it was just in my dream!! 😂😂 In the end I was never actually able to play against him 😢😂 Pretty crazy dream though. Today we were planning to travel to Yonago for exchanges with the ZL's, my beloved Dietrich長老 and Lao長老 🔥 We did a bit of music Practice at the church, then headed off for the station. On the way we stopped a guy who said hi in English! He was super chill, and over the phone told the person on the other end he was talking with a super hot foreigner lol. He gave me a business card for the bar he owns/works at LOL. He does have his number On the card and seems like he'd probably be down to hang out sometime, so hoping that works out. We got to the station, ate a quick Combini lunch, and got on our train… But… it didn't move. An announcement came on that the train Tracks were too hot?! They canceled all trains going where we needed to go for the day it seems like. We were fortunately able to take an alternate route, but it added like 3 hours and 20 bucks to the trip 😭 throughout our travels we weren't able to talk to a bunch of people, but I talked to a guy with a camera, and we were able to talk a little bit with a woman While we waited for a train. She was nice, and was returning from visiting her parents, both in their 90s. We told her we're missionaries, hopefully she has a good opinion of missionaries now as just nice people who are down to talk haha.

Thursday Had some wild dream again last night, I'm On a roll lately. I think that my dreams mostly took place at like a school, but there was definitely some kind of missionary gathering. It was actually like half a school and half a mall. I remember so Many small details so I won't go on about it for too long lol. It is sufficient to say that it was a bunch of random things. Today was our awesome exchange. It was probably the best exchange I’ve ever been on! Gotta give the Credit to Elder Dietrich 🔥 Amazing missionary. We started off the day by traveling over to the church to plan out our day, and join a devotional (zone conference follow up) over zoom. Before lunch, we went out and did like an hour-ish of housing. We weren't able to get any contact information or talk a bunch, but we did have a few small conversations. There was one time we went to a house, and I demonstrated the “flower approach” to Dietrich長老。I have maybe mentioned it in the past, but if someone has beautiful flowers in front of their house, you ring their doorbell and ask if you can take a picture of them. I tried it with one lady, she looked pretty grumpy and seemed cold, and when I asked why I was taking a picture, I answered saying “because they are pretty” and her whole demeanor changed. She said she was busy but she was kind enough to tell me the name of the flowers. It's interesting to see how people change when you try to show interest in something they have worked hard on or have interest in. After our housing (extremely hot BTW, we were drenched after just an hour in the sun) we took a bus to a Barbecue??! There was a group of MamaTomo which means mommy friends haha and somehow the Yonago Elders got invited to join the Barbecue, and it happened to be on exchanges!! Yessssss! 😆😆 We got there before Nakazawa長老 and Lau長老 so we helped prepare some food and got to talk with the Moms, one of their husband's, and a bunch of kids. One of the kids' names is Taro kun, and he was apparently the fastest swimmer in the entire prefecture like a year ago 😱 super cool. He also plays the sax. He reminds me of myself when I was younger, it was fun to talk with him. After some garlic bread got taken out of the oven and we were ready start eating, I asked if I could offer a prayer for all of us. It was super cool, they were totally fine with it! Apparently Taro kun even went to a catholic school when he was a kid, so everyone was a bit familiar with Christian stuff. Either way it was so cool!! After a little while Elder Lau and Nakagawa showed up, and we all talked and ate. I was consistently eating for about an hour and a half or two hours, and by the end I still had some room! I think I'm a growing boy 👦 😂 Before we left we played the blindfolded-try-and-hit-the-watermelon-with-a-plastic-baseball-bat Game. It was pretty fun. After that we even had a watermelon seed spitting competition (just us and the kids) and I smoked them 😂 I got a seed to go like 15 feet, it was awesome. The kids were losing their minds 😂 After that, Dietrich長老 and I headed off to go do some streeting! It was pretty fun, but we weren't able to make Any super bold invitations unfortunately. We talked with a bunch of people though! It was around 1½-2 hours, was Very hot. Some highlights: A guy talked with us for almost 10 minutes, he doesn't live close but we talked about our cultures and he thought it was super cool how we can learn from each other's experiences and stuff. He said he'd be down to talk again sometime, but he didn't want to exchange contact info, and even gave Dietrich長老 a wink 😂 Talked With a guy whose friend went to BYU Idaho and stayed with members of our church! Talked with a guy who wanted us to join him for dinner right then!! It would have been so cool but we had a lesson scheduled right after 😭 On to the lesson! We had a lesson with a member and a friend called Fumi San. We were hoping to talk more about the spirit and re-invite her to baptism, but we ended up talking about her work drama for around an hour and the lesson ended up being mostly about prioritizing Sundays. It was really good, but it went on for a looooong time. By the way during our contacting earlier today, I calked up the guy who gave me a business card the day before. BEST PHONE CALL EVER! Bro was super excited that I called him back and we talked like best friends for like 15 minutes. I told him I'm a volunteer for my church, and he didn't really mind. He wants to meet up as soon as we can to get some food and talk!! So awesome. He's really nice, and seems like he might be willing to listen to some spiritual stuff too as long as its from an attractive foreigner like me 😏😂 To end off the night, Elder Lau played me in Magic with his proxy deck and I proooobably could Have won if I was smart but things got out of hand and he ended up winning. No boardwipes to save me that time 😢 Good good day.

Friday Crazier dreams last night than the night before! In my dream I was in some kind of sacrament meeting, and I was for sone reason messing around with my guitar Amp and some wires (I wasn't being loud btw) and one of the speakers noticed and started saying how I was extremely disrespectful, and an embarrassment. All in front of everyone! It was suuuuper awkward! I felt like everyone there hated me. I was thinking of how to respond to her attack, but I think I woke up before I got a chance to stick up for myself. It was pretty crazy 😂 definitely stressful. We were able to ride our trains fine today fortunately, but on our last train home (there's an awesome song called Last Train Home by Pat Metheny BTW go listen to it lol) I realized I had the key to Lau長老’s bike. Hopefully they can figure out a way to make it work because we definitely can't afford to go back, nor do we have the time 😅 UPDATE so It turns out Dietrich長老 messaged me and said that they had to carry the bike like 30 minutes to a bike shop to get the lock cut 😂😂 Pretty inconvenient I can only imagine 😂 I told Dietrich長老 to tell Lau長老 he deserves it for trashing me in the card game last night 😆 I'll Attach a photo of Lau長老 carrying his bike 🚲 Anyways, on our way back home we stopped by the community center again to ask about any card game events people have, and the guy at the desk very hesitantly told me they had an event on the 17th from 10AM. Sunday right when church starts 😂 so that's unfortunately a nope. Maybe I'll ask the worker at the toy store if he can set us up with someone to play Magic with next time we visit. On our way back To the apartment, we stopped at a splash pad and I lightly soaked myself in water, and Nakazawa長老 did less lol. It was kind of awkward walking up to the place because it was all just little kids running around. I bet that I definitely made some of them a bit weirded out 😅😂 After we got back, we called the ZLs to do a review of our exchange. Dietrich長老 and I finished in like half an hour, then sent a few messages to Tanaka san, the guy who I called yesterday. He said that with the start of the new month, he was going to the shrine to pray to have a successful next month at his store. He said we should totally join him, and that he was leaving right now! I told him We’d meet him over at the shrine, but my Companion and Lau長老 took like 30 minutes longer for their Review, it was kinda stressful, but I bet they talked about important stuff. Anyways, after it was over, we headed over to the shrine, and met him and his super nice Lexus in front of the shrine! He was also wearing a solid gold necklace with the queen of England and a horse on it. Pretty boujie. He was Super happy to see me, and I got to properly introduce my companion (they hadn't really talked up until then). We found out he's 33 years old, and the whole time was on the phone with his girlfriend?? Almost girlfriend?? Idk but she had plenty of things to say about the cool tall foreigner lol. I feel like most of this interaction was Tanaka san and his maybe girlfriend just geeking out over me, Nakazawa even joined in too 😭😂 I got a few cool pictures at the shrine, I'll try and attach one or two. After we left the shrine, we talked a bit by Tanaay's car and I even had him try a Sour Patch Kid or two, which he loved. He remembered that when he went to Hawaii as a kid he actually tried the candy for the first time, and just forgot about it! It was super fun talking, and he wants to meet up for lunch with us too. Hopefully we will get a chance to introduce a gospel principle next week! Tonight we had Game night, it was pretty fun. We played Uno as usual, and there was a pretty awesome moment. There's a person there named Minori, I think they might be a guy but just dress like a girl, unsure. Anyways whenever Minori plays a card Like a draw two or a draw 4 she always makes this big inhaling sound and slowly plays the card down from above our heads down onto the pile. Super dramatic lol. Anyways Minori played a few draw 2 and draw 4 cards this one game, and only had one card left when Nakazawa長老 played a draw 4 on her the same way, with the inhaling sound and everything 😂😂😂😂😂😂 It was actually so funny, Minori san couldn't get over it either 😂 I was almost in tears it was so funny. I also tried playing a bit of Boggle with Matsuoka San and Minori san while the other 3 people were in an uno game, but we had a hard time seeing the letters and I kind of just destroyed them as expected 😅 It was fun to try though lol. Next time we have Eikaiwa Matsuoka san said she's gonna bring her Boggle to try together. I'm looking forward to it!

Saturday Today was a pretty crazy day! In the morning we biked again to the art shop we went to before, and we were able to talk with the lady we met at the art gallery, Obayashi san. We even exchanged LINE so that we can coordinate a service opportunity. We had some more awesome art conversations, and I was able to give the store some good advice about art supplies, because of my background working at Artist Corner. It was so fun! Tonight we had our music concert, which was pretty awesome! There were I think 27 people total. My and Nakazawa長老’s performance didn't go great but it was OK. If I had focused more on the guitar it would have probably sounded better overall, but I tried to sing nice (and still messed up). It's over now, so it's ok 👍😂 The Pashmina group sang some pretty awesome songs. I was slightly worried about how it would go but it was solid! Afterwards we all ate a bunch of snacks (including My homemade caramel on ice cream) which was super fun. I Also found out I love Ebi Senbei (shrimp crackers) dipped in ice cream 😋 Salty and sweet can't be beat 🔥

Sunday Last night was some wild dreams again. One highlight was that I had some kind of interesting power that if I touched something, I could create a clone of it, which would disappear after a little time, but I could still like interact with the clones. I was using it to make clones pf frisbees so when I thew them they split into two. It was pretty cool! I also could like do it with a playing card, rip the playing card clone into pieces, then after some time it would disappear. Anyways, today's been a good Sunday. We had church, and I bore my testimony About the importance of ordinances like the sacrament, and how we can focus on the Savior through it. I also shared a scripture from Moroni Chapter 4, it's verse 2. “And they did kneel down with the church, and pray to the Father in the name of Christ.” This is right before the Sacrament Blessings are written, and I never noticed that the whole church would kneel together. We don't do that in our church now, probably out of consideration of people's knees haha, but I can imagine that the whole church would feel so united kneeling together. After church we had DCS, and talked for a while with Hattori会長 about missionary work, then headed back to our apartment.

It's been a good week. Time seems to fly by lately. I already know my Mission is gonna be over before I know it 😅😢 I'm working hard to make it the best I can. I'm still not perfect.
