But I'm determined to give it my best. I love you all! Even if I write a bunch of stuff I'm certain I'll have more to share when I come home! There's some stuff you just can't convey in a letter. See you soon 🙃

Love you!! 🫶💜🔥😁

Elder Killian
summary: Noted daily journal entry: Met Magic Gathering players, learned about Japanese culture, planned gift for store owner. Feeling drained but hopeful.

subject: Honey Butter 🧈🍯
body:
This week I made us some French toast, and we ate it with Honeybutter から I titled my email after it! (English-Japanese Grammar Fusion 😎💣)

August 4th partly cloudy ⛅️

Today was a pretty fun Pday! Here's a little rundown of what we did. As soon as I woke up in the morning, I took a quick shower and called up my family earlier than usual from our apartment, so that I could play Magic the Gathering over video call with Dobberfuhl長老. It was super fun to talk with my family (as always 😚) and it was really fun getting to play some Magic too. Elder Dobberfuhl beat me the first game, but I managed to win the second time. It was great! After that, Nakazawa長老 and I headed to Cafe Gusto for a lunch of pizza and Ice cream (for Me) lol. We also stopped by the toy store as usual, and I got a pretty cool new Magic card for my deck! We also got to chat with the lady working at the front desk for a while, she’s really nice! Doesn't seem like she has much interest, but maybe we just need to keep talking with her and praying that she'll feel prompted to meet with us! Next we stopped by a home improvement store for supplies To upgrade Nakazawa長老’s nunchucks. Now they are attached with a real chain rather than just a faux leather cord. Lastly we got some groceries, (mostly to make food for our family home evening activity that night) and while we were at the store I tried out a fun little gumball machine where you control a platform and guide a gumball up a ramp: if you make it to the top the machine gives you two or 3 pieces of gum instead of just the one! I did poorly, and Nakazawa長老 gave it a whirl. He got the gumball to the top! His gumball went into a special hole, and out popped- 1 piece of gum 😂😂😂 All of his meticulous control of the machine and it didn't give him more than he would've gotten if his gumball fell off the platform immediately lol. I thought it was hilarious. After that we headed over to the church to wind down and get some food ready for the family home evening activity. We were in over our heads. Koumoto兄弟 doesn't know when to stop talking 😂. It was a great lesson With Him and Nishioka san, but it ended up going until like 10:00 PM 😢 We need to try and politely address him about how we can try and do things more efficiently next time 😅 All in all, a pretty good Pday.

August 5th rainy afternoon 🌧

Today was awesome! I feel like today we made a big step as far as finding time (and protecting finding time) goes. We started off the day with DCM, and I gave a training about how it's important as missionaries to teach for understanding. I shared about my experiences teaching Nao and Mai with Sonomura長老, and how it's important we break things down into manageable chunks depending on the friends level of comprehension. I also expressed the importance of our friends’ agency, and how even if we teach perfectly, it's still up for them to let our message into their hearts, and work in them. It was pretty nice. Afterwards we headed out to do some housing, for around 2 ish hours. It was pretty dry as far as responses go, but it felt great to be out doing the work! We were able to get out a lot of flyers and cards, so it was a success even if we couldn't see it in the moment. Later we had studies, Media Dendou, and lastly Eikaiwa! It was a really fun Eikaiwa tonight, and Katayama san, Matsuoka San and I even played some Boggle together! I really love those two. Afterwards Nakazawa長老 shared a spiritual Message about the importance of prayer (It's super important!!! Every day!!!) And we talked and enjoyed some snacks all together. I told everyone about how I love looking for CDs and got a few tips as to where I might look. All in all, super great day 😊

August 6th Mostly Cloudy ☁️🌥

Dream: Tonight I had some crazy dreams that mostly took place in some Kind of store. In the front area of the store there was a huge squid that was kind of encased in the wall and was the power source for something, it was a squid engine. Another interesting thing was that there was a game console, and my mom somehow destroyed it, and like threw it into the ocean as well lol. She was showing me some tiny game consoles that she found, and a tiny one that fit in a small backpack that Elder Dobberfuhl made. There was also in the back of the store some construction that went on, and looking at some past surveillance footage, the workers had to spend hours and hours stacking up purple hangers.

Very weird dream lol, there was a bunch of other stuff including caves and spiders but it's all so disconnected its kind of a mess to write it lol. Also I feel like everything lately is turning purple 🟪 😂

Today was a good day! After leaving the apartment today, we went out and did a solid hour of housing in the morning. It's refreshing to have a streak of good finding time each day, I'm hoping we can keep up the momentum for tomorrow as well! We weren't able to see any huge success with our housing, but we were out and doing the Lord’s work, which is what really counts! In the afternoon (after some stew courtesy of Nakazawa長老) I was able to call my mom and spend some time chatting with her because it was her birthday! If you remember, I mentioned in the MTC how it was my mom's birthday and I completely forgot, well… Let's just say that if she didn't send me a message about how she was enjoying her birthday, I have realized much too late... 😵 I can't believe I didn't learn my lesson from last time 😢 later today we headed to the church to help Morimoto姉妹 with Kodomo Eikaiwa (kids english conversation!) This time there were two new and naughty boys who joined the crowd. It was good, but exhausting, and I'm sure Morimoto姉妹 felt it more than we did 😅 later we did some Media finding, and headed to bed. The days really do go by quickly ⏳️🪽

August 7th Rainy Morning 🌧

Dreamed that I hid in a bathtub and scared my elementary school friend, dreamed about being at my great grandparents house, and how I defended my sister from my grandpa

Today we had a great mix of missionary work, and something actually crazy happened 😱😱😱😱

I started off the day by accidentally bonking my head on an overhang in our apartment, which was nice 🙃 And after some studies we set out to do some streeting by a small college. Heres where it begins. We are biking around the college borders, looking for some people to talk with, and suddenly Nakazawa長老 realized that right now it's summer vacation, so like nobody would be here. We Press forward regardless, and a minute Of riding out bikes later, we stop in front of a bike parking lot abd talk to two girls that were walking by. [Background: Since the start of this week, we've been watching bits and pieces of a film by our church called “Meet the Mormons” during our companion study. While watching it, I had the idea to try a streeting approach in which we ask people what they think about Christians, kind of a short survey style question. Back to the story!!] We asked them what they thought about Christians, and they said Christmas is the first thing that comes to mind. It kind of tapered off and felt a bit forced but as the two left, a curly haired guy pulled up, and dapped me up as I was wrapping up our conversation with the two girls. By the way, as this is happening there's another guy staring at us from his car, kind of circling us a bit and smiling at the curly haired guy. Super weird lol. Anyways, we talk with Curly and ask what he thinks about Christians. He was super open with us, and said he wasn't opposed to joining a church, but he didn't really have any reason to, as he doesn't know much about church in general. Then the guy who circled us before pulled up and joined in our conversation. They were both insanely chill, probably partly cause they're both our age. Anyways, they asked if we're asking a lot of people the survey, and we said yes. Then… they pretty much walked us inside of the university right into the middle of the main study hall with like 100+ college students all preparing for a test 😱😱😱 They pretty much told us to go around table to table and ask everyone the questions, they were insanely supportive and wanted to help us out. Everyone was staring at us from the second we walked I'm, so it was hard to be calm lol. We talked with like 30 different people about what they know about Christians, almost nobody knew much about Christians, and nobody had seen missionaries like us before. The two that brought us in also took a bunch of our flyers and cards and passed them out to everyone, get these two missionary nametags already 😂😂😂 After some time they kinda escorted us out in a hurry, I think their test was about to start. After we were outside, we even got to talk with one of their professors! He was also super nice and talked with us, it was incredible. In the end we didn't get Either of the Two’s contact info which SUUUUUCKS but they have our number, and hopefully we can see them again by streeting near the university again! Now a bunch of young people know our faces, and everyone we talked to seemed to like us and have some level of interest in even just talking with us. It was insane. Still can't get over this. The two guys also took a BeReal with us and a bunch of photos and videos, I need to meet them again if not just to get the vids lol 😂 Later today we got some more flyers printed and passed them out for around an hour while housing. One thing that stuck out was one nice lady. We knocked on her door, and as she opened it up, a huge wasp flew inside. She managed to smack it back outside with her hand, but she was super sweet and even asked where I'm from. (It's like super rare to have someone ask a question to us while housing btw) She didn't have any interest in Eikaiwa, Game Night, Or the Gospel, but she was very sweet. She even popped her head out the window as we left to inform us that her neighbor wasn't home, so they wouldn't answer the door. Super sweet! Afterwards we studied the Book of Mormon at the church a little bit and then headed off to Japanese class at the community center. Before our meeting started, I talked a lot with one of the teachers, and taught her some English. I also made a joke Which cracked her up. (I've been making so many Jokes recently! I'm trying to get better at it lol) She was really sweet, and it was so fun to talk. She really reminds me of my mom, which is a big plus ➕️ Class went well, we Ended up talking a decent bit about old technology, like floppy Disks (I'm looking at you Dad!) The Teacher was amazed that I knew about Floppy disks and 8 track recording stuff, Because nobody else really did haha. I'm grateful for what my Dad taught me :) Today was a huge miracle ^_^ One neat thing was that Nakazawa長老 was inspired, and felt like we should Go over by the college for finding, however, if we tried just a normal approach like usual, we would have never gotten to have that experience meeting so many college students. It was only because we were both inspired (by God!) and acted upon it that we were able to see such a huge miracle. I’m so grateful.

August 8th Rainy Afternoon 🌧 Today we had our weekly planning and Game night. For game night Nishioka San and Matsuoka San both showed up, and Nishioka san brought a bunch of sweets. Cheesecake, Ice cream, and then a couple packs of yogurt randomly lol. It was super fun playing Uno, I think we only had like 2 full games before the spiritual thought, I won both times 😊 Theres Definitely more to Uno than just luck, because I think my luck is generally bad so the only other option is that I'm incredibly talented 😊 hehe. Anyways, I shared a spiritual message, and I felt the spirit so strongly, especially as I prayed for the two of our friends at the end. I hope that in this next week or two we can see more growth with them!

Poetry Kyūkei (break)

佐用へ

佐用なら 私たちとは サヨナラだ

Neon Lights in the Rain dripping down the window Of my one-way train.

As I stare at my Reflection, I try to see Your face. But, I can't.

This next thing isn't poetry, just an interesting thought I had: I believe that God has given us our lives and trials as a way to learn and grow and become like him one day, but it made me wonder if God still learns. Can a supreme being with all the knowledge of the universe possibly still learn? I think the answer is yes, but I don't have any real way to know for sure at the moment. Here's an example: If you memorize a book, then you could probably say you “know” everything written in the book. But from the Book, you can still have new ideas, and “grow” in that sense I think. I bet that our Heavenly Father can see and maybe even learn a bit from our experiences! It's said that the teacher can even learn from the student, the parent can learn from the child. We are His children after all!

August 9th Suddenly Rainy 🕑🌧🌧

Dream about guitar, headphones, mine craft, neighbors driveway, milk and cereal, playing sax instrument with strings too, Elder Dobberfuhl got a 100 yen coin out of an arcade machine

Today was pretty odd, but really good. We started out our day by heading over to the Morimoto’s house to do some more weeding. It was awesome, they were super grateful. We went inside and had some super tasty Udon with a bunch of cool flavors and healthy ingredients, and even some ice cream!! It was awesome. After we ate, we had a short Mogi (roleplay) invitation to church, and had a prayer together. I love the Morimotos. Morimoto姉妹 also reminds me of my mom so I feel at home in their home 😁 Kindness and hospitality speak volumes! Later today due to the rain we were kinda stuck in the church doing some Facebook finding, but later that day we were able to go and play Volleyball with a bunch of young people around our age. They were all so good! I was very rusty. We got to talk a lot with a guy named Towa and a girl named Yuzuki. Hopefully we will have good experiences with them in the future!

August 10th Unconditionally Rainy 🌧🌧🌧

Dreamed about a huge house with some weird carpet and a huge attic space, dreamed about waiting in line and spilling my tiny dice all over 😭😂

Today was an awesome Sacrament meeting! The Imai couple visited from Takahashi, and they shared some amazing testimonies. There was also a couple from another ward that came by, one of them was a foreigner who served his mission in Tokyo South. They brought cookies for everyone! Another cool thing was Yamada Okada (Okada姉妹’s son) came to sacrament meeting! I'd invited him to come before, and today he pulled up! I didn't get to talk with him much after the meeting, but Nakazawa長老 sat by him. I sat by… a new member of the Tsuyama Branch!? There's a lady named Magi姉妹 from the Philippines who is moving Into the branch for the next 3 years! She's got a husband who's a member back in the Philippines, but she is working here. That'd be tough being away from your spouse For so long, hope they get to see each other occasionally 😅 Anyways I attempted to be her interpreter for the day, it was really hard not gonna lie. The speakers today were all obstructed from view, and they were all pretty Elderly, and used a lot of vocabulary I didn't know 😅 It was pretty barebones, and when the meeting was over Magi姉妹 said she was sleepy so that isn't exactly a great sign 😂😂 One other funny note from Sacrament meeting was after Magi姉妹’s arrival to Tsuyama was mentioned, Mitani兄弟 started clapping for her and the other members had to stare at him to make him stop 😂😂 it was so funny. The rest of today is Rain-Delayed train travel to Tottori. That's a fun sentence lol.

Well, it seems another week has passed us by. It's been really fast! Next week is gonna be even faster though, especially with District Pday, transfer news, DCM in person and exchanges. It's gonna be crazy! I'm super excited for it. Hey, guess what! Yeah, you guessed it. I love you! You guys are all so awesome. I hope that you found at least one thing from my weekly experiences, dreams and poetry to be of value to you. I can't wait to see you 💩! I would say “I'm gonna have to wait” but this mission is going by so quickly it wouldn't be accurate to call it waiting. Anyways, Love you guys! 😊💜

Elder Killian
summary: Woke up early, played Magic with family, had lunch, bought supplies, tried gumball machine.

subject: ❄️生まれーて初めーて❄️ (For the first time in forever)
body:
Bit of a long Email this time? I don't know, I think most of my emails are pretty long haha 😅 Have fun, I hope it isn't boring 😁🙌✨️

Pday! Last night we were able to arrive safely in Tottori, and we were welcomed by some oven pizza 🍕. It was pretty solid. Anyways, I know for sure I had some crazy dreams but I can't exactly remember them 😅 we started off our day by getting some breakfast from the Combini, then heading to the church to call our families. After a little while, we met up with the Sister missionaries and the 7 of us headed off to go bowling. It was pretty fun, I attempted to learn the strategy where you bowl without your thumb in the bowling ball and get spin on the ball, but I definitely need some practice. I was the first one to get a strike which was pretty lit though! 🎳💥 Afterwards we got some ice cream from McDonald's, then took a bus to Hamazushi (better than Sushiro 😏😉😂) and I ate like only Saba, which is Mackerel. It was really good but I think I ate too much of it or something because later that day I had a pretty miserable experience, let's just leave it at that 😂 Aaaaanyways after sushi we went back to the church, and had a waterfight out in the parking lot! It was already a pretty rainy day so we were a bit wet, but using water guns and a bucket I found laying around (I always bring some kind of game-breaking tool to these simple fun things 😂) we were soaked by the end of things. I was sitting Down at one point and someone poured the bucket full of water straight down my back and into my pants. I was not entirely dry until I changed into Pajamas that night lol. All in all, pretty fun solid Pday!

Tuesday Today we started off to a strong start with some french toast and caramel courtesy of me 😎 It was pretty fun to make breakfast. For everyone, I'd like to get better at making food. My French toast was good but it's not exactly all that complicated 😆 I think the Elders appreciated it though. After our breakfast, we headed over to the church for our last DCM! It was really fun, and I got to be mogi partners with Yamashida長老、he gave me some great insights for my teaching in the future. After DCM, we headed to Marugame Seimen to eat lunch!! (UDON!!) It was awesome, and a fun chance to sit down and talk with the district. Afterwards we split up, and started our exchange with the DL companionship! I ended up with Allen長老, a super awesome missionary who apparently went to my high school?? Yeah we tried so hard to find a class that we might have had in common, but we couldn’t find anything 😂😂 we Swear we've seen each other at some point though. Anyways, we got to do some awesome housing, and help out in Eikaiwa together. The housing was AWESOME. We found a new person who be down to learn about the church! Just wish she was in Tsuyama 😭 Her name is Fumie (foo-me-ay), a 60ish year old lady, and what started out as an interest in Tottori's Eikaiwa ended up being interest in the church! We started off by asking how she learns English, and she told us that she's always loved watching English Movies, and that she especially liked Horror movies and… Christian movies??? She then went on to say she's always loved the idea of Jesus, and she feels like there's someone always watching over us, and that our trials are for our growth! She was super accepting of our message, and also told us she enjoys searching for the day to day miracles. It was so cool. We said we'd come back, and I offered a prayer of gratitude with the 3 of us on her doorstep. such a neat experience, I felt so much love for her, and felt how much God loves her. Eikaiwa was pretty hilarious, and the guy that drove us to the station was kind enough to play one of my favorite Japanese songs, which he knew, and we sang along to it 😎 Last thing pretty unrelated but I asked Alken長老 what he sees in my eyes, and he said “death and anger.” Pretty intense lol.

Wednesday Today we were planning on leaving around 10:00 from the apartment, but the trains we were planning on using the day before were cancelled because of a fallen tree or something, so we had to leave earlier, around 8:30. When we arrived at the Main Station to buy our fancy tickets home (the not fancy trains were so delayed that we wouldn't make it back home until like 9 or 10 😢) so we paid like 30 bucks for some nice seats in an express train, but took too long making the tickets that we missed the train 😭 So we had to wait about an hour and a half before we could leave. In that time we grabbed a bit to eat from the Combini, and I said hi to a European lady with her grandson? Maybe? I told her I was from Utah, said “yes” when asked if I belonged to the Mormon Church, and she told me she’s met missionaries in Japan before and they were all really nice! I wished her safe travels 😊 I also got the chance to try out the 0.1 percent French that I remember from High School, and talked with some French tourists, and offered to take their picture together! Afterwards I even got a photo with them on my phone, it was awesome. They were so grateful that I came up to say hi, it was sweet 😊💕 And then, we were off! At our next stop we had to wait about another 2 hours in the heat for a train that was also delayed (maybe for the same reason as before?) Which was a bit of a drag, but it was ok. Finally after arriving in Tsuyama, we headed back to the church to prep for Eikaiwa, and also got to talk on the phone again with Katayama兄弟. He's pretty funny so it's always nice to chat. Eikaiwa was pretty fun, We talked about marriage stories, and it was so fun to hear about Matsuokaさん and Katayamaさん’s (different guy from before btw, same last name) experiences leading up to their marriages. Katayamaさん actually gas like the craziest broken love story I’ve ever heard. In college he fell in love with a researcher 4 years older than him, invited her to his home town to meet his parents, told his parents about it and they rejected the idea and made him marry someone they picked out. He got married, divorced like a year later, then married another researcher he'd met at college and moved to America. She's been living far away in Tokyo because she doesn't like Tsuyama (for at least 20 years I think??), but they are still married. What's Crazier is around 5 years ago, Katayamaさん got a phone call from a different woman he'd met at the research lab way back in college, saying she wanted to live with him?? So he Said yes and she came to Tsuyama and was living with him for a while, but her health started getting worse, and Katayamaさん couldn't take care of her, so he sent her far away to some care center. Pretty crazy, and sad. I want to think of a way that I can be support to him, especially when he is so against receiving the gospel with his current mindset. Unfortunately I'm NOT married so I could only attribute so much to the conversation haha 😂

Thursday This morning at around 3 AM we woke up to some cats outside angrily making noises at each other for like 30 minutes. It was pitch black and we weren't about to go out and look for them but I'm sure it was quite the standoff lol. They eventually stopped and we were able to get back to sleep 😴 In the real morning, I made some pancakes and we headed off to do do some service for the Okada family! We biked like 30 minutes to get there, and waited around a bit in the heat while they talked with some students coming over to practice their instruments on the second floor practice room. (Still pretty crazy they have all this stuff in their house lol.) After a little while, we got together and had a prayer, then commenced the weeding! I spent the majority of my time squatted down on their Gravel driveway pulling up grass, in the very hot sun. I am not gonna lie, I kind of vibe with the heat! Everyone thinks I'm crazy but I'm just having a good time experiencing new weather for the first time here in Japan haha. I made sure to drink plenty of water, and wear sunscreen, don't worry! While doing weeding I got a chance to talk with (Sister?) Mrs. Okada, and she told me how they not only have a grand piano on the 2nd floor, but also on their third floor??! They had to use a crane to get it there!! Just thinking of it reminds me of the movie cliche where a piano falls on someone lol. Afterwards we met up inside and shared a spiritual message, and Encouraged Okada姉妹 and her son to think about continuing lessons with us. You could definitely see the gears turning, especially with her son. Hopefully we can continue to foster a good relationship and keep supporting them! I think we also tried to do some housing today, but I cannot remember what happened so there's a possibility that it didn't happen 😂 (I'm writing this the night before I send this out)

Friday Dreamed that there was some kind I'd bike trail going over water and it was on like Lily pads, it was also covered in some kind of berry. It was owned by some guy living in a house nearby, and he was shocked when he realized I had eaten a bunch of berries that went down the trail. However, he wasn't mad, he was just surprised I was helping him out?? I only ate about half of all of the berries, and I think he wanted me to eat the rest. I'm pretty sure that in this dream, biking down the path of berries also meant I somehow ate the berries at the same time, so confusing. Also, apparently on one end of the trail there was a big tunnel that went down into kind of a water slide cave system; I managed to avoid going down, but apparently a while ago some girl was going by on a tube slide and got carried down into the tunnels lol. Pretty odd. Today was a bit of a slow, we got our weekly planning mostly done which was good, but besides that there wasn't much until game night. Game night had a pretty big turnout! I think there were 7 of us in total. We had a pretty insane game of uno at the end too. We shared a solid spiritual message, and wished everyone safe travels home. Its also worth noting that every time we have Game night Nishiokaさん always brings some kind of authentic Japanese sweets. This time it was some rice crackers, which were pretty solid. Literally. Many Japanese sweets I've found to be very hard! I don't mind it but I definitely cringe watching a few 60-70 year-olds chomping on these things 💀 gotta be careful with your teeth!

Saturday Dreams Dreamed about really needing to use the bathroom, I had to search all over the place to find one. Also Sonomura長老 and I took pictures together somewhere.

Today was a little bit of a drag, but let's get into it! We started off the day by heading to the Library to volunteer reading English books to kids. A couple days ago I got a package from my parents, and it had a new book (series Of books in a single volume) to read to the kids! It is called Elephant and Piggie, and it's definitely one of my favorite kids books. Adults and Kids can read it and enjoy it lol. Anyways, we got to the library, And talked with a new lady about the reading. Something crazy that happened was after walking into the library, we saw an open door leading to a dark room, glanced inside and saw a black and white film right as it panned to Jesus Christ on the cross? 😱 It was definitely a shock to see in Japan, apparently it was a movie related somehow to Catholicism somewhere in Europe. After that, we waited around for a bit before we heard a chime, then headed inside of the “reading hut” or some name similar to that. It's a big circular room in the middle of one side of the library. It was time to start reading and… there weren't any kids that were even there 😅😢 The lady who led us over there seemed pretty panicked, And started trying to get people to join us, but it didn't seem like any of the kids were interested. We decided to just start reading the book to her in hopes someone would hear and be interested lol. Eventually a small family agreed to join in, and we finished the book, then started it over again because the kids only got the last part. It like 90 percent went over their heads, but nobody started crying or anything, so I'd say it's a success ✌️ hopefully next month there will be more kids 😅 After our funny excursion at the library, we headed down to the main floor (the library is on the 4th floor of a big building) and found a bench in the connected covered Marketplace (Shoutengai) to do our contacting at. I attempted to call a handful of Grey Dots, people who haven't been taught or contacted in ages, and I didn't get any responses. We Then decided to make an attempt at streeting people to find someone to go and eat lunch with. I stopped a couple High schoolers walking one of their bikes, the two of them were on the school baseball club. I asked if they knew of anywhere nearby to eat for a reasonable price, and they were nice enough to walk us to a shop… which was closed! Lol! I told them that they were liars 😂 There happened to be another shop nearby so we ended up eating there instead. They had to leave, and weren't interested in joining our Eikaiwa which was a bummer, but we appreciated the restaurant tip. It was an Udon shop, which was pretty solid. Also they gave us each a tiny little shot glass of coffee and we won't talk about how Nakazawa長老 literally drank some 😂 (Like, how could he not tell it was coffee???) After we ate, we had some language study time and then started walking to a coin laundry place that Yamasakiさん wanted us to meet her at for introducing ourselves. Nakazawa長老 has met her once, and he reeeeally thinks she's weird. To be fair she does send us weird pictures like “nice job today” and “be careful in the heat” with cats with maracas 🪇 and all sorts of random “cutesy” things lol. She also uses an emoji for every other word she types it feels like haha. I was really looking forward to meeting her but…. As we were walking over (in the intense heat) we got a text saying she couldn't make it 🌀🌀 Dang. Well, we had nothing to do but head back to the church for our Book of Mormon study time with Koumoto兄弟 and Mitani兄弟. It was a great time with the two of them, and at the end we talked about going out sometime to eat together. Hopefully we will get a chance next week! Bit of an odd day, but we tried our best with the circumstances 😅

But wait… this evening during dinner we got a phone call. It wasn't the branch president or a member we often calm with, it was one of the Grey Dots I had called earlier! His name is Dōiさん, and we says he has interest in church, and wants to come tomorrow morning!!? Let's goooooooo! 🎊🪅🎉

Sunday: Dōi didn't pull up 😢😭😅 Today I translated again for Magi姉妹 which was not any easier. Dōi san didn't make it today which really stinks. I hope that we can stay somewhat in contact with him. After church we set off on an adventure with Koumoto兄弟 and Hattori会長 to visit a bunch of less active members and friends. It was cool, and we got to visit maybe 4 houses. Even just introducing ourselves I can tell brings a light to those around us.

Poetry Station 🚉

It's hard being far away From the ones I love But even if it's not today I will see them again Living in a foreign land Blessed from up above I can feel the unseen hand Encouragement from 天 ( “Ten,” meaning Heaven )

僕の好きな色、グレーパープル

紫は グレーになるん ですが、それ は全然いい だよ。大好きだ！

Finding the meaning in what I'm writing is not quite as natural a phenomenon as one might think. Take my word for it.

Monday Random note, my right eye has been twitching like crazy this week. I'm not sure if it's because my Mom said it's been happening to her as well in an email, but It's been brutal! I think it's just been an exhausting, stressful week, especially Sunday. Sundays just went from being the most calm day of the week to the most stressful, the 2 hour interpreting is rough fr. I'm the only one who can do it though 😢 so I guess I'll just be doing this until I leave the area. I trust that I'll be blessed for my efforts, and I'm honestly grateful to help out a member in need! Anyways, back to Monday!! OK so no more Monday, I'll write about it next week. Hahaha 😂

I love you guys so much! It's been a long week for sure, but so many cool things happened. I'm super excited for this next one! (Lots of cool stuff happened on Monday too, so look forward to hearing about it!)

Whatever your situation is, wherever you are, know that you are loved by a Heavenly Father, and by your brother and Savior Jesus Christ. These 2 are here To support you through thick and thin. I have had a pretty good life so far, but there have been countless dark and miserable times for sure. Sometimes I felt like I was entirely alone. While knowing that there Is a loving Father and our Savior watching over us can provide comfort, we will still experience sadness and discomfort. Like Fumieさん, however, I believe that these trials can help to grow us, and increase our spiritual Strength! I'd encourage you to think about how you view your trials. I promise that as you think about your hardships as a means for growth, and keep in mind the presence of Heavenly Father and Jesus Christ, you will be able to make it through that difficult trip, or that night shift, or your tense family situation; whatever. You can do it, but don't try to do it alone.

I love you. So much! See you next time 😊💜🩶🫶
summary: Arrived in Japan, met trainers, tried bowling, had water fight, enjoyed sushi.

subject: Four Percent (My Phone's Battery)
body:
Long maybe? It's up to you! 😂

By the way the title doesn't really hold any special significance lol.

Monday Today we had a pretty lit experience at the University. We were trying to figure out how to ask about making a circle there about our church, and we ended up going on the campus and talking with a super cool counselor guy about it. He said if we could capture a student (lol) that we could make a group in the college! But until we do we can't. He did take our flyers though, and said there's a lot of students there who would be interested in learning English with us!! So awesome! We also had Family Home Evening at the church with Koumoto兄弟 and Nishioka san. It was great, and we watched a video about the first vision together. Hopefully things keep progressing with Nishioka San.

Pday Today I found some more CD's (for less than a dollar each 🤑) by my favorite artist Sumimaswitch!!!!!!! Listen to Zurachinaruka by Sukimaswitch do it do it do it

Wednesday Today was another interesting mixture of things. We started the day with some contacting, Facebook Missionary Work, and Companionship study. After eating some lunch we set off in the heat to bike to an art installation to talk with Ogawa San from the art store. She said she was gonna be there, and we were hoping to talk with her and ask what she thinks about Christianity and if she'd be down to check out our Church. So, we started biking there. OK. I had noticed before we left if Nakazawa長老 knew where we were going, and he said he knows the place, so I thought that he could lead us there, and I didn't check the map. OK at the time I had the feeling it might be a bad idea but dismissed it because I thought I could place some trust in my companion! Bad idea 😂 It wasn't bad to try and trust him with the directions, but I really should have just verified where we were going. First we went to the big mall, he thought it was there. We arrived, he looked at the map, and it turns out it was on the other side of the river lol! So, we biked all the over to the other side, and slowly came to a halt on the sidewalk. After another check of the map, it turns out we had to go back across to the side of the river we were on at the start!! 😂😂 Honestly I don't mind the heat all that much, but poor Nakazawa長老 wasn't doing Great 🔥🥵🔥 We finally made it at the place where we were supposed to meet Ogawa San and… SHE WASNT THERE 😂😂 We texted her when we got there and she said it was too hot so she didn't go, and she was super sorry when she realized we'd gone all the way there lol. We literally told her that morning we were going!! I guess she thought we meant another day or something 😭💀 Anyways, rather than have a cool conversation with her we ended up doing some housing instead. It was very hot and the responses were pretty dry. There were definitely a few nice people, but nobody was really interested. We got to see a few really pretty traditional houses though, It's really neat. If I end up living in America permanently I might try and build a Japanese style house, at the very least I'm gonna have a room with some Tatami Mats though 🔥 After our very hot housing, we biked over to Mitani兄弟’s house to say hi and coordinate a time to get some food with him next week. We showed, up, rang his doorbell and nobody was home, gave him a call and… he was home but just didn't notice the bell I guess lol. He invited us inside! While in the entryway I petted his dog and it peed all over a rug and the floor, reminds me of my dog Pepper back at home 🏡🐶🟨☂️ We sat down and chatted with him for a bit about old Japanese money and a bunch of pictures he has ganging up. One crazy thing is that his Daughter’s Husband’s Brother invented Hatsune Miku, a synthetic voice/character featured in a bunch of Japanese songs. The genre is called Vocaloid, and the voices are all done by computer. He even has a meishi (business card) from the guy! Super cool!!! We also went on a little walk with Mitani兄弟 and his dog (very hot lol) and then headed back to the church for kids Eikaiwa! We were asked by Morimoto姉妹 to prepare some drawings of facial features to use during our lesson. It was fun to draw and cut them out, and it makes me look forward to helping my kids with their book reports and posters some day. It also makes me grateful and appreciate all of the work my mom puts into crafts and art for primary and all of my siblings posters from school throughout the years. I hope I can live up to her Christlike and pure example! When Morimoto姉妹 arrived at the church and walked into the room where we were going to have kids Eikaiwa, she frowned and said "It smells bad in here" in English. That kinda stung. 😂😂😂 There isn't much we can do with the heat, no matter what we do, if we go outside we will come back in soaked in sweat lol. I thought it was a hilarious comment. In the end, Kids Eikaiwa went great! Hopefully next week I'll get a chance to read from a storybook my mom mailed me, but we didn't have time today 😢

Thursday Today was weekly planning. It took like 4 hours (crazy) and we didn't even finish everything (even crazier) today we also had a lesson with Mitani兄弟 and Japanese class at the community center. It was pretty fun, and I was able to talk with a guy named Bob. He's super funny, and probably around the same age as my dad. He's a foreigner, and he's pretty simple worded. I talked with him in English for a little while and in a couple weeks we're gonna meet up for lunch! I'm excited to hear what he thinks about the gospel 🔥🔥

Weird fact about me: big strong people intimidate me and I feel super self conscious when talking with them. Also fun fact I'm doing push ups every day and increasing by 2 every day now. Right now (this is on Sunday that I'm writing this) I'm up to 56. In not to long I'm gonna be triple digits, we will see how I hold Up! I have never had consistent daily exercise in my life so this is weird but Fun. I’m also noticing I'm eating a lot more probably since I'm burning calories lol. It's my strength Era 🦾💪🤳🔥🔥🔥

Friday I definitely had some odd dreams tonight. I dreamed about Elder Martinson being 2 different people, and one of them was shedding tears over the other one doing something. It was pretty odd 😂 I also dreamed that I met those Two college students we met a couple weeks ago. I really hope we can meet them again.

Today we biked like 10 Kilometers (one way) through the mountains to a less active member's family's house. At first Nakazawa長老 was a bit hesitant with the idea of it (after he Noticed how far it was lol) but I mentioned how it was his idea in the first place, and we headed off. It was a pretty intense bike ride. I definitely hit like 40 or 50 miles an hour at some point going down one of those huge hills 😂 It was just maybe not the most fun biking up them. We finally pulled up to the house (somehow survived) and the family was actually there!! They are the Yamauchi family, and they are so chill. We talked for a long time, and they were super grateful that we showed up, and expressed gratitude that it was just the two of us and not the branch president lol. They don't really like talking with him I guess 😅 The son showed us a bunch of cool Gundam figures he's been building, and Sister Yamauchi even let us pet their baby chicken! Shoutout to my dad and his 5 chickens. Apparently there used to be 6 little chicks at the Yamauchi house but 5 died so there is only the one left. That's rough 😭 🐤🪦🐥🪦🐣🪦🐥🪦🐤🪦 It was a great chance to talk, and we shared a spiritual message and gave them a snack, and headed back up the hills (and down them) to get to the church to call Katayama兄弟 on the phone and have Game night. Game night also went well, and we had a lesson/message with Matsuoka San and Nishioka san talking about the Sacrament, and invited them both to church. Nishioka san doesn't really want to commit to going yet, and matsuoka san seems like shes try and go, but has work. Bit by bit, I feel like we are making progress! By the way I killed it at Uno again, I think I'm actually getting pretty good at it. I literally didn't think there was any strategy to it, but that's changed since I've been playing it like 3 times a week. I can pretty consistently win like half the games I play 😎😎 I attribute my success to my father, for playing a game of uno with me as a kid (just the two of us) that took lowkey 2 hours. Crazy memory.

Saturday Part of my dreams today took place in a pretty abandoned Church building in the big gym area. It was absolutely soaked in water everywhere! Pretty odd.

Today went by soooo fast. It's weird, it feels like the more big long things that happen that take a lot of chunks of time seem to make things feel slow, whereas a bunch of small things in a week make it seem fast. Maybe I've just been more focused in on my missionary purpose this week?? No idea. Today we started off by biking to the library to participate in a lecture that Katayama san from Eikaiwa recommended me. He said I probably wouldn't understand it very well but that I would like it. So, we arrived and at the front of the Library Katayama san greeted us in English and said that it was a much more difficult subject than he had expected, and that even HE was gonna find it hard to understand lol. It was an hour and a half lecture, and it flew right by! I only understood bits and pieces, but it ended up being based around people's personalities and what attributes make up different parts of people's Characters. Looooot of Japanese I didn't understand, but afterwards I went up to share an interesting thought that I'd had with the professor. He's a really fast talker but was nice and emphasized the importance of what I had mentioned. It's also worth noting that during the lecture, Katayama san gave quite the handful of comments, and one question that completely baffled the professor, like he was actually at a loss for words 😂😂 Pretty entertaining. As Katayama san was leaving, I mentioned it would be fun to talk together about the lecture. He asked how much time we had afterwards, and I said, “maybe 10-15 minutes?” but he said that wasn't enough time, so we will Talk about it later I guess 😂 As we left the Library out in the bike parking I accidentally started a domino effect with the bikes knocking each other over. It's pretty Wild how quickly it can get out of hand if you just bump one of them the wrong way 😂 managed to stop it after like 3 or so but it was scary. After our college-style Lecture we headed back to the apartment to cut some new flyers that we printed at a combini. On the way, I suggested that we stop by the toy store from before, because I had noticed on out way to the Library that a guy I had talked with before was working. We stopped By, said Hi, and he was nice enough to write down my information so that the group of people that play Magic the Gathering at a community center can let me know when they play!! I'm really hoping that I can meet up with some cool people that share an interest! After cutting out the flyers at the apartment, we headed to the park to try and talk with some people using Nakazawa長老’s Diavlos, which are these cool big Yoyos that you guide with two handheld sticks attached by a cord. 🪀 There was like nobody there!! But… there was one guy alone playing basketball. I don't like basketball. Ever since I've been a kid and was semi-forced to play a game or two of basketball at Church with a bunch of kids that didn't really like me, I've never quite looked at it the same way. However, I'm a new man lately. Looking at stuff in my past with a new lens 😎😎👓🕶🥸 So, I said, “Let's go and play basketball with him!!” I walked up and said that we only had like 15 minutes, but could we join him? He said yes, and was super hyped! We played with him for a little while, and got his name and LINE info! His name is Kaito. He's super nice, and a cool guy. He doesn't live here which stinks but maybe we can refer him to the missionaries in Okayama to Play basketball with him! Super cool interaction, and it wouldn't have happened if I didn't decide to be brave and try something I'm not a big fan of. Hey, it was pretty fun! Following our basketball, we headed back to the church to read from The Book of Mormon with Koumoto兄弟 (kyoudai). It was a neat experience, we shared our testimonies of how God has shown us his love, it was very spiritual. Afterwards we played a round of Uno せっかくだから 😂 and finished up our studies at the church. Overall, it was a great day!

Sunday (Not any less stressful 😭) Today Nakagawa長老 gave a talk in church! It was cool, and about How we need to have a personal testimony of the Savior and of the Gospel. He also shared how we can't just get a testimony alone. We need to rely on and trust others. I was able to interpret his talk alright, but I was also being swarmed by little kids at the same time, and it took like 20 minutes before an adult Actually came over and took the kids away so I could focus 😂 It's so hard trying to show attention to some 4 year Olds while also interpreting 😭 It only got more difficult as the next speakers went on, and the kids were so loud I could barely hear the speakers (also due to their muted voices from wearing masks 😷) let alone trying to decipher a difficult topic for a talk. It was pretty intense on my brain. After Sacrament meeting I bonked my head on the metal part of a door frame and got ready for my next hour of interpretation. This time I got to join the relief society??? Normally guys don't participate but as Maggi姉妹 (shimai)’s interpreter I got to join in on the fun! It was somewhat Less stressful than Sacrament meeting but I still had to really focus in the whole time. Afterwards we said bye to Maggi姉妹, and stayed at the church afterwards for another 2 and a half hours talking with the branch president, branch mission council, and trying to figure out ideas to make our activities from here on more catered towards our friends and spiritual. Finally we headed home and had lunch around 3PM. By this point I was pretty wasted. Besides just being a little Hangry, I Was pretty deprived of any mental energy for the day. We ended our day with followups with the district leader, some painting And origami for Nishioka san, and a zone devotional. Today was quite the mental workout, and I wish I maybe prioritized some more spiritual meditation type time, especially with how busy the stuff I couldn't control today was. But in the end, I'm not perfect. I just have something I can try to do better next week.

The same goes for you too, whoever you may be.

You aren't perfect, hate to break it to you lol. I'm honestly probably not the one telling you, I bet that you are aware of your faults and weaknesses. I don't intend to be demeaning, but you aren't enough on your own. You can't do this by yourself.

But, you don't have to do this alone.

You have a loving Heavenly Father watching over you minute to minute. His son, Jesus Christ, knows your faults and knows about the tears you have shed over the times you feel that you fell short.

You have others around you. If you feel that you don't have any friends or anyone close, reach out! Be someone else's friend! Reach out to me!! I love you, whoever you are.

More than I ever can love you, however, Jesus Christ and Heavenly Father love you. Please place your trust in them. They will guide you to happiness. And once you've tasted even a bit of that happiness, go and share it with others. That's what I'm trying to do right now! As a missionary, but even through this Email! 😆

Let’s both keep going strong this week.

✨️Elder Killian✨️
summary: Monday: Lit experience, Family Home Evening; Pday: Found CD's by Sumimaswitch; Wednesday: Contacting, bike to art installation, trust issue.

subject: Chivalry Isn't Dead (Part 1)
body:
Welcome to the first 2 part email of my mission 😲 I have a lot of stuff to write, and I figured that half of it could be done on a train while heading up to Zone conference, so that's what I'm planning on!

Starting off the email with a missed story from last week…

Last week (Don't remember which day) we were biking back to the church and I remembered a pretty battered looking art shop I'd noticed, and realized it would be along the path we were taking (ok I just remembered this was on Pday, and we were actually leaving from the church lol). Anyways, we stopped by, and we were able to talk with a super cool guy who was the owner. He's familiar with our church, and seems to respect and enjoy the missionaries' company! We talked for like an hour, and he even gave us each a cool quartz crystal from a box. Turns out hes got some status as a Buddhist leader, but he loves Christian hymns! I wanted to invite him to Join us and sing hymns at the church together on Sunday, but I kind of got cut off unintentionally and ended up not saying it. As I’m writing this a week later I realize that we should totally go back and invite him!! He's a pretty humble guy, And a really neat artist too. He sells a bunch of replica paintings he makes with acrylic, and they are all super impressive. he’s got like 5 paintings of the “woman with the pearl earring” and they look just like the original! He expressed that he was grateful just to have someone to talk too, maybe we can stop by occasionally and he will have more interest one day! Even if it's little by little I believe that God is trusting us to help any person to come closer to Him.

Pday Today was a pretty crazy start to the week. For a week or two now, a member from Nakazawa長老’s home ward has been trying to mail. Package to us! However, we missed the delivery guy dropping it off, so in the end there was a need for us to pick it up from the post office. Now allow me to introduce a lady named Kei san. She's a nice lady in her 60's who's been messaging us for a long time, and always sends us cute photos and emojis almost every day! She loves English and usually messages us in Straight English, which is pretty impressive. Anyways, she lives kind of far from the church, but apparently she has attended Eikaiwa in the past. So, back to the original story, we made up our minds to go to the post office and get that package once and for all. We went there, and guess who showed up… Some random lady whom we had never met before suddenly walked up to us and introduced herself as we were filling out some information to receive the package! Her name was Hongo san, and she'd also apparently been to Eikaiwa in the past. We gave her our phone number, and then… as we were talking with her, we met KEI SAN! She apparently works at the post office!! She was way smaller and younger looking than I had expected (lol) its probably just because she was working at a post office but she reminded me of an Elf working in Santa’s workshop 🧑‍🎄🧝‍♀️ we'd never seen each other before, but she kind of heard us speaking and then asked if we were who she thought we were! 😂 She was mindblown that I could speak Japanese lol. She told us that she would send us some letters later. So sweet! On the way out of the post office, Hongo san was determined to buy us a drink from the vending machine, but the machine kept on spitting back out her bill! It was too new apparently, and she was pretty ticked off lol. She went over to another random old lady and asked her to give her change for the bill, and it seemed like that lady didn't have enough coins. In the end I actually had enough change to exchange the bill, and she bought us both some ice cocoa, and sent us on our way. This whole interaction really made me feel like I was in a movie lol. *Note* At this point, Nakazawa長老 and I both had our backpacks completely stuffed full of our belongings to stay the night in Tottori for DCM the next day, and the package we got from the post office was pretty big, and we didn't have time to go to the apartment so I had to awkwardly take it on my bike to the station, then all the way to Tottori! Pretty obnoxious lol. After visiting the card shop (and getting some cards for super cheap because the owner is kind of friends with us now) we headed off to Tottori. It took longer than expected (as expected lol), but when we got to Tottori we went to a CD/Records shop and talked with the owner about music. He has an INSANE knowledge of American music. Like he could name a song from an album that I liked, and tell me that it was a cover of a song by Simon and Garfunkel, and that it showed up in a movie or something, pretty wild. He recognized every artist I brought up. I bought a couple American CD’s (kind of Ironic, right?? 😂) and wished him well. And finally, we made it to the apartment to rest for the night. (Not before playing a game of magic with Shieffer長老’s new bean) (He destroyed me 😂).

Poetry

I will never regret the time that I'm spending Here far away in Japan Though at times the trials may feel unending It's making me into a man

I want to be A father A teacher A husband A worker And more It’s hard but I trust dedicating these years Will surely have blessings in store.

Well….. that's it for part 1 (even though it was lowkey just 1 day from this week 😂😂😂😢😅). I'm all out of time for tonight. Look forward to part 2 😲✌️
summary: Arrived Japan, met trainer, started email with train story, shared art shop visit.

subject: Chivalry Isn't Dead (Part 2)
body:
Welcome to Part 2 ✌️😲✌️

Tuesday Today was an awesome DCM in person! We actually did make it to Tottori, and we learned a lot as well! We all met together afterwards to eat Udon (and the desert we brought all the way to Yonago). The rest of our day was unfortunately traveling back home lol. The trains do not like us.

Wednesday Today we visited Ogi San again, and brought him some fruit! He was super reluctant to take any but in the end agreed to take one. Later we will visit him again! We tried some housing on the way back to the church, to little avail. No reason to be discouraged though! I believe that we are leaving an impact on even the people that initially refuse us. Today we also met up and got some ramen for dinner with Mitani Kyoudai!! Great day!

Thursday Today was SKK so we didn't do much besides planning. It was pretty productive as far as planning goes, so that's great! We also had the lesson with Mitani Kyoudai at the church, and afterwards Japanese class at the community center. I swear I need to start tape recording these conversations. I swear the mix of personalities between us at that table in the community center is crazy. Fujita Sensei referenced “Lets Make a Deal” and we had to guess which of 3 boxes a toy car was inside of. It was insanely deep psychological stuff and I felt my brain like grow 3 sizes even if I can’t explain anything that I learned that night. Next time I'm gonna bring Uno and we are gonna play it at our table lol. I’m looking forward to it!!

Dreamed about さくちん, Bananas, an arcade (maybe?) 書店会

Friday Today we did some sign streeting in front of a high school! Well, we started off by doing sign streeting. The approach that Nakazawa長老 wanted to use was to try drawing pokemon for students, then invite them to our Eikawa. We ended talking with a handful of students but only 3 took a request for the drawing. The first person was a girl who we've talked with a couple times randomly at this point. She's very quiet and composed, and even though we didn't talk with her about the gospel this time around, I really feel like she would listen and have an interest in it if we are genuine with her. After our finding at the high school, we headed back to the church for game night. I dominated uno as usual, and played some super fun ping pong with Matsuoka San. We shared a couple scriptures for our spiritual thought, here they are: Alma 37:6-7 6 Now ye may suppose that this is foolishness in me; but behold I say unto you, that by small and simple things are great things brought to pass; and small means in many instances doth confound the wise. 7 And the Lord God doth work by means to bring about his great and eternal purposes; and by very small means the Lord doth confound the wise and bringeth about the salvation of many souls. These are pretty famous scriptures in our church, but that's no reason to downplay their importance and truth. If we spend our time looking for big miracles and “meaningful” things, we are going to lose sight of the countless simple wonders and tender mercies that are abundant in our lives. Look for the details. Look for God's love. I promise that you will find it.

Saturday My dreams were actually insane tonight. Dreamed about: Kanye, birds turning into burgers that when placed on my head communicated with me, pizza, fishing watches out of sand with my companion, weird stuff

Chivalry is not dead! Today we studied the Book of Mormon with members, and helped give some water to an almost dead cat at the church. We were also able to do some housing and gave out a decent amount of Eikaiwa flyers. There were definitely a few people we met with good potential! Pray that they will follow through on what they felt and call us 😁Earlier today we were able to meet with Katayama san and talk a bit about his thoughts on religion. I can tell that there's a change working in his heart. We were able to join a small concert that he invited us to, and it was pretty neat. There were 3 ladies performing songs from Gibli movies, and before the performance I noticed they were struggling to set up one of their phones against a box to record their performance. So, with my nice fancy phone stand, I strolled over and offered It to them to use. They were super happy!! At amother point today I also got the door for a lady wheeling product into a Combini; Chivalry isn't dead. 😎😎

Sunday Long long long long long Today we were at the church from 9AM until 5PM. It was all pretty productive, but definitely took a loooong time. We were so hungry by the end 😅

🌟Some more poetry? Don't mind me-🌟

Trains trains trains trains trains Trains trains trains trains trains trains trains Trains trains trains trains trains

This could be just a personal problem But writing the same word time after time I begin to question the truth in the spelling And the spelling certainly questions mine

I need to go to the bathroom I haven't since I left the church And I fear I face doom When I feel my stomach lurch

My companion was asked to order me some water From the vending machine. However, I felt the urge to slaughter When a different drink he handed me (An exaggeration by the way lol)

A face in the fog in my mind I don't know whose it may be Honestly, I don't mind As long as I can see A face in the fog in my mind I don't know whose it may be Honestly, I don't mind As long as I can see That there's a reason to move past the trials and grief that at times we experience It's not going to last forever, I trust I'm willing to bet more than a pence That it'll be for my good, therefore my hardships are a must A face in the fog smiles back

花が咲く ではないー笑 暑すぎだ

秋来んね 日が終わんねー ねぇー なぜ？

ウソっぽい 僕の詩が ち ょっと変

Pday Today has been crazy already. We started off the day by calling our families at the church, then started biking to a pretty far away Mcdonalds to meet up with a girl named Yuzuki. On the way we stopped by the trusty card shop and got some beyond reasonably discounted cards, then biked like 30-40 minutes in the heat. It was a pretty fun bike ride, and we got to see a few places I've never been to before in Tsuyama along the way. We got there a decent bit earlier than expected, so we ordered a drink and waited for Yuzuki to show up. Yuzuki showed up, we ate and talked for a little while, and explained to her a bit about the word of wisdom. She was pretty surprised that we don't drink tea or coffee! She said she works at Starbucks and I told her about how Sakurako from Sennan works at a café and is planning on getting baptized this year 😲 They are only a year apart from each other, so hopefully Yuzuki San can consider joining our church as something that is a possibility for her. After Mcdonalds, we biked over to the shopping mall and took some photos at a photo booth, and walked around the dollar store before realizing we had to leave right then or we wouldn't be able to take our train! So, we said goodbye and rushed to the station… we just barely made it. On the way Nakazawa長老’s back tire Popped, and a lady almost ran me over. We are gonna have to worry about the bike when we get back, but somehow even with the flat we were able to make it to the station in the nick of time. After that, it was just trains until we made it to Yonago. At least I hope we made it! Guess We will find out next week ✌️

Well, congrats! You've made it to the end of my email. I love you so much!!

Let's do our best this next week!

Love, Elder Killian
summary: Welcome back! DCM in Tottori, visited Ogi San, SKK planning, Japanese class, sign streeting.

subject: It's not all hopeless.
body:
I thought this was gonna be kind of short, but it's kind of moderately lengthed. Enjoy!

Tuesday Today we had Zone Conference in Yonago! It was a great chance to get a refresher of some inspiration to keep moving forward in our missionary work. My English is failing me lol. Today I got a good chance to talk with Sister Sano. Her favorite food is tomatoes lol, I find that so cute 😂 She says That after she found out so many people dislike tomatoes she usually gives a different answer when asked her favorite food. After Zone Conference, we had interviews with President Sano, which went all right. We had to really hurry afterwards to make it to the Station on time for our train! We ended up making it 💪 Due to some rain, however, it was quite the slow ride at first, the train fully stopped many times along the way. Saw some crazy lightning too! When we made it back home, President Hattori and Brother Hisamune helped us to make it back home with the flat tire. We left the bikes at the church after they picked them up for us, and we got driven To the apartment.

Wednesday Today we went and got the flat tire fixed, and got slightly scolded for not keeping our tires more pumped. Now I know what kind of tire pressure we need though, so it should be A-ok. Today we planned on trying some finding by the university, but it seems like it's still on summer break, so there weren't really any people around. We ended up going over to the art shop, and talking with the owner this time. They've done an event or something in the past with our church, and the owner knew about us a bit. We talked a lot with Ogawa San, she also gave us some art homework. lol We are gonna use some of our free time to make some art to bring back to the store sometime in a week or so. it's gonna get put up somewhere with a bunch of people’s designs, I'll definitely send a picture if we can make it to the event! After that, we did housing for roughly an hour, with pretty limited positive responses. But we are doing our best, that's all that the Lord requires! He will provide those who seek Him in His own time, as long as we keep working hard! Later today we had kids Eikaiwa, followed up by adult Eikaiwa. It was back to back craziness, but it was really awesome.

Thursday Today was SKK, so we mostly just planned. However, we did get to have a phone call with Brother Katayama, and Sister Ushio suddenly dropped by the church with some food for us, as well as some bookmarks she made. She is the sweetest! Last Sunday she also gave us each a cute little hand towel. So generous, and such a great example.

Friday Today we went back to the university, entered without permission and surprised the front office again. This time I had my camera around my neck too and everyone was sooooo sussed out lol. The same nice teacher from before talked with us though, and Said I'm not suspicious because it's my second time. We inquired as to if we could participate in the campus clubs even if we aren't students, and uh, well, we can't lol. But! He told us again if we can find a student friend that we kind of have a window into things. He was nice enough to also tell us when summer break ends, which is in like 2 weeks. He suggested that if we're looking for young people to meet with that we should work at a nearby Combini lol. Super nice suggestion, probably not gonna work out but we love the effort and ideas on his part haha. After that, we did some more housing nearby, also to not exactly much avail. We're gonna keep pressing forward though. We did have a few nice people we met, and technically one guy said if he had interest he'd pull up to our church/eikaiwa. He kept saying “IF I'm interested, IF I'm interested” lol. Tonight we had Game night back at the church, and it was an arousing success! (I know this is the wrong word Mom, but it was just too funny not to use 😂😂 please erase it before you send out the email 😀 😂) Today one of Nishioka San’s friends came!!! Nishioka san invited her and even drove to the church with her the day before to show her what it's like. HOW IS NISHIOKA SAN NOT A MEMBER. Lol. Anyways, her friends name is Taguchi san. She is like super super way too humble lol. She ended up having a great time, and she even brought donuts!! We shared a spiritual thought at the endaiI think she also liked it. Nishioka san also convinced Taguchi san and her Husband?! to join us for family home evening on Monday night. What a huge blessing! I pray that they will both be touched by the spirit, and continue to develop a relationship with the members here. I also got to play some ping pong with Matsuoka San, and she told me she is getting the art I made for her framed lol. She Wants my business card to go with it. I hope that she can tell how much we care about her and that she will continue to seek out our message of the Gospel.

Saturday Today we had a pretty cool experience. We were able to visit Sister Magi and her roommates to give Sister Magi a priesthood blessing. Her two roommates she was with are both from the Philippines, and were both so grateful for us to come and bless Sister Magi. She's been feeling sick this last week, and hasn't been able to make it to work. President Hattori drove us, and Brother Koumoto also came with us. We brought them a treat to share, and it was a really neat experience. Kind of relieving to talk with super open and friendly people lol. I love Filipinos 🔥 What's even cooler, is Sister Magi invited them both to come to church! We also asked them to come, and they both said sure! Huge miracle!! After driving back to the church, (and eating some Taiyaki on the way back) we studied from the Book of Mormon with Brother Koumoto and President Hattori. Pretty awesome day.

Sunday In the end, only one of Sister Magi’s friends was able to make it. Her name is Jamcy. I sat in between the two of them and interpreted the Sacrament meeting the best I could. Today was kind of a big day for Jamcy. She kind of distanced herself from religion for the past year or two due to the way her church responded to some issue or event back in the Philippines. I think mostly from the awesome example Sister Magi is setting, she's had the desire to build back up her relationship with God. Today during second hour I was also able to introduce her to The Book of Mormon, and she was immediately intrigued. I gave her a copy, and she's going to start reading it. I explained to her how it connects to the Bible somewhat, but hopefully the spirit did some of the explaining, I don't feel like I did a great job. I felt the spirit all of today though. It was a super cool chance to get to know Jamcy (27 btw) and introduce her to our church! She said she was so relieved to have people to speak with in English, and I think she also felt the spirit. What an amazing Sacrament meeting! I'm so grateful. After church, we visited the Yamauchi family with President Hattori, as well as brother Ikegami. It was a good chance to show our support for less active members, and I pray that they will both feel the desire and need to come more fully back into the arms of the Savior.

It's been a really really really hard week for me emotionally due to some stuff I shouldn't really go into much detail about. But look at all of these great experiences and miracles even through the hard times. I'm so so grateful for God's hand in my life. As long as I am pressing forward with the desire to do good, and love those around me, I know I will make it through whatever comes my way. I am grateful for your prayers, and I am grateful for you.

Yes, you! I love you so so so much! Each and every one of you are so precious to me. Please keep your head up, and know that there are others going through trials too! Also know that God understands your trials better than you do! Be humble. And choose the hard right over the easy wrong.

Guess what, it's gonna be hard! but it's 1000000% worth it, thanks to the Savior, Jesus Christ.

Love you guys!
summary: Tuesday: Zone Conference, interviews, train delay; Wednesday: Flat tire repair, art shop visit.

subject: 🐛🪲🐞 Bug Observer (my companion)
body:
This was a very densely packed week, that's for sure. I'm probably gonna be pretty summarize-y with stuff, but I have some insights I'm gonna share to try and make up for it ✌️

<> Poetry Time <> (The longer I'm here in Japan the harder it is for me to write decent English poetry 😵)

Staring at my Phone Writing this email is just… Exhausting, lowkey 😆

今週 頑張って見た 疲れてる

Dragons rise in the east …or so it's been said Is there even such a beast?? Or are they all, well, dead?

I believe in Dragons …is what I'd like to say… To “hop on the wagon” To “join the par-tay”

But, I- You know what, wait a second… I… I believe in Dragons. If Dragons are real they'd probably be like super powerful and magical and would definitely have a way to hide their trace. Maybe the fact that I haven't seen any proof of Dragons is proof enough that they are really there. It's settled.

I believe in Dragons 🐉

Breathe in, breathe out

There's a pit that fills my chest The pit is empty as often as it is full I think maybe that's what life is What about you?

Ok poetry time is OVER! Let's go through my summarized week

Monday Today for family home evening at the church, Nishioka san invited her friend Taguchi san from before, and her husband came too! He's super buff, and plays what's called “bound tennis.” We arm wrestled and he beat me! Maybe I'll be able to pin him down after my every-day push-ups increase! We shared a spiritual message, and Taguchi san and her Husband seemed to like it. I don't think they are super earnestly seeking for the gospel at the moment, but having made the step to come to the church building, meet with us and talk about spiritual things together is an awesome step! Hopefully we can keep working with the two of them and they will have the desire to feel and experience more!

Tuesday Today we did quite a bit of housing, and met a lady named Yamagata san! Like 30 years ago she went to one of our sacrament meetings! She was super nice, and loved talking with us, and even gave us some grapes. We also played with some cats in front of her house together lol. Elder Nakazawa is going to go back to her place and do some surprise weeding with Elder McCune when they go on exchanges here! I'm excited to hear how it goes, and what they will be able to teach her! Besides that our housing was mostly refusals, but we did wave to some foreigners demolishing a building in the distance. I feel like they will hear our message, but I didn't make an attempt to go up to them because they were, well, demolishing a house 😅 Its probably a valid excuse for safety reasons, but they seemed friendly and I wish I could have gotten their info. Tonight was Eikaiwa, and we shared about the plan of salvation at the end. I think that from here on it would be good to go through the plan step by step each time we have Eikawa.

Wednesday For lunch we met up with a guy named Maruyama san who I talked with on Facebook. We went out to eat together at our favorite Karaage (a kind of fried chicken) shop and talked about how he has played ping pong at our church before and knew the past missionaries. He lives kind of far so it's hard for him to come to the church for Sacrament meeting or activities. We did invite him afterwards to church, and he instead told one of his friends about us and invited them to church lol! He also said that sometime he'd be willing to drive us down to Okayama lol. That's pretty dang far but it might be fun someday, we will see what happens. Today we did a lot of housing as well! We met: A guy who was extremely enthusiastic about religion, like super energetic but like super big refused us in a nice way lol 2 nice ladies who said that they might come to sacrament meeting if they have some time Bob san! We randomly saw him coming home from work and talked for a bit A guy playing with 3 cats, who in the end silently refused to talk with us and walked away A woman and her son feeding some fishes, we invited them to our kids English class We have been focusing our invitations on the invitation to Sacrament meeting this week, it's been interesting so far, not a lot of super positive initial responses, but it feels good to be inviting about something so directly correlated to our church and missionary purpose. Sometimes invitations to game night or eikaiwa seem like excuses to stray away from direct invitations, although I do feel that both have value.

Thursday Dreamed about playing Elder sofoul in pokemon, my sister was dating someone, crazy robots, buying pokemon cards, girl with long hair who can pretend to be a bush. Interesting dreams for sure lol. Today in real life we did mostly just weekly planning. We did have a lesson with Mitani兄弟 at the church, as well as call over the phone with Katayama兄弟. It was a solid day for planning! Tonight we also had Japanese class with Lan and Sou, it was fun!

Friday Today we did like 3 hours of housing, got some pizza, and had game night! Tonight for game night Lan and Sou came! We shared a good spiritual message at the end, and it seems like Sou san from Japanese class is gonna be a pretty regular attendee of some of our activities here! He seemed to have a good time, excited to share with him more about the gospel.

Here's a quote I liked from a BYU speech I Read recently.

「My best efforts produced frustration, worry, and illness. In time, I was prompted to divide mentally and physically, where possible, all of the challenges and tasks and assignments given to me into two categories: First, those for which I had some ability to control and to resolve, I put into a mental basket called “concern.” Second, all the rest of the things that were either brought to me or I imagined I had the responsibility to carry out, but over which I had no control, I put in a basket called “worry.” I realized I could not change them to any significant degree, so I studiously strove to completely forget them. The items in the “concern” basket were ordered in priority. I conscientiously tried to resolve them to the best of my ability. I realized that I could not always fulfill all of them on schedule or to the degree of competence I desired, but I did my conscientious best.

Occasionally as I sat in my office, I’d feel my stomach muscles tighten and tension overcome me. I would cease whatever activity I was engaged in and with earnest prayer for support, concentrate on relaxing and overcoming the barrier that worry produced in my life. Over a period of time, those efforts were blessed by the Lord. I again came to understand how the Lord is willing to strengthen, fortify, guide, and direct every phase of life. The symptoms of illness passed, and I learned to face tasks under pressure.」

https://speeches.byu.edu/talks/richard-g-scott/lonely-misunderstood/

I read this talk and got a lot out of it! I hope that you will read This too if you get the chance, it's awesome!

I've got a few days left to talk about but I'm gonna have to save it for next time.

I love you guys. I'm Not perfect, But I’m really trying to be an effective missionary. I feel like a child. But even if I'm a child, I can rest easy knowing I'm a child of a loving Heavenly Father (and incredible Earthly parents too!)

I don't feel like this email maybe had a great rolling theme or anything, but I hope something throughout this was meaningful to you!

I love you, I miss you, and I hope you're well! This goes for all of you :)

Let's have a good next week!

Elder Killian

Cats
summary: Dense week: Poetic insights, exhausted, no Dragons found. Family home evening, new friend, housing.

subject: Anko is one of my favorite things ever
body:
Pre-preface (Long email alert)

Preface Train ride is off to a miserable start 😭 my soap somehow leaked all the way through to the bottom of my backpack and is now kind of a puddle on the train. Why. Why. Why would my soap do this to me. Kind of ironic that soap when unwanted or in the wrong place can make things messy or dirty. I think that I learned something pretty deep just now lol 😲😂

Well, I've got a long train ride to Tottori so I'm gonna try and recall this last week as best as I can ✌️

Ok, in the end I ended up writing almost nothing on the train, so I'm writing this in Tottori ☠️

If you read nothing else from this email, read through the second Sunday with the star emojis 🌟

Saturday Today we ate lunch with Kei san, exchanged a bunch of cute art and stuff with her, and talked about the church. It seems like she's down to read the Book of Mormon but just doesn't really know where to start. The desire is there though! We are gonna try and keep teaching her through LINE too.
