After eating, we passed by a couple dudes with cameras. I felt like I should talk with them, but it seemed a bit inconvenient to like turn around then, so I kind of blew it off. However, Nakazawa長老 had to stop at a combini to go to the bathroom, and when we headed back home we saw them in front of us! I pulled them over, and asked what they were doing (lol, the way I’m describing this makes it sound like im a cop). They said they were just taking photos for fun. I asked if I could join them and walk around together, and they said yes! We ended up exchanging LINE, and they taught me some awesome camera tips. Hopefully we can meet up again. Today we read books at the library to kids, and we also visited one of the art stores to ask if they would take a flyer from us to show people (they did!!). We also studied from the Book of Mormon at the church, all around good Day. ei, cameras, library, art store,

Sunday Today Sister Maggi came, but Jamsy couldn't make it. She apparently had wanted to come but was sleeping when Sister Maggi was going to go. I ended up interpreting in Relief society as well, good times. Today for our Missionary work coordination meeting Nakazawa長老 got pretty scolded by a member for how he handled things lol. He basically Said the way we are doing the meeting is meaningless haha. We learned a few good tips, but Nakazawa長老 definitely took things personally. I got a good chance to talk with him that night though, and I think it was good to discuss things. after church we were able to visit Brother Katayama!! I'd never met him before, it was super exciting. He's really tall! I had expected just a short small Japanese guy, but he's super bulky. He made us some homemade grape juice, and it was about as seedy and pulpy as it gets. Managed to drink it all though. Normally we aren't allowed to visit him (definitely not by ourself) because apparently he has some mental issues, but we were safe to go with Brother Koumoto and President Hattori. We even read all together from the scriptures and prayed. It was a great experience.

Monday Solid Pday! Can't really remember what happened but I'm sure it was great lol.

Tuesday Today we had DCM, taught eikaiwa, and even chatted with some kids in the rain and played a bit of spikeball together. It was a great day, bit unusual lol. Oh yeah, we literally biked for like 2 hours going from park to park trying to find literally anyone to play spikeball with, and finally ended up at that one park. It was a drain.

Wednesday Today we did some housing in the rain, didn't have much immediate success but did have a couple silly encounters. We walked up to a lady who was being visited by her friend in the entryway to her house. The friend looked absolutely flabbergasted when she saw me 😂😂 like the most shocked I've ever seen a person in my life. It was hilarious. We later met a nice old man who super politely scolded us for being out in the rain and kind of shouted at us to go home to safety when he saw we were still going around and housing LOL. By the way, today I saw more lightning and heard more thunder than I ever have in my life. It was awesome. I wish you could have heard it Mom! Afterwards we got to the church and taught kids Eikaiwa. It was pretty fun, some of the kids are getting a bit less shy, it's super cute to see them try and be confident with English. Good day!

Poetry!

The weather is cooler but some still complain; “This temperature's awful! Its melting my brain!” I don't mind the heat I don't mind the cold I don't really mind getting soaked in the rain I'm not one to shake if the thunder roars As long as things stand, and I'm feeling this way Something as insignificant as the weather is no cause for dismay.

Thursday Today we had exchanges with the Tottori Elders in Tsuyama. I was with Elder Schiefer. It was super fun! We did housing, met a 90 year old lady named Kitagawa san who wants to go to Eikaiwa and also might have Interest in the gospel, got licked by a lady’s dog, talked at the church with Matsuoka san in a lesson, ate lunch from a butcher shop (I ate 10 chicken skewers), went to Japanese class, and played soccer with a couple junior high schoolers who now want to come to Eikaiwa! Great day! (Also I found my new favorite ice cream 🔥🔥)

Friday (Shoutout Grandpa Killian’s request!) Today we started off by driving with branch president and Katayama san to go and get curry Udon together, because Katayama san loves it! It was like a super far drive, and looking at the map we were actually outside of the Zone by a teeny bit 😲 oopsies. Anyways Katayama san thought it was average. Lol. I bought some sweets there and shared them with everyone afterwards too. I am starting to realize just how much I love sweets. Like seriously. Today we also visited the card shop as well as Sun Art (with Kimura san!). We ended up talking with Kimura san for like an hour. He shared with us quite a bit from Buddhist teachings, and even how he views things in a bit more of a Christian way than most Buddhists do. It seems like our teachings are roughly the same except for the names of deities lol. I also showed him some art I made, and he told me what he thought it meant. It was really cool, he said it was representative of the eyes of the Savior, and the tears he shed for each of our sakes through the atonement. I had no intention of making that, but it was really eye opening to have him describe it in that way. We invited him to church, but he's busy this week. He also showed us his copy of the Book of Mormon, and we read a couple scriptures from it together. It's tough because he's like a Buddhist leader, but I think that if he seeks the truth he will be led by the spirit. Gonna keep trying to work with him! The card shop guy is also the goat. We showed up to ask if we could hang up a couple flyers for our events, and without hesitation he took both flyers and taped them over a picture hanging up by the register, right in front of our eyes. I also showed him an english game I have, and he lowkey looked it up in like 15 seconds, found translated Japanese instructions, and printed them out for me so I can help other people learn the game. I didn't even ask. Like what this guy is too nice. He showed a little interest in our activities, but next time we meet him I want to be bold and invite him to hang out with us sometime we're free. I can sense his potential.

Saturday Today we met up with Shingo from Ibaraki/Okamachi! He was headed up to Yonago and arranged with us to get some lunch together. We met up, had some fun eating and catching up, and after our delicious Karaage I suggested we get some Gelato together. While Nakazawa長老 and Shingo were ordering, I started a conversation with 4 college students who pulled up to get some gelato. Afterwards I was able to give them each an Eikaiwa flyer, it seemed like a couple of them were seriously interested in showing up! After saying goodbye to Shingo, we headed to the church for weekly planning, Book of Mormon study with members, followed by an hour of listening to the branch president telling us most of what we already knew about one of our friends 😭 I was losing my mind!!! But it's ok. He had good intentions and there was a good piece of advice he gave us, so it worked out.

🌟Sunday🌟 Today we had a great Sacrament meeting, and Nakazawa長老 gave a great talk. Unfortunately, Sister Maggi wasn't able to come, and neither was Jamsy. So I thought I wouldn't be interpreting… but… a member named Sonia (also from the Philippines!) came today! So, I invited her to sit with me when she came in, so I could help her understand the talks. Well, at least that was my intention. After we took the sacrament, I stood up to turn down the temperature for Sister Ushio (she was complaining about it lol) and when I stood up Sister Sonia ran away to the row in front to sit by Sister Morimoto (who can also kind of interpret a little lol) 😂 So I kind of sighed and chuckled to myself, but like a 3rd of the way through the meeting Sister Morimoto gave me a pleading gaze so I moved up to the next row and started helping interpret afterall 😂 this was all pretty silly. It turns out she doesn’t really need that much help, she can actually understand things pretty well haha. After the meeting we had Sunday school, then our Branch Missionary Coordination meeting which went well! After eating Some hard boiled eggs from Sister Ushio, we got to chat for a bit with Sister Sonia. She came to Japan because she married a Japanese guy, and she's been living here for like 20 years. She has two kids, one 21 year old girl and one 19 year old guy (in the army 😲). They are both moved out, and she also said she's separated from her husband. I think she is probably really lonely. She drove by herself 40 minutes to get here, and she apparently hasn't been to church for like 2 years. She said she was scrolling on her phone and that it's a big problem for her, but that she stopped when her finger ran over Sister Morimoto's name. She felt prompted to send her a message, and she says that she felt a light come over her, and she felt like she was having a warm hug. She ended up coming to church, and I think she's planning on coming this next week too! I told her that she did the right thing, and that God appreciates her effort, even if it's difficult for her to come so far for church. What a great example of the spirit and God's love in action. I know that God understands our circumstances, and he will give us inspiration and guidance. In the end, it's up to us whether or not we act on it, but if we do, I can promise it's worth it. After that cool conversation, we packed our bags and headed to the station to travel to Tottori. Right by the station, we were just getting to light and it started flashing, preparing to turn to red. We were very close, and we wanted to make it to the station on time, so I went for it (by the way this is a super short one lane road with no cars, it was safe) but… my companion grinded to a halt. 😅 He still had plenty of time before it turned red but he's a pretty hesitant guy, kinda drives me crazy sometimes lol. I sighed once I looked back to see him stopped, then I pulled over to the side to wait. As I was waiting, I was facing towards a house going down a sort of driveway. I felt something looking at it, and thought in my head maybe it would be significant to try and knock on the door. As I was looking over, I noticed some movement at one of the windows, and there was some figure concealed by the blinds peeking through a curtain at me. I gave a friendly wave, and once Nakazawa長老 was back by me, I said “let's go and talk with this person.” We ended up talking with a nice old lady, who was familiar with Missionaries and our church when there was a building close by her like 30 years ago. The missionaries from that time also used to live in the apartment next door to her house! She told up to keep up the hard work, and we gave her a flyer with the new address to the church. It was a cool interaction. After that, we got a little snack at the station, then headed off to Tottori.

Long email, sorry not sorry.

I love you all so much. Can you tell?

I'm not sure. But anyways, I do, ok!

Love you! 💜

Elder Killian
summary: Train delay due to leak; Sunday activities included teaching, reading books, and visiting art stores.

subject: No brain power
body:
Today (and with this transfer) I've had quite a bit to think about and it's been busy and in the end I didn't really get a chance to write an email. Sorry 😅 I will try and make even this short email meaningful though!

Here's a few fun things you'll get to hear about next week!

I got a bean! Meaning I'm going to be training a new missionary! 😲

I lost a lot of stuff 😅 just not my temper 😎✌️

We have up to 5 new people coming to our Eikaiwa this next week 🎉🎉

We were able to meet up with some people at a community center and play Magic the Gathering 🔥🔥

That's a few of them!

Last message: Don't be upset over things out of your control, or try not to be upset over them. There are a lot of things that we really don't have too much influence over, even if we are trying our hardest to influence them. If you feel like you are giving your 100% and it's not enough please don't worry too much about it! God loves effort. He loves you!

I love you too!

I'll write more next week!
summary: Transfer, busy day, new missionary training, lost stuff, up to 5 new people, Magic the Gathering meetup.

subject: I didn't think it was possible
body:
I don't have time to write much rn, so things are a bit spacey. Hope it's an OK email!

Monday Today we had district Pday, and we all went to the Sand Dunes, as well as a sand museum. it was super cool! I'll attach a photo or two if I can!

Tuesday Today I found out that Elder Nakazawa is leaving… and that I'm training here in Tsuyama! Besides that we had in person DCM, a long train back home, and Eikaiwa, in which I got to talk a bit with Katayama san about the Book of Mormon. It was pretty neat to hear some of his thoughts. I think that he may be much more interested than he is willing to admit, lol. We also played a fun game where Matsuoka san and Katayama san gave me English instructions on how to draw a popular Japanese character, without me seeing the picture of it. They seemed to really enjoy it 😂 maybe I’ll try it again in the future sometime!

Wednesday Today we were able to have a lesson with Nishioka san, and do some housing. While we were housing, we met a guy who told us that one of the apostles’ tombs is in Japan somewhere, or something along those lines. Pretty interesting. Today we were also able to GET IN THE COLLEGE AGAIN. This time it feels like everything worked out perfectly. At first, we tried talking with a few guys out in the parking lot of the church. We asked them if they'd be down to play spikeball with us, or if they could help us find people to play with. They took us inside to talk with the office again. In the office… we met with the same guys who helped us take surveys about our religion with a bunch of people in the study hall. AND we were able to exchange info. AND Elder Nakazawa went crazy demonstrating hIs Diablo (huge yoyo) moves out in the middle of the campus, people were screaming, it was crazy lol. We even played spikeball! The best thing was we were able to exchange info with a bunch of the guys that we had met before. There were also 3 girls who started trying to talk with me, and giggling walked into a nearby building. After a bit a college professor walked up to me, and told me there were some girls who wanted to chat, and that I should go talk with them lol. I went with my companion, we introduced ourselves, and made a group chat lol. They said they'd like to go to Eikaiwa, but we will see lol.

Thursday and Friday I thought it couldn't get any worse. I thought I couldn't have a messier, more difficult or stressful transfer than last time. Spoiler alert, I DID. It starts off with Thursday morning, when choosing which pants I'd like to wear for the day, I select the ones which have a big hole in the left pocket. Usually I leave them until last to wear, but today I felt like wearing them. 🙃 After a great lesson With Matsuoka san (She prayed at the end of the lesson for us!!!!!!), eating lunch together at a noodles shop, calling brother Katayama, having a lesson with Brother Mitani and participating in Japanese class at the community center, I was left with one less important item than I had started with. The key to the apartment. We got there, and no matter how hard I looked I couldn't find it in my bag or anywhere. We walked a bunch of places by foot Retracing our steps, but this is like 10 at night. We called the police to no avail, and in the end, we had to sleep at the Church! 😂 It was the worst night of sleep I've had in a while. I even woke up at 4AM to some horrible Nightmares! Why! Why! We didn't have any bedding so we made due with the church “sofa” (definitely more like a bench) and some folding chairs made into a bed With a small pad on top (Elder Nakazawa used the chairs). In the morning we woke up to retrace our steps further, but alas, no key. That same morning I also lost my wallet lol. Courtesy of the hole in my pants. In the end The office elders drove all the way to Tsuyama to give us an extra key, so we were just barely able to get Elder Nakazawa's stuff in Time to leave. It was intense. In the middle of everything we were able to talk with some old ladies playing ground golf at a park, and another lady up on a hill. I am sure that we needed to meet with them that day. Anyways, it was insane. After leaving for Okayama, we took a bunch of trains As usual and made it there. I had another not great night of sleep, as I this time slept on a bed of relatively hard chairs. Yeah.

Saturday Today I met my SON! My bean! My new missionary companion straight from America! His name is Elder Lear 😎 He's from a place called Grantsville in Utah! I'm super excited to train him! After leaving Kobe (was kind of cool to go back and visit for the first time since I became a missionary btw) we left for Tsuyama, and after making it to Himeji we started off for Tsuyama. It was all one train line, so I figured it would just go all the way back to Tsuyama, right?

👁 👁

You might be able to guess what happened next… a couple hours after riding the train we realized we were pulling back up to the same station we started at, and that because we didn't transfer at one place that we just went in a circle. I learned a good lesson though lol. Now, on the way back to Tsuyama with new companion in tow, we were able to talk with a couple nice ladies who just got back from a day of drinking in the city lol. It took me a while to realize that they were kind of drunk. They were pretty loud lol. We talked on the entire train ride home! We ended up exchanging info, and they said they wanna come to Eikaiwa!

Sunday Today was a great Sunday with Elder Lear. Sister Maggie got to meet Sister Sonia today at church, and they spoke a lot in Tagalog together! So awesome. Sister Sonia needs her support and love as she’s coming back to church. I got a message today or yesterday from the art store where Ogawa san works, and she told me she got called by the community center about my lost wallet! Would you happen to guess what else was happening at the community center… A Magic the Gathering event! 😲 We were able to retrieve my wallet then join a group of like 10 people in a small room and play together!!!!! It was Elder Lear’s first time playing the game, and playing With me and 2 other guys all talking in pretty wild Japanese was definitely a bit overwhelming lol. I let him use the deck that Elder Mackay gave me, and in the end he actually won 😂😂 it was super strong lol. I am not gonna lie I gave myself a pretty terrible start but I think it was good that he won 😂 It was a great chance to meet some new people, and they were kind enough to add me to the group chat for their events, afterwards in which they announced our Eikaiwa and Game Night to everyone! So chill. Pretty wild Sunday, but a great time.

Monday Today we did a bunch of random shopping for new things for Elder Lear, one of which being a bike. We went to a bike shop, but a good option for a bike isn't available until about a month. So we are just going to have to wait until then lol. In the meantime the shop is letting Elder Lear rent a bike for free, which is pretty awesome. It's not an amazing bike but it'll do the job. I think that having a trusty solid bike for his next 2 years is gonna be worth the wait. Today looking for sheets we came across a furniture Shop, which referred us to a cheaper place to shop lol. Shopping, furniture Shop, bike, fhe

Tuesday and Wednesday Eikaiwa, a lot of housing, and visiting Brother Mitani. At and house we were talking to a lady and her son, and they thought that we were so cool and nice. However, a lady across the canal was shouting to them about us, saying that afterwards she'd let them know about us lol. She kept holding up her arms in a big X. I felt like I was in a sitcom lol.

Thursday Today my companion told me he heard me Sleeptalking a bit in Japanese 😂😂 Today we had weekly planning, another lesson with Matsuoka san (she prayed for us again!) And Japanese class. It was a good day, but the lesson was like 2 and a half hours long! We made it clear we are gonna try and be more timely from now on. Don't get me wrong, it was a great lesson though!

Friday Today we met a cool artist guy while Housing! We are gonna come back to his place soon, and hopefully introduce the Book of Mormon!

Saturday Today we went to a concert in Okayama with Nishioka san, Sister Maggie and one of her coworkers we've never met before, Reggie. (A girl) it was pretty fun, and on the way back we got Ramen!

Sunday Today we had a great fast Sunday, and I got to help interpret for sister Maggie again. We also got to call and talk a bit with Brother Katayama today. He seems to feel discouraged about being unable to come to church, and he feels that the branch leaders are just telling him the same thing over and over again. We were able to provide hopefully a bit of reassurance though. Pretty quiet day 😌

Pday Today was a pretty fun Pday. We went to this huge Warehouse place and I found a cool CD and my companion bought a coin purse. We also got some cards, a pretty pricey donut, karaage (friend chicken), Cereal and milk. We also had family home evening at the church with Lan san and Nishioka san.

I learned a lot from this last week. I am gonna try to improve and keep being a good trainer! I love each of you so much! God loves you.

Elder Killian

By the way, Sister Ushio gave us stuff like so much this last week or two, she's so nice. Give unto those around you!
summary: Monday: Sand Dunes trip, photo attached. Tuesday: Nakazawa leaving, Eikaiwa with Katayama, drawing game. Wednesday: College visit, spikeball, info exchange

subject: Elder Killian's Special Audio Experience
body:
It's been a bit of a long week in a good way, there's a lot of stuff that happened that I wanna explain and I don't have writing power right now, so please enjoy this description of my week and spiritual message! 😎

The audio file should be attached to this email!

Love you!

キリアン長老
summary: Kaden had a blast with his new companion, Elder Lear, and they had a good time at family home evening with some investigators, despite the investigators' doubts and excuses.

subject: Audio Message + Poetry
body:
Lit photo right?

Today is another awesome audio message! And a little poetry.

I was told to write a poem about Febreeze I got laundry to do, but my knees... Are weak from the biking I've done all day... Guess I've got nothing left to say...

I love you guys, each and every one. Living in Japan is a lot of fun. Not much longer and I'll be done.

I don't know how I should feel about that...

😘😘😘😘😘 LOVE YOU GUYS!

(Imagine a photograph of some tasty food (was too big of a file 😅))
summary: Kaden had a busy week with Zone Conference and State Conference, but enjoyed hearing from Elder Esplin of the 70, who spoke in Japanese, and learned a valuable analogy about doing the dishes.

subject: Eucalyptus to Yak
body:
For this email, the first little bit is in writing, and after that it's all Audio message. Hopefully it's a fun mix! We're starting off with some poetry ✌️

Rungs on a ladder support me Every step of the way Remove one, And the next step is harder.

Remove them all. Watch me. If you thought I'd stay At the bottom, think again, think harder.

I'll shimmy my way up Stand at the top Laugh And enjoy a snack from a combini.

If you don't watch out I'll take one of those beams, And with a shout, I'll dash your dreams.

選択の 自由。なんか、さ、 すごくない？

Tuesday Today we had Eikaiwa, and we were even able to meet with Kitagawa san again!!! We went to her place and we're gonna turn around but decided to knock on the place even though we weren't sure what the entrance to the house was. Her Son answered the door, told us to wait a little bit, and Kitagawa san came out of the house with the Church Pamphlet we gave her in Hand!! She had read through the whole thing! She said she loved it. We were able to give her a Book of Mormon, although she says she doesn't get much free time. Even though she's 90 she's working hard every day to maintain the family shrine. We are coming back with branch president next week to talk with her again! After that we went and did some housing, which also went well. We were able to meet a lady that used to go to our Eikaiwa 👀 but stopped because it's too late. Her name is Mochihara san. We got her phone number and we're gonna try and figure something out. Solid Tuesday!

Wednesday Today we had a great lesson With Kei san and Sister Morimoto! We met and talked a bit about baptism and about the Holy Ghost. We didn't feel like it was the right time to invite Her to Baptism, but we did invite her to pray to know if our church is true. I feel like with hindsight she might not be taking it quite as seriously as we had hoped. I don't think it was bad and she's saying yes to our invitations but I want her to have a spiritual experience through prayer, according to her own desire, not just from what we say. Kei san also brought a whole bag of stones for carving! It was so lit. I am now carving stones for a bunch of people 😂

Continued in the Audio Message!

I love you!

Picture from last week. I look like orange Mario
summary: Kaden tried to do some finding using science reading and caricature drawing, but it didn't go well, although he did meet some school students and drew some funny art for them.

subject: Fruit juice 😢
body:
Welcome to my email! Tuesday-Saturday is on the audio portion! Love you!! Enjoy!

Sunday Today we were able to attend fast and testimony sacrament meeting, and I interpreted for Sister Maggi as usual. It was great! After church we got some studies done then got picked up by Brother Koumoto to visit Brother Katayama! Last week he had the branch presidency send us some persimmons, and I used them to make some jam to take to him! He gave us some banana juice and we were able to have a good time administering the sacrament to him as well as sharing some spiritual insights through his trials. Good time!

Monday Fruit juice Fruit juice Sure, another glass of fruit juice! … We had too much Fruit juice. 5-6 cups of Fruit juice did us wrong today. I don't think I've ever heard sounds like that in my life 😱

Today we went to a concert that we got free tickets for from a super cool lady we met last week. We were able to say hi to her, she was super happy we came! On the way up to the community center, we chatted with a lady, her son, and her mom. We ended up sitting by them in the concert hall, getting their number, and they are super interested in English and our Game night!! The mom wants to take her son to the game night! So awesome!!! On the way out we also met a past Eikaiwa student who also stopped coming because Eikaiwa is too late. In this next week or two when we make an early Eikaiwa, there will be like 5-6 people who will show up right away! It's amazing! I also bought a deck of new Magic cards and some card sleeves with some birthday cash from the fam ❤️ Today we also had a crazy lesson today with the Okadas! We tried to invite them to baptism, but didn't get a very solid answer from them, and invited them to pray about it. I feel that they are both seriously thinking about our invitation. I am excited to see where things go with them from here 😁 Today we biked about 10 miles which was fun! To end things off we had an insane amount of food at Family Home Evening, and I shared my testimony with everyone. It was very spiritual and uplifting! Good times.

I love you guys 💜 cheers to a new week!
summary: Kaden had a great week, meeting with some new people, including a 13-year-old boy and his grandma, and sharing a spiritual message with them, and also visited a lady named Kitagawa-san with President Hattori.

subject: Zombie Head
body:
Today is just an audio message and a couple pictures of art I made. Enjoy!

I love you!!!

Elder Killian
summary: Kaden had a close call while biking to visit a member, but felt protected by the Lord, and also had a great experience visiting the Yamauchi family and seeing the Lord's hand in his mission.

subject: Yeah baby
body:
Monday Today we ate lunch with Katayama san! Afterwards we went to a park and took some pictures with him too. He actually requested some artwork, so I gave it to him in person today, but a couple days later he sent me an email asking me to change it 😂 I guess that's what I get making an art for someone with such particular standards 🤷‍♂️😁 After that we biked pretty far to check out a secondhand store and the mall. At the mall there were some people at a table and they were taking a survey of customers at the mall the reward for taking the survey was a bag of potato chips so we decided we'd stop by and take the survey because I love surveys LOL so we sat down to take the survey they told us it would take just a few minutes and we began and we noticed that it would take more than just a few minutes LOL we ended up taking this survey for probably 10 minutes not understanding most of it and we left with, as promised, a bag of chips. 😂 The same night we also met at the church with Nishioka san and had a family home evening activity as usual. We made some letters for friends and asked Nishioka san to give one to one of her friends 😁

Tuesday Today we started off with a district meeting over Zoom, and had some great housing too! We met a lady who was super sweet, and a guy who invited us into his house to show us his ART AND ELECTRIC GUITAR????? You kidding Me? We are absolutely Going back to jam out with bro. Today was also Eikaiwa, and Matsuoka san brought me a late birthday present 👀 It's a Sukimaswitch CD 20th anniversary album!!!!!!!!!!! Literally the Album I listened to in America that made me fall in love with Sukimaswitch. Thats an “up there” birthday present 😆

Wednesday Played soccer with some kids at a park! One of them knows a kid who's going to our activities, and he took some of our flyers to pass out to his friends, and he's asking his mom to let him come to game night! We will see how it goes!

Thursday Today we met a few cool people housing, ome of which thought we were their mother impersonating some young men 😂 (through the doorbell voicebox) as well as had a fantastic Daytime Eikaiwa for the first time! 5 new people showed up, and we already have an appointment set up with one of them! 😁😁✨️ A highlight for sure!

Friday We met with Kimura san in his art store, and had a 2 way lesson about our religions lol. Buddhism is really just all the beliefs of Christianity with a different name attached to the deity which is pretty interesting and I wouldn’t have expected. We also had Game night and attempted to visit a bunch of inactive members by bike (we weren't able to meet any of them 😢). Solid day!

No more time to write so I'll talk about the rest next week!! I love you guys!!!!!!! ❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️💜

Saturday Today we had

Sunday
summary: Monday: Met Katayama san, biked to mall. Tuesday: Zoom meeting, Eikaiwa, received Sukimaswitch birthday gift. Wednesday: Played soccer with kids. Thursday:

subject: Hard week... but....
body:
....not hard enough to stop me from feeling the spirit when I testify of the Savior 😎

Just a short testimony audio message today. I wish I had more time! Maybe I'll use it wisely this next week 😆

I love you!!
summary: Despite having a challenging week, Kaden saw many blessings and miracles as he focused on testifying about Jesus Christ and serving others, and learned the importance of forgetting oneself and serving others.

subject: Stressful but plentiful
body:
Monday Today we cleaned our apartment, and had some awful stressful drama with a member, but its over so no worries 😁✨️

Tuesday-Thursday Outside of the usual, we had an apartment inspection (A++++), met to draw with Kanda san, went to the dentist, and had Kids Eikaiwa! For some reason one of the kids parents gave us 5 American dollars she found in her house, and we split them up between the kids lol

Friday Today we cooked up some fancy steak that Kanda san gave us, and also met and invited Kitagawa san to Baptism! She was shocked lol, she's the owner of a shrine, but she's seriously considering our invitation. She was asking a lot of good questions, and she even called us on this next Monday to ask when we can meet to talk more about it. She's so cool. Tonight was Game night, and Hinata kun brought one of his friends!! Having young people at the church is a blessing, even if they are like in their early teens lol.

Saturday Today we had a lot of time for housing, and met a girl in her 20s who wants to meet up with us again! We prayed about where we should go finding, and she was at the first house we Knocked on. She loves art, and is like a Professional at manga! so Cool, her name is Akari san. Today we also had a good lesson with Matsuoka san. Good day.

Sunday Today we visited so many people! We caroled and blessed the Sacrament for Sister Junko, Brother Katayama, and Sang for Sister Maggi and her Filipino Roomates! We felt the spirit so strongly 😊 We were also able to visit the Yamauchi Family and the guy named Shinichi san (who likes radio) with Brother Hisamune. It was so fun, and a huge Blessing.

Jesus Christ loves you. So do I! Pray in your afflictions. It will all be OK. Catch you next week 😊✨️
summary: Monday: Stressful drama, Tuesday-Thursday: Appliance inspection, Kids Eikaiwa, Friday: Steak dinner, meeting Kitagawa san, Saturday: Housing, Sunday: Visited

subject: Peak Missionary Life in Tsuyama
body:
This week was peak stress

Also, peak progression

This week's been a lot (I make a confession)

From drama with members and much mental tax

To 3 friends to Church!! A Tsuyama Max!

The second to last week I will spend in this place

Has felt quite intensely as sort of a race.

Rush to the Church, rush to the store

Rush to get Curry...

...

...then rush some more.

This sharp back and forth has my mind filled with froth

(Unrelated, but this week I vacuumed a moth 🦋)

The burdens this week were heavy to bear

But

Throughout all my trials, my Savior was there.

He loves me and you, and he loves mine and yours

And I'm sure if He could, He'd take us on tours;

Of Paris, of Italy, Rome and Hawaii

He'd help us relax- I'd let out a sigh-ee 😤

Though I made it all sound like a dream,

His comfort and love ain't as far as it seem(s)

To access his Mercy we need only pray

To His Godly Father, by night and by day.

To some kinds of people it might be a chore,

But with all of our efforts, we're rewarded by 4

Thousand

Million

Trillion

Times what we think possible.

It's true.

I testify now, that Jesus loves you!

And me, like I said, and your family dog

Lizards and geckos and even a...

...

Toad 🐸

No burden or weight is to heavy a load

For our Redeemer and Brother and Friend

Who sees to it that we will meet up in the end.

So don't let yourself be bogged down or be gloomy

For in Christ's pure heart, there is plenty of room-ee

For you and for me and for all of the world

His banner of freedom and goodness unfurled

The moment he came from the heavens above

To love, live, teach, heal, bring in and to...

...

Love.

Even if I said love twice, it's nice cause its true.

I hope that my poetry (maybe) touched you 🔥

I love you!
summary: Peak stress and progression this week; heavy but Savior present.

subject: Guess what...
body:
Miracles do still happen.

This week was my last one in Tsuyama. A lot of stuff happened...

Monday-Tuesday We had district Pday and In-Person District Council Meeting! For Pday we went to the Sand Museum (second time for me and one other district member) as well as get some sushi for lunch. In person DCM was great too! On the way back to Tsuyama we talked to a couple High Schoolers at the station, and made them some art for them. It was a fun interaction for sure!

Wednesday-Thursday Getting to do one last Kids Eikaiwa class was a blast! For some reason everyone was actually kind of into it this time, and 2 new kids were there too! Sister Morimoto had us do a cute activity where we cut paper to make a star, with only one straight cut! (She prepared the paper beforehand and folded it so that happened)

Friday-Sunday! Listen to the recording!!

I got transferred to a place called Gobo, it's mad Country! My new companion is Elder Warnick, looking forward to getting to know him 🔥

Have a great Christmas everyone ❤️❤️

I love you!
summary: Kaden had an amazing Sunday, with 10 friends attending church, and he gave his last talk in Tsuyama, sharing what he had learned about humility and love, and is now transferring to a new area called Gobo.

subject: 🚨Exiled🚨 (but...)
body:
The title has a couple of meanings behind it;

1

I was transferred from Tsuyama, to Gobo. The most infamously Inaka (country) part of the mission. Don't get me wrong, there could be a couple places in our mission that have slightly less, but Gobo is the first place people say when they think of small and far away areas. It's pretty dang barren 😂 Ever since I've heard about Gobo (and since I visited a few times for exchanges) I've always wanted to go 😂😂

2

This entire past week we were sick and had to stay in our apartment 💀

Here's a few highlights since coming to Gobo 🔥

On Christmas, we randomly greeted a young guy and ended up spending the next like 6-8 hours with him, shared about the gospel and even ate out with the Missionary Work Coordination leader and his wife with this guy 😂 His name is Jian! He came to church with us too, and it was awesome. He's a really cool guy, 22 years old, likes to hang out, talk, and smoke. He smokes 1 pack a month which isn't like crazy for a smoker by any means, but he doesn't wanna stop to join our church. We haven't been able to communicate wuth him for these past two weeks cause we don't have his contact info (his phone is in need of repairs 😂) but he knows how to reach us. Hopefully we can just bump into him again soon, because it stinks that it's hard to meet up consistently.

Elder Warnick (my new companion) is a fun guy 😂 He is a bit of a clean freak and he hates his hair like 90 percent of the day, but he's really focused in on missionary work. Seems like he hasn't had a chance to do much of the approaching or speaking in missionary work yet, so I'm trying to provide ample opportunity for him!

The members in Gobo are awesome!!! On new years we watched the sunrise of the new year with the Kinoshita family, and then got to spend the whole day eating a bunch of traditional food and playing games with them and their family at their house all day! It was so fun, and made me feel pretty at home for the first time in a while.

We have tried a few fun ways to meet with people! Spikeball missionary work, drawing art for people, housing, bike streeting, a lot of good stuff! This week even though we've been sick, a girl messaged us on a social media app and wants to meet with us, so hopefully she can respond and we can meet up!

Poetry Time

Wings are stick-on for some; Fly for as long as you want, and then it's a Peel-off the bandage type thing when you're done.

I wonder why wings bring shame to people. Are the colors too bright? Do they not fold up just right?

Flight isn't a thing to be embarrassed about! Its graceful and freeing, is it not? Why can't you be happy with the great good that you've got??

Take pride in your wings and fly far far away To a land filled with wings and where all of us say "Our wings stick around."

I'm writing some of this on a train to Wakayama, and it's worth noting that travel in Gobo is much easier and faster than Tsuyama 😂 Tsuyama was like 4 hours to the nearest area and Gobo is like one. So it's definitely an upgrade.

It's been a wild past few weeks, and honestly it doesn't feel like it's been half of the transfer already 😅 I'm sincerely hoping that we will be able to make a lot of solid time for finding and teaching in Gobo this next few weeks. I'm really excited to share about it!

I know that God loves us and is desperate for us to turn to him. Even as a missionary I feel like I need to make more room for Him in my day to day life. I'd encourage you to as much as you can, seek to use your time in a way that's spiritually enlightening. On Sunday we had a Zone devotional and I was able to reflect on the experiences I've had dispelling darkness with positive spiritual things. Darkness doesn't have to look dark, or even be an inherently bad thing. But if it's not working towards bettering you as a person, and it doesn't follow God's standards, it will work as a darkness in your life if you are unconscious of it.

Be careful and evaluate what you're doing with your time, and pray about it too. God will consecrate your efforts to be positive works of light.

I love you guys! I'm sorry I haven't written in a while. There's a lot of fun stuff happening in Gobo so look forward to my next email!

I love you!!!!!
summary: Transferred to rural Gobo, met interesting missionary, spent time with new friend Jian.

subject: Re: Cover
body:
This week has been off to a much better start 😁 I'm pretty much feeling back to normal, and Elder Warnick is feeling better too.

Monday

Tuesday Today we had in person DCM, which was pretty fun. Afterwards we all got Indo Curry together, and one of the senior missionaries, Elder McArthur paid for everyone! 👀 After Elder Warnick and I got back to Gobo we tried a bit of finding and managed to make friends with a high schooler who is insane at one arcade game. We couldn't exchange info (besides giving him our number and telling him where the church is) but when we meet again hopefully we will have our game night Flyer ready to pass to him! His name was Yuuto. He also went to a Christian day care as a kid, and he has a good view of the church I think. I'm glad we met him! We also went to visit a store that a past friend owns to introduce ourselves. The owner is really chill, and we were able to talk with him and his wife. I'm hoping we can go back soon for a little tour of their workshop!

Wednesday Today we got up early to head to Zone conference, and had a great time in Abeno!! It was a pretty cool Zone conference compared to normal I think; I feel like I learned a lot about the spirit, and how important it plays a role in teaching and finding our friends. After getting home from Zone conference we had Eikaiwa! A new person came too! Her name is Sachiko, and she was invited by another Eikaiwa member 😄 good day ✨️

Thursday Today we actually taught Eikaiwa at a high school in Gobo! It was pretty fun, but stressful. One of the teachers wants us to make sure more missionaries can join us next time so we can participate more evenly with the students. After that, we visited a guy named Mori, and our friend Hatsue san. We talked with her for a while, and talked a lot about the resurrection and afterlife. We were just talking on the doorstep, but her husband pulled up, so we went inside and talked over some yuzu-tea for a little while. We sent her a chapter from the Book of Mormon to read, and she read it twice like immediately after the message, and shared her thoughts with us. She doesn't necessarily believe in everything the way it's written in the Book of Mormon right now, but I feel like she's really progressing!

Friday Today we talked with a lady at a stationery store and I made her some quick art. Afterwards we went to a bunch of community centers trying to get information about events we can join to get to know the people in Gobo and hopefully make new friends! Good day for gathering info 😎

Saturday Today we traveled to Tanabe, which was my first time! It's a bigger city in our area that's a 40 minute train ride away from Gobo. We met up with Brother and Sister Enomoto, and we got to have food at one of his friends' restaurants. I made some art for his friend and signed it "from the missionaries" haha. Afterwards we got dropped off at a nearby shopping mall and talked with a group of like 4 teenagers and got a couple pictures with them! I challenged them to talk with the missionaries next time they see some! I also made some art for them, and I was requested to draw the logo of a casino 😂 I told them to avoid going there if they can lol. Later when we got back to Gobo, we had Game night at the church. Sachiko came, and even brought her 18 year old niece! We played ping pong all together and it was hilarious 😂 Sachiko faced off against Elder Warnick and beat him 2-1 with plenty of slams 😂 She's so funny. It's really cool how far one connection with a person can go!

Sunday Today was an awesome Sunday! I got to meet our newly baptized member Harold for the first time. (He's been in the Philippines until now) He's very chill and laid back. His Japanese still needs some work so I'm interpreting again 💫💫😵😵 It's worth the blessings but it's exhausting 😅 I need to up my Japanese study. Today, a random Filipino lady named Sister Nikki also showed up! She works on a cruise ship, and was stopped nearby so she came to church. (I interpreted for her too) And to top things off, Sachiko san came to church! She said she had work so she couldn't make it, but she came during her break to Sacrament meeting! It was such a cool surprise 😮 She had to leave early too, but she was really enjoying the talks. It was so cooool! Looking forward to seeing her again on Wednesday!

Monday Today for Pday Elder Warnick and I traveled to Tanabe again and traveled to a bunch of fun stores. My favorite was this thrift/antique shop; I got cool CDs and a Japanese card game! Before all that though, we met wuth brother Enomoto and helped trim some trees in front of the Tanabe church building. Afterwards we went and got Sushi together! At one of the stores Elder Warnick and I spent like 15 bucks trying to get some Magic cards from a "stop the light" game and it was rigged and we didn't get anything 😅😅 Elder Warnick felt sick and says he'll never waste money like that again but he's glad that he learned his lesson lol. Me too! 😂 Another cool thing from today! We met Jian on the train, and got a time set up with him to meet!! Lit!

All in all, it's been a pretty solid week, but we were only in Gobo for half of it! This next week I'm excited to see what miracles we can experience. It'll be Elder Warnicks last full week in Gobo (we both talked with President Sano during our interviews so we know Elder Warnick is leaving), so we gotta make sure it's a good one.

I love you guys so much, seriously. I have so many cool friends and loved ones on my email list, every one of you are special to me. Please trust that your trials are temporary, and place your trust in God! He will help you to find comfort!

Love you!
summary: Started well, DCM fun, made friends, Zone conference learning, Eikaiwa teaching.

subject: Elder Killian Time
body:
It's been how long since I've sent an email?? Like a month? 😅

I've been a mixture of lazy and unmotivated in regards to emails lately, not entirely sure why.

I might wrote more later, but I wanna get out a least a message right now.

Or course, I love you guys, you hopefully can tell!

But today I wanna share a little about my more recent experiences with the Spirit.

I don't think I really knew how to follow through with promptings from the spirit until more recently as a missionary. All my life I've done things that probably have been inspired by the spirit, but actively seeking to follow it day to day is different; making a consistent conscious effort has helped lead me to experience so many wonderful things.

For example, me and Elder Childs (my new comp!!) went to try and visit someone living in an apartment complex, but they weren't there. On the way back to our bikes, I noticed a person walking towards the apartment from far away. We were in the middle of crossing the road, and I felt like we should talk with her, so I curved my walking back around 😂 and we kind of jogged to go and talk with her. She ended up being a Catholic Filipino, and she exchanged contact information with us! It was pretty simple, but going with the feeling I felt led us to have a meaningful interaction with someone we normally wouldn't have met.

I try every day to follow the spirit. I pray to Heavenly Father to help me understand what he would desire I do.

You can do this!! We need His spirit in our lives. Pray humbly, and ask God to guide you and bless you and comfort you. Show him gratitude for all that you have, and you will feel that! I promise!!

I love you! I promise that God loves you too. His plan and your plan may feel like two completely different things but as you prioritize his will, your day to day will be so much more meaningful!

I'll write next week!!

Elder Killian
summary: Recovered from sprain, shared missionary experiences, emphasized following spirit.

subject: 2 AM Nightmare from Above (and a cockroach)
body:
Ignore the title of the email for now, and let's go over some cool things from this last week!

We had Eikaiwa at a small high school, Elder Childs and I were joined by Elder McCord and Elder Stedler! It was pretty fun, and one of the kids is here on a scholarship for basketball and wants to come to church. Pretty lit 🔥🔥

We also visited Jian's house for the first time and got to meet his dad, who is super chill and used to be a Gymnast. We visited them twice in these past two weeks, and the second time I got to give Jian's dad a back massage. It was pretty lit.

We have done a lot of cool art finding these past couple weeks, making watercolors and doodles and all kinds of things for people. I've had a great time spreading around the Gospel message with art!

Lessons these past two weeks have been great; we invited our friend Yamamoto san to Baptism and got an "I'll think about it" response, but we're seeing growth in all of our friends! We have made plans to invite each of them to Baptism as soon as we can, and as the spirit guides.

As for the title of my email, let me set the stage. It's 3:00 AM, and I awake to sone scary sounds from the top bunk. Sounds like some weird sputtering awfulness and then from the top bunk comes the rain of vomit. Elder Childs probably kept hurling over the edge of the bunk bed for like 3 minutes straight. I was just kind of sitting there, stuck in the bottom bunk for fear of the napalm. I avoided getting covered in throw up, but I got some decent splash-age, but did throw away a blanket. I then got a clothespin on my nose and got up and swept all the vomit into a dustpan then a bag. Pretty Nasty lol. In the middle of everything a cockroach came from nowhere and I obliterated it. My comp was ok, the night before he had some raw squid thing and it did not agree with him. 😂

I've been learning about Humility and boldness recently.

I've found it hard to be bold while feeling like I'm staying humble. How do I balance the two?

If you have any ideas for me I'd love to hear from you!

I love everyone of you so much. You are in my prayers. Look for God's hand in your life and you'll be surprised 🔥🔥

Love you!!

Elder Killian
summary: Visited Japan, Eikaiwa at high school, met Jian's dad, taught art lessons, planned baptisms, woke up to vomit. Learned humility and boldness.

subject: Poetry's great escape
body:
Stanzas trickle from ma-mouth you hear it? Stance: Wide- flickin in n out you fear it? Low bars doesn't mean my bar is set low- Hurdle high dare to fly don't try to run bro

Yes those were some epic rap lyrics I wrote 🔥🔥 Lowkey harder to write rap than traditional poetry

Anyways it's been an awesome week. We were able to have lessons with our friends and 2 of them came to church! Huge steps for Gobo!

Sunday was a highlight not just because of two friends coming to church, but 4 dudes from America also pulled up on a trip! Even though they understood little, they testified about the spirit they felt.

A fun thing from this week was going to Tanabe and meeting a bunch of kids by the beach! We somehow got put into their friend group chat and we even made a tik tok with them. So lit

This Pday was cool too! The Senior couple the McArthurs took us with their grandkids up to a place called Koyasan, and it had a bunch of sweet shrines and stuff. While there, I was able to talk with two Japanese guys (one of which loves Welcome to Demon School!!) And get their contact info, and I randomly met a non-member couple from Provo Utah!! The guy even used to live in Lindon for a few months! I didn't get to share all that much besides how awesome a mission is, but I believe it left an impression.

I'm probably leaving Gobo this next transfer, heading into my last two in a new area... or old one...

We will see! I love you! I promise that God loves us and blesses us as we trust Him and put effort into doing His will.

Toodleoo!
summary: Wrote rap, met friends at church, visited kids by beach, toured shrine.

subject: Nishiwaki
body:
This week was my last week in Gobo 👀 Now I'm in Nishiwaki! It's double the population, and we even have a friend on Baptismal Date 😮 SPEAKING OF WHICH!

In my last lesson with Elder Childs and Jian, we invited him to baptism, and he said yes 🎊 Jian will be baptized on the 4th of April. Super exciting!

Another cool thing from this last week in Gobo was getting to give a priesthood blessing to Sister Kuwata in Japanese. She was feeling poorly and called us up for a blessing. We both had to learn unfamiliar Japanese vocabulary, but it was really spiritual.

Now that I'm in Nishiwaki, I'm companions with Elder Talbot. He's really chill and a calm dude so it's gonna be fun to work with him!

One thing I focused on this past week was looking at those around me as the Savior would. It's really hard to try and treat everyone the way that He would, but we can learn so much from it.

There's nobody you hate, nobody that you view as less talented, there isn't ant kind of preconception based on appearance or situation.

It's only reaching out in love, and with Christlike confidence. We can be confident if we know our actions are backed by Charity, Christlike love.

I love you guys! Perhaps not like the Savior, but I'm trying my best. I wish you all the best!!

Here's a few pictures from this past week too!
summary: Last Gobo week, baptized friend, gave blessing in Japanese, new companion, practiced loving everyone.

subject: Spiritual Growth
body:
Hello readers! 🤠👢👢

Welcome to my brief email about these past 2 weeks!

A few things

Elder Talbot and I get along really easily

Following the spirit as a missionary involves speaking your mind to your companion, especially if what came to mind wasn't in your daily plan

Japanese women keep saying I have a tiny face (not my companion, just me)

Our baptismal date friend Shin has ghosted us for a week, still no contact from him

Pasta with broccoli

Played memory (for an hour and a half), but the cards are all Japanese fish names (extremely hard)

Zone conference! We got some cool flipbooks about the Savior we can use to teach people

Met someone who played Magic the Gathering like 10 years ago and his eyes grew wide as I showed him my cards

We're desperately holding on until one of us gets paid or a reimbursement lol

I made a bunch of art for people

We played Pokémon to do missionary work

Anyways it's been a fun past couple of weeks.

I've been reading from the Book of Mormon and I'm reading about the events leading up to the Savior's birth. Having faith in Him before he was even born must have been a big spiritual challenge for the people at the time. But many people softened their hearts, followed the spirit, and received a testimony of Christ. And guess what! He came around. He's still here and He's here for each of us. I promise!

I love you guys! I'll write again soon
summary: Arrived Japan, met trainer, baptized friend ghosted, played games, read Book of Mormon.

subject: Anxiously Engaged (Anxiously)
body:
This week has been great! We got soaked in the rain, played card games at a book store to make friends, joined some old folks for some play-gambling, took some pictures of the cherry blossoms, used some cool flipbooks to teach about Jesus, and even played skip-bo with 3 12 year old girls at the mall.

It's been a pretty fun week, but getting to the end of it has been stressful and anxious. I think that a lack of focused studies and preparing for my Talk on Sunday took a toll on me. I'm invigorated going into this next week though! Elder Talbot and I had a fun day running around the town exploring. We got some candy from a candy shop, visited a shrine, and got some old stuff from an old guy and his scrap yard.

I spoke in Church about the Resurrection and Salvation. Basically how Resurrection is Unconditional, but the manner in which we spend eternity/where we go in Heaven is based upon our actions on this Earth.

It went pretty well, and it was fun to speak! I already know I'm gonna have some fun talks when I get back and can ramble on in English too lol.

I'm so grateful for Jesus Christ. The amount of times I stop to view my actions and compare them with how The Savior would act/think and revise what I would do is crazy. I'm so grateful for his example. It's impossible to be perfect like him right now, but striving our best to follow his example is what counts.

I love you guys! I know that Jesus and our Heavenly Father love you too. 😁
summary: Rain-soaked adventures, friend-making games, cherry blossom photos, teaching talks, Resurrection talk success. Grateful for Christ's example. Love you.

subject: Welcome to the Jungle
body:
Note about the title, it's completely irrelevant to anything I did this week

This week was my last week with Elder Talbot 😢 a few things were meeting a cool kid named Hikaru, having a lady geek out over my art, and playing Magic with Zawa san.

It was pouring rain, and we were walking to the card shop to try and talk with people playing card games, and we saw a kid Japanese squatting under a bike parking place. He was on his phone, but we walked up and started a conversation with him! He was super Chill, said his name was Hikaru. Hes 17, and that he doesnt know what religion he is. We told him our church was nearby, so we walked him over and gave him a tour! The whole way he was like "you guys aren't gonna hurt me, right?" And that kind of a thing, all while he was smiling lol. He took a BeReal with us, but he doesn't have LINE!! 😢 We told him we'd definitely meet again. Looking forward to the day!!

BTW my new comp is Elder Gifford! He's from near Las Vegas and he likes video games and playing magic (thanks to me 😏)

We were doing some rare housing on Sunday, and we met a lady who didn't seem very interested at her house. I saw she had some art hanging up inside, so I showed her some of mine, and she was so obsessed lol. She said she'd buy it but I obviously couldn't do that lol. She said to come back and show her more. So... maybe I'll go back and make her some lit Gospel related art!

The first day Elder Gifford got here we played Magic together with Zawa san! In the last week he put together 2 full decks to play with lol. It went well, and we were able to set up a schedule of meeting once a week to play and share a spiritual message at the church. Zawa san is like super straightforward and if he opens his heart to the spirit, I know he will see blessings and miracles.

Well, that's a little something something from this last week. Last transfer boutta go off. Love you guys!!!!!

BTW I painted this card 🔥
summary: Special devotional with Elder Kearon, high expectations discussion, no sleep, chess losses, gym time, go-and-do-time, goodbye emails, flight departure tonight.

subject: Mission Airy
body:
Wassup fam friends and book-ends. Hope you are all having a lovely time! It's been unfortunately a week of being sick locked away in the apartment 🤧

We used our time in pretty productive ways, contacting friends and making art for people, but it was still rough.

"Survived" a small earthquake

Visited a member's son

Here's a cool experience we had with the Spirit!

We were heading back home from trying to find at a super busy park (very uncharacteristic of the rest of our very countryside area) and I stopped us on a bridge overlooking a valley. I said "where does God need us to go down there?" And my companion turned to me and said he remembered a member lived down there who we could visit. On the way, we saw an old lady working in a field. I felt like I should give some art to her, so we stepped over some frogs and handed her a drawing I'd made.

She was really glad, and although she wasn't immediately asking to go to church, she probably normally wouldn't get to meet the missionaries, and is busy taking care of her incapable husband. (Sounds kind of harsh but he's like really old)

I'm grateful that we could show her some love. I want to try and do more things like that.

On Pday we drove all the way to Kobe to visit some card shops with our friend Zawa san. We talked about plenty of unhinged stuff in the car ride there and back, but I was able to tell him a lot about Church Organization and missionary work. He described the prophet as having lightning powers like Darth Sidious which was interesting.

I love you guys. I'm trying to gain a stronger desire to follow the Savior and learn more about him. I'm gonna do my best to focus on that this week. Love you guys!!!!!

Here's some art I've made this week:
summary: Fell ill, visited friend's son, helped old lady, drove to Kobe for card shopping. Art included. Learned about Church organization. Love family.
